**(B) Additional Insured.** The City of Evanston, its officers, employees, agents, and elected officials shall be named as additional insureds on all commercial general liability and product liability insurance policies maintained by the permit holder. The additional insured endorsement shall be in a form acceptable to the City Attorney.

**(C) Insurer Qualifications.** All insurance policies required by this Section shall be issued by insurance companies authorized to do business in the State of Illinois and rated no lower than \"A-\" (Excellent) by AM Best Company, or an equivalent rating by another nationally recognized insurance rating organization.

**(D) Certificates of Insurance.** The permit holder shall provide certificates of insurance evidencing all required coverages to the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator prior to permit issuance and upon each policy renewal. The permit holder shall provide the City not less than thirty (30) days\' advance written notice of any cancellation, non-renewal, or material change in coverage affecting any required insurance policy.

**(E) Lapse in Coverage.** Failure to maintain any required insurance coverage at any time during the term of the permit shall constitute an immediate violation of this Article. Upon any lapse in required insurance coverage, the permit holder shall immediately notify the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator and shall suspend all cannabis operations within forty-eight (48) hours of the lapse. The permit holder shall not resume operations until the required insurance coverage has been restored and proof of insurance has been provided to and accepted by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator. A lapse in insurance coverage exceeding fourteen (14) consecutive calendar days shall constitute grounds for immediate permit suspension under Section 9-5-25.

**[Section 9-5-17. Performance Bond or Letter of Credit]{.underline}**

**(A)** Prior to the issuance of a cannabis dispensary permit under this Article, the applicant shall provide a performance bond or irrevocable standby letter of credit in the amount of fifty thousand dollars (\$50,000.00), in a form and substance acceptable to the City Attorney.

**(B)** The performance bond shall be issued by a surety company authorized to do business in the State of Illinois and rated no lower than \"A-\" by AM Best Company. If the applicant elects to provide an irrevocable standby letter of credit in lieu of a performance bond, the letter of credit shall be issued by a federally insured financial institution authorized to do business in the State of Illinois, and shall be in a form that permits the City to draw upon the letter of credit upon written demand by the City Attorney.

**(C)** The performance bond or letter of credit shall guarantee the permit holder\'s faithful compliance with all provisions of this Article and all conditions of the permit. The City may draw upon the bond or letter of credit to cover costs of remediation, code enforcement, emergency abatement, or other expenses arising from the permit holder\'s violation of this Article or failure to comply with permit conditions. Any draw upon the bond or letter of credit shall be preceded by written notice to the permit holder, except in cases of emergency where immediate action is necessary to protect public health or safety.

**(D)** The bond or letter of credit shall remain in full force and effect for the entire duration of the permit and for one (1) year following the expiration, revocation, or voluntary surrender of the permit, whichever occurs last.

**(E)** The bond or letter of credit shall be renewed or replaced at least thirty (30) days prior to its expiration. Failure to maintain a valid bond or letter of credit shall constitute a violation of this Article and grounds for permit suspension.

**[Section 9-5-18. Records and Reporting]{.underline}**

**(A) Record-Keeping.** Every permit holder operating under a permit issued pursuant to this Article shall maintain complete, accurate, and contemporaneous records of all cannabis transactions (including sales, returns, and disposals), inventory movements, employee records (including agent identification card numbers and employment dates), security incidents, customer complaints, odor complaints and remediation actions, waste disposals, and all financial records relating to the operation of the dispensary.

**(B) Retention Period.** All records required under this Section shall be retained by the permit holder for a minimum of five (5) years from the date of the record\'s creation or receipt. Records shall be maintained in a secure location, either on the premises or at the permit holder\'s principal office within the State of Illinois, and shall be organized and indexed in a manner that permits efficient retrieval and review.

**(C) Access and Inspection.** All records shall be made available for inspection and copying by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator, the Evanston Police Department, the Evanston Health Department, or any other authorized City official upon forty-eight (48) hours\' written notice. In the event of an active investigation into a security breach, theft, or diversion, records shall be made available within twenty-four (24) hours of the request.

**(D) Required Reports.** The permit holder shall submit the following reports to the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator:

> \(1\) **Quarterly Compliance Self-Audit Reports** as required by Section 9-5-13(K) of this Article, due within fifteen (15) calendar days following the end of each calendar quarter;
>
> \(2\) **Annual Audited Financial Statements**, prepared by a certified public accountant licensed in the State of Illinois, submitted within ninety (90) calendar days of the end of each permit year;
>
> \(3\) **Annual Workforce Demographics Report**, detailing the number of employees, their residency by census tract, their demographic composition, and the permit holder\'s progress toward meeting the diversity and inclusion goals set forth in its approved plan, submitted within ninety (90) calendar days of the end of each permit year; and
>
> \(4\) **Immediate Incident Reports** for any security breach, theft, attempted theft, diversion, robbery, burglary, significant inventory discrepancy, or other adverse event affecting the security or integrity of the dispensary, submitted within twenty-four (24) hours of the occurrence of the incident. The incident report shall include a detailed description of the event, the time and date of occurrence, the identities of all persons involved (if known), the actions taken in response, and the status of any law enforcement investigation.

**(E)** Failure to maintain records as required by this Section, or failure to submit required reports in a complete and timely manner, shall constitute a violation of this Article and may result in penalties under Section 9-5-25.

**[Section 9-5-19. Social Equity Program]{.underline}**

**(A) Purpose.** The City of Evanston recognizes that certain communities and individuals have been disproportionately impacted by the enforcement of cannabis prohibition laws. The Social Equity Program established by this Section is intended to promote inclusion and meaningful participation by individuals from impacted communities in the legal cannabis industry, to reduce barriers to entry, and to ensure that the economic benefits of cannabis legalization are shared equitably among all residents of the City of Evanston.

**(B) Eligibility.** An applicant shall qualify as a Social Equity Applicant if the applicant demonstrates that one or more of the following criteria are met:

> \(1\) The applicant, or a principal officer holding a majority of the ownership interest in the applicant, is a current resident of the City of Evanston and resides in a census tract designated as an R3 Area by the City Council pursuant to subsection (G) of this Section. For purposes of this subsection, \"current resident\" means a person who has maintained a primary residence in the applicable census tract for a continuous period of not less than twenty-four (24) months immediately preceding the date of application;
>
> \(2\) The applicant, or a principal officer holding a majority of the ownership interest in the applicant, has a prior cannabis-related arrest, conviction, or other record that has been subsequently expunged, sealed, or pardoned by the Governor of Illinois. For purposes of this subsection, a \"cannabis-related\" arrest, conviction, or record means one arising under the Cannabis Control Act (720 ILCS 550/), the Illinois Controlled Substances Act (720 ILCS 570/), or any comparable federal or state statute, where the underlying conduct involved the possession, use, sale, manufacture, or distribution of cannabis; or
>
> \(3\) The applicant, or a principal officer holding a majority of the ownership interest in the applicant, is a member of a family in which a parent, sibling, or child of the applicant or principal officer was arrested for or convicted of a cannabis offense, as described in subsection (B)(2), prior to June 25, 2019 (the effective date of the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act).

**(C) Social Equity Scoring Bonus.** Applicants who qualify as Social Equity Applicants under subsection (B) of this Section shall receive a bonus of twenty (20) points added to their application score calculated under Section 9-5-10, resulting in a maximum possible application score of two hundred seventy (270) points. The social equity scoring bonus shall be awarded upon verification of the applicant\'s eligibility by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator.

**(D) Workforce Hiring Requirement.** Social Equity Applicants who receive the social equity scoring bonus under subsection (C) shall commit to employing a workforce in which at least fifty percent (50%) of all employees, including full-time and part-time employees, are residents of R3-designated census tracts within the City of Evanston. This workforce hiring requirement shall be a binding and continuing condition of the permit for any permit holder that received the social equity scoring bonus. Compliance with this requirement shall be monitored and verified at each annual renewal through the workforce demographics report required by Section 9-5-18(C)(3). The permit holder shall maintain contemporaneous records of employee residency, including proof of address for each employee claimed as an R3 resident, and shall make such records available for inspection upon request.

**(E) Cannabis Business Mentorship Program.** Social Equity Applicants who receive the social equity scoring bonus under subsection (C) shall participate in the City of Evanston Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum period of two (2) years from the date of initial permit issuance. The Mentorship Program shall be administered by the Department of Community Development, in coordination with local business development organizations and cannabis industry professionals, and shall provide business development resources, regulatory compliance support, financial management training, and networking opportunities. Participation in the Mentorship Program shall include the following components:

> \(1\) Attendance at quarterly mentorship meetings organized by the Department of Community Development, at which participants shall receive guidance on business operations, regulatory compliance, and industry best practices;
>
> \(2\) Completion of an annual business development workshop covering topics such as financial planning, marketing, human resources, and community relations; and
>
> \(3\) Submission of semi-annual progress reports to the Mentorship Program Coordinator within the Department of Community Development, detailing the participant\'s business development activities, challenges encountered, and progress toward stated business goals.

The Mentorship Program is a mandatory condition of the social equity scoring bonus, and participation shall continue for the full two-year minimum period regardless of any changes in the permit holder\'s business circumstances.

**(F) Revocation of Social Equity Status.** If, at any annual renewal, the permit holder is found to be non-compliant with the workforce hiring requirement of subsection (D) or the mentorship program participation requirement of subsection (E), the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator shall issue a written notice of non-compliance. The permit holder shall have thirty (30) calendar days to cure the non-compliance. If the non-compliance is not cured within the thirty-day period, the social equity scoring bonus shall be revoked for the current renewal period and all future renewal periods. Revocation of social equity status shall also be considered as an adverse factor in the evaluation of the renewal application and may constitute grounds for permit revocation under Section 9-5-25 of this Article.

**(G) Designated R3 Areas.** The following U.S. Census Bureau census tracts within the City of Evanston are hereby designated as R3 Areas for purposes of the City\'s Social Equity Program: 8092.01, 8092.02, 8093.00, 8094.01, 8094.02, 8095.00, and 8096.00. The City Council may amend the list of designated R3 Areas at any time by resolution, based on updated data regarding the disproportionate impact of cannabis prohibition enforcement, socioeconomic indicators, and community need.

**[Section 9-5-20. Diversity and Inclusion Plan]{.underline}**

**(A) Requirement.** Every applicant for a cannabis business establishment permit under this Article shall submit a diversity and inclusion plan as part of its application. The diversity and inclusion plan shall describe the applicant\'s commitment to promoting diversity in ownership, management, employment, and contracting, and shall set forth specific, measurable, and achievable goals for advancing diversity and inclusion in all aspects of the dispensary\'s operations.

**(B) Plan Contents.** The diversity and inclusion plan shall include, at a minimum, the following elements:

> \(1\) A description of the applicant\'s hiring practices, including outreach methods, recruitment channels, and any partnerships with local workforce development organizations, community colleges, vocational training programs, or other entities that serve diverse populations;
>
> \(2\) Specific and measurable goals for recruiting and retaining employees from diverse backgrounds, including residents of R3-designated census tracts within the City of Evanston, individuals with prior cannabis-related convictions that have been expunged or sealed, and members of other underrepresented communities;
>
> \(3\) A plan for vendor and contractor diversity, including strategies for identifying and engaging minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, and locally owned businesses as vendors and contractors;
>
> \(4\) A description of any training programs related to diversity, equity, and inclusion that will be provided to employees and management, including unconscious bias training, cultural competency programs, and anti-discrimination policies; and
>
> \(5\) A description of how the applicant will measure and report on progress toward its diversity and inclusion goals, including the metrics and benchmarks that will be used.

**(C) Additional Requirements for Social Equity Applicants.** Applicants that qualify as Social Equity Applicants under Section 9-5-19 shall include, as an additional component of the diversity and inclusion plan, a detailed hiring pipeline for meeting the fifty percent (50%) R3 workforce requirement set forth in Section 9-5-19(D). The hiring pipeline shall identify the specific workforce development partners with which the applicant has established or intends to establish relationships, the recruitment methods to be employed, the timeline for achieving full compliance with the workforce hiring requirement, and any contingency plans in the event that initial recruitment efforts are insufficient to meet the required threshold.

**(D)** The diversity and inclusion plan shall be evaluated and scored as part of the application scoring process under Section 9-5-10(C)(5) of this Article.

**[Section 9-5-21. Prohibited Acts]{.underline}**

No cannabis dispensary permit holder, and no agent, employee, or representative of a cannabis dispensary, shall engage in any of the following prohibited acts:

**(A)** Sell, dispense, distribute, or otherwise provide cannabis or cannabis-infused products to any person under twenty-one (21) years of age for adult-use sales, or to any person who does not hold a valid medical cannabis registration card for medical sales, as applicable;

**(B)** Operate the dispensary or conduct sales or dispensing activities outside of the hours of operation specified in Section 9-5-13(F);

**(C)** Exceed the maximum customer occupancy of the retail floor area as established under Section 9-5-13(J);

**(D)** Allow or permit the consumption of cannabis or cannabis-infused products on the premises of the dispensary, including in any indoor or outdoor area controlled by the permit holder;

**(E)** Sell, dispense, or distribute cannabis or cannabis-infused products that have not been tracked through the State-approved seed-to-sale tracking system as required by Section 9-5-13(D);

**(F)** Allow any person who does not hold a valid Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Card to handle, sell, dispense, or otherwise manage cannabis products;

**(G)** Fail to install, maintain, or operate any of the security measures required by Section 9-5-13 of this Article;

**(H)** Engage in advertising that is designed to appeal to or target persons under twenty-one (21) years of age, or that makes false, misleading, or unsubstantiated health or therapeutic claims regarding cannabis or cannabis-infused products;

**(I)** Transfer, assign, convey, or encumber the permit, or transfer any ownership interest in the permit holder, without the prior written approval of the City Council as required by Section 9-5-24 of this Article; or

**(J)** Violate any provision of the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/), the rules and regulations adopted thereunder, or any other applicable State or federal law.

**[Section 9-5-22. Background Checks]{.underline}**

**(A) Background Check Requirement.** All principal officers of the applicant, including any person who holds a financial or voting interest of five percent (5%) or greater in the applying entity, shall submit to a comprehensive fingerprint-based background check conducted through the Illinois State Police and the Federal Bureau of Investigation. Each principal officer shall complete and submit a background check authorization form as prescribed by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator and shall submit to fingerprinting at a location designated by the City or the Illinois State Police.

**(B) Supplemental Investigation.** In addition to the fingerprint-based criminal background check, the City may conduct a supplemental background investigation of the applicant and all principal officers, which may include a review of local law enforcement records within the City of Evanston and Cook County, civil litigation history, bankruptcy history, regulatory compliance history (including any prior cannabis license applications, approvals, denials, or disciplinary actions in any jurisdiction), and any other information relevant to the applicant\'s fitness to hold a cannabis dispensary permit.

**(C) Disqualifying Offenses.** The following shall constitute grounds for denial of a cannabis business establishment permit application:

> \(1\) Any felony conviction within ten (10) years of the date of application, regardless of the nature of the felony, unless otherwise excepted under subsection (D) of this Section;
>
> \(2\) Any conviction for a controlled substance felony under federal or state law, regardless of the date of conviction, including but not limited to offenses involving the manufacture, distribution, or trafficking of controlled substances (other than cannabis offenses that have been pardoned, expunged, or sealed as provided in subsection (D));
>
> \(3\) Any conviction for a sex offense as defined in the Sex Offender Registration Act (730 ILCS 150/), regardless of the date of conviction; and
>
> \(4\) Any conviction for a violent felony, including but not limited to murder, attempted murder, kidnapping, armed robbery, aggravated battery, arson, and home invasion, regardless of the date of conviction.

**(D) Exceptions and Mitigating Factors.** The following exceptions and mitigating factors shall apply to the evaluation of background check results:

> \(1\) The Cannabis Licensing Coordinator may, in the Coordinator\'s sole discretion, consider mitigating factors when evaluating a principal officer\'s criminal history, including but not limited to the nature and severity of the offense, the length of time elapsed since the commission of the offense or the completion of any sentence or period of supervision, evidence of rehabilitation (including completion of educational programs, community service, stable employment history, and letters of recommendation), and the relevance of the offense to the operation of a cannabis business establishment;
>
> \(2\) A prior cannabis-related conviction that has been subsequently pardoned by the Governor of Illinois shall not be considered a disqualifying offense under subsection (C) of this Section and shall not be weighed adversely in the evaluation of the application; and
>
> \(3\) Expunged or sealed convictions shall not be considered disqualifying for purposes of this Section. No applicant or principal officer shall be denied a cannabis business establishment permit solely on the basis of a conviction that has been expunged or sealed pursuant to applicable State or federal law.

**(E) Timing.** Background checks shall be initiated promptly upon receipt of the background check authorization form and fingerprint submission. The results of all background checks must be received by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator before the application may be deemed complete under Section 9-5-7(C). Any delays in the receipt of background check results shall not extend the application review timeline, and the applicant bears the risk of delay.

**(F) Cost.** The cost of all background checks, including fingerprinting fees, Illinois State Police processing fees, and FBI processing fees, shall be borne by the applicant and shall be paid at the time of fingerprint submission. Background check costs are non-refundable.

**[Section 9-5-23. Inspections]{.underline}**

**(A) Right of Inspection.** The Cannabis Licensing Coordinator, the Evanston Police Department, the Evanston Health Department, the Evanston Fire Department, and any other City official authorized by the City Manager shall have the right to inspect any cannabis business establishment operating under a permit issued pursuant to this Article at any time during hours of operation without prior notice, and at any other time upon twenty-four (24) hours\' written notice to the permit holder or the Agent-in-Charge.

**(B) Pre-Opening Inspection.** A comprehensive pre-opening inspection shall be conducted before the initial permit is physically issued. The pre-opening inspection shall verify compliance with all security system requirements, building code requirements, fire code requirements, ADA accessibility requirements, operational requirements, and all other applicable standards set forth in this Article and in any conditions imposed by the City Council. No cannabis dispensary shall commence operations until the pre-opening inspection has been completed and all identified deficiencies have been corrected to the satisfaction of the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator.

**(C) Routine Compliance Inspections.** The City shall conduct no fewer than two (2) unannounced compliance inspections per year during the term of each active permit. Compliance inspections may address any or all provisions of this Article, including but not limited to security systems, surveillance system functionality and recording retention, employee identification compliance, inventory tracking accuracy, odor mitigation effectiveness, record-keeping, and operational hours compliance.

**(D) Refusal to Permit Inspection.** Refusal by the permit holder, the Agent-in-Charge, or any employee of the dispensary to permit an authorized inspection shall constitute a violation of this Article and grounds for immediate permit suspension under Section 9-5-25(C).

**[Section 9-5-24. Transfer of Ownership or Permit]{.underline}**

**(A)** No cannabis dispensary permit issued under this Article shall be transferred, assigned, conveyed, pledged, or otherwise encumbered or disposed of to any other person, entity, or successor in interest without the prior written approval of the City Council. Any attempted transfer without such approval shall be void and of no legal effect.

**(B)** Any transfer, assignment, sale, or other disposition of an ownership interest of ten percent (10%) or greater in the permit holder, whether in a single transaction or a series of related transactions, shall require prior written approval by the City Council. For purposes of this subsection, \"ownership interest\" includes equity interests, membership interests, partnership interests, stock, and any other form of financial or voting interest in the permit holder.

**(C)** An applicant seeking approval of a transfer under this Section shall submit a transfer application to the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator, which shall include all information, documentation, and materials required for an initial application under Section 9-5-5 as they pertain to the proposed new owner(s) or transferee(s), including but not limited to background check authorizations, financial disclosures, and proof of insurance and bonding capacity.

**(D)** A non-refundable transfer application fee of two thousand five hundred dollars (\$2,500.00) shall accompany each transfer application. Payment shall be by certified check or electronic funds transfer payable to the City of Evanston.

**(E)** The City Council shall evaluate the transfer application and may approve, approve with conditions, or deny the transfer. The City Council\'s decision shall be final.

**(F)** Failure to obtain prior City Council approval for any transfer or disposition of ownership subject to this Section shall constitute a violation of this Article and grounds for immediate permit revocation under Section 9-5-25.

**[Section 9-5-25. Violations; Penalties; Suspension and Revocation]{.underline}**

**(A) Penalties.** Any violation of this Article, any condition of a permit issued hereunder, or any order, directive, or notice issued by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator pursuant to this Article, shall subject the permit holder to one or more of the following penalties, as determined by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator or the City Council:

> \(1\) A written warning for a first minor violation that does not pose an immediate threat to public health or safety;
>
> \(2\) A fine of not less than five hundred dollars (\$500.00) and not more than five thousand dollars (\$5,000.00) per violation per day that the violation continues. Each day that a violation continues shall constitute a separate and distinct offense;
>
> \(3\) Suspension of the permit for a period not to exceed ninety (90) days, during which time the permit holder shall cease all cannabis operations at the permitted location; or
>
> \(4\) Revocation of the permit, which shall permanently terminate the permit holder\'s authorization to operate a cannabis dispensary at the permitted location.

**(B) Notice and Hearing.** The Cannabis Licensing Coordinator shall provide written notice of any alleged violation to the permit holder, which notice shall specify the nature of the alleged violation, the provision(s) of this Article or permit condition(s) alleged to have been violated, and the proposed penalty. The permit holder shall have fifteen (15) calendar days from the date of the notice to submit a written request for a hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board. If no hearing is requested within the fifteen-day period, the proposed penalty shall become final.

**(C) Grounds for Immediate Suspension.** The Cannabis Licensing Coordinator may order the immediate suspension of a permit, pending a hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board, upon a finding that one or more of the following conditions exist:

> \(1\) Any act or omission by the permit holder that poses an immediate and substantial threat to public health or safety;
>
> \(2\) Refusal by the permit holder or any agent to permit an authorized inspection under Section 9-5-23;
>
> \(3\) A lapse in required insurance coverage exceeding forty-eight (48) hours; or
>
> \(4\) Operation of the dispensary outside of the hours permitted under Section 9-5-13(F).

In the event of an immediate suspension, a hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board shall be scheduled within fifteen (15) calendar days of the suspension order.

**(D) Grounds for Revocation.** The following shall constitute grounds for revocation of a cannabis dispensary permit:

> \(1\) The accumulation of three (3) or more violations of this Article within any twelve (12) month period;
>
> \(2\) The conviction of any principal officer of a disqualifying offense described in Section 9-5-22(C) during the term of the permit;
>
> \(3\) Failure to pay the annual permit fee or the annual community benefit contribution when due, after written notice and a reasonable opportunity to cure;
>
> \(4\) Failure to comply with the social equity conditions set forth in Section 9-5-19, including the workforce hiring requirement of subsection (D) and the mentorship program requirement of subsection (E), following the notice and cure process set forth in Section 9-5-19(F);
>
> \(5\) Failure to complete the Cannabis Business Mentorship Program within the required two-year period under Section 9-5-19(E);
>
> \(6\) A material misrepresentation of fact in the application or in any report, document, or filing submitted to the City under this Article;
>
> \(7\) Operation of the dispensary without a valid State of Illinois cannabis dispensing organization license; or
>
> \(8\) Any other act or omission that, in the judgment of the City Council, demonstrates that the permit holder is unfit to hold a cannabis dispensary permit or that continued operation of the dispensary would pose a threat to public health, safety, or welfare.

**(E) Disqualification Following Revocation.** Revocation of a cannabis dispensary permit shall disqualify the permit holder, and all principal officers of the permit holder at the time of revocation, from applying for a new cannabis business establishment permit within the City of Evanston for a period of five (5) years from the date of revocation.

**[Section 9-5-26. Waivers]{.underline}**

**(A) Authority.** The Cannabis Licensing Coordinator may, upon written petition by an applicant or permit holder, waive or modify any non-statutory requirement of this Article upon a finding that all of the following criteria are satisfied:

> \(1\) Strict compliance with the specific provision for which a waiver is sought would impose an undue hardship on the applicant or permit holder due to circumstances beyond the applicant\'s or permit holder\'s reasonable control;
>
> \(2\) The waiver or modification, if granted, would not compromise public health, safety, or general welfare, and would not adversely affect the interests of neighboring property owners or the community at large; and
>
> \(3\) The applicant or permit holder has demonstrated substantial compliance with the spirit and intent of the requirement for which the waiver is sought, or has proposed alternative measures that would achieve the same or substantially equivalent protective purpose.

**(B) Waiver Request.** A request for a waiver shall be submitted in writing to the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator and shall include the following:

> \(1\) The specific Section and subsection of this Article for which the waiver is requested;
>
> \(2\) A detailed description of the basis for the request, including the nature of the hardship and the circumstances giving rise to the request;
>
> \(3\) A description of any alternative measures the applicant or permit holder proposes to undertake in lieu of strict compliance with the subject provision; and
>
> \(4\) Any supporting documentation that the applicant or permit holder believes to be relevant to the Coordinator\'s evaluation of the request.

**(C) Appeal.** The Cannabis Licensing Coordinator shall issue a written decision on each waiver request within thirty (30) calendar days of receipt of the request. If the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator denies the waiver request or grants the waiver with conditions that the applicant or permit holder finds unacceptable, the applicant or permit holder may appeal the decision to the Liquor & Cannabis Board within fifteen (15) calendar days of the date of the written decision.

**(D) Non-Waivable Provisions.** The waiver authority granted under this Section shall not apply to the following provisions, which may not be waived or modified under any circumstances:

> \(1\) The buffer distance requirements set forth in Section 9-5-9(B);
>
> \(2\) The background check requirements and disqualifying offenses set forth in Section 9-5-22(C);
>
> \(3\) The minimum insurance requirements set forth in Section 9-5-16; and
>
> \(4\) Any requirement imposed by State law, including the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/) and the rules and regulations adopted thereunder.

**[Section 9-5-27. Relationship to State Law]{.underline}**

**(A)** This Article is adopted pursuant to the home rule authority of the City of Evanston under Article VII, Section 6 of the Illinois Constitution of 1970, and in furtherance of the provisions of the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/), which expressly authorizes units of local government to enact reasonable regulations governing cannabis business establishments within their jurisdictions.

**(B)** In the event of any conflict between a provision of this Article and a provision of State law, the more restrictive provision shall control, except where State law expressly preempts local regulation on the specific subject matter in question. The City reserves all rights under its home rule authority to adopt regulations that are more restrictive than those imposed by State law, to the extent permitted by the Illinois Constitution and applicable case law.

**(C)** Nothing in this Article shall be construed to authorize, permit, or condone any activity that is prohibited by federal law, including but not limited to the federal Controlled Substances Act (21 U.S.C. § 801 et seq.). The City of Evanston makes no representation, warranty, or assurance regarding the federal legality of cannabis cultivation, processing, distribution, sale, or use, and assumes no liability whatsoever for any federal enforcement action taken against a permit holder, applicant, or any other person in connection with activities conducted under this Article.

**(D)** Holders of a local cannabis dispensary permit issued under this Article must at all times maintain a valid State of Illinois cannabis dispensing organization license authorizing operations at the permitted location. The revocation, suspension, expiration, or non-renewal of the State license shall automatically and immediately suspend the local permit until such time as the State license is reinstated, renewed, or reissued. The permit holder shall notify the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator within twenty-four (24) hours of any action by the State affecting the status of the State license.

**[Section 9-5-28. Severability]{.underline}**

If any Section, subsection, clause, phrase, or provision of this Article, or the application thereof to any person, entity, or circumstance, is held invalid, unconstitutional, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, unconstitutionality, or unenforceability shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining Sections, subsections, clauses, phrases, or provisions of this Article, or the application thereof to other persons, entities, or circumstances. To this end, the provisions of this Article are hereby declared to be severable. The City Council hereby declares that it would have adopted this Article and each Section, subsection, clause, phrase, and provision thereof, irrespective of the fact that any one or more Sections, subsections, clauses, phrases, or provisions may be held invalid, unconstitutional, or unenforceable.

**[Section 9-5-29. Effective Date]{.underline}**

This Article shall take effect on January 1, 2024, following its adoption by the City Council and publication as provided by law.

**ADOPTED** by the City Council of the City of Evanston, Cook County, Illinois, this 11th day of December, 2023.

CITY OF EVANSTON

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Title: Mayor

result:
**CITY OF EVANSTON** *Office of the City Manager* *Department of Community Development*

**Cannabis Dispensary Permit** **Application Checklist and Submission Guide**

Issued by the Department of Community Development Cannabis Licensing Division

Pursuant to Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5

Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, Illinois 60201

**Cannabis Licensing Coordinator:** Renata Vasquez-Morris **Phone:** (847) 448-4311 \| **Email:** rvasquezmorris@cityofevanston.org

**Publication Date:** January 15, 2024 **Effective for Application Window:** February 1, 2024 through May 31, 2024

**[Section 1: General Instructions and Application Overview]{.underline}**

This guide accompanies the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form and provides detailed instructions for completing and submitting a complete application package. All prospective applicants for a local cannabis dispensary permit are advised to review this guide in its entirety, along with Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5, before beginning the application process.

Applications for a Cannabis Dispensary Permit will be accepted during the open application window from **February 1, 2024 through May 31, 2024**. No applications will be accepted before or after this window. Applications will be processed in the order received.

The City targets a 90-day review period from the date a complete application is accepted. Following completeness determination, a public hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board will be scheduled within 45 days. After the public hearing, the Zoning Board of Appeals will issue its recommendation, and final action by the City Council is required. Applicants should plan for a total processing timeline of approximately 120 to 150 days from submission to final permit issuance, depending on scheduling and any requests for additional information.

**Incomplete applications** will receive a written deficiency notice. Applicants will have **fourteen (14) calendar days** from the date of the deficiency notice to cure all identified deficiencies. If deficiencies are not cured within this period, the application will be deemed withdrawn and the application fee will not be refunded.

**Submission Requirements:** All application materials must be submitted in hard copy (**three collated sets**) and one electronic copy (**USB drive, PDF format**) to:

> Department of Community Development Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, IL 60201
>
> Attention: Renata Vasquez-Morris, Cannabis Licensing Coordinator

A **non-refundable application fee of \$5,000.00** must accompany the application. Payment must be made by cashier\'s check or money order payable to \"City of Evanston.\" Personal checks and credit cards are not accepted.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5 in its entirety before beginning the application. The City is available for pre-application conferences by appointment.

**[Section 2: Applicant Eligibility Requirements]{.underline}**

The following eligibility requirements must be met by all applicants at the time of application submission:

**Entity Status.** The applicant must be a licensed business entity in good standing in the State of Illinois. Sole proprietorships are not eligible to hold a cannabis dispensary permit.

**State License.** The applicant must hold a valid State of Illinois conditional or permanent adult-use dispensing organization license issued pursuant to the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/). The State license number and expiration date must be provided with the application. Applicants holding conditional licenses should refer to Checklist Item 3 for additional documentation requirements.

**Principal Officer and Owner Disclosure.** All principal officers, board members, and persons holding five percent (5%) or more ownership interest in the applicant entity must be identified in the application. Each such individual must authorize and undergo a fingerprint-based background check through the Illinois State Police and the Federal Bureau of Investigation. The following constitute disqualifying offenses: (a) any felony conviction within the preceding ten (10) years; (b) any felony conviction for a controlled substance offense, regardless of when the conviction occurred; (c) any conviction for a sex offense, regardless of when the conviction occurred; and (d) any conviction for a violent felony, regardless of when the conviction occurred. Expunged or sealed convictions are addressed in Evanston Municipal Code Section 9-5-22(D)(3); applicants should consult that provision carefully.

**Social Equity Eligibility.** Applicants may qualify for social equity designation and a 20-point scoring bonus if at least fifty-one percent (51%) of the qualifying ownership interest is held by one or more individuals who: (a) are residents of R3 (Restorative, Reinvest, Renew) designated census tracts within the City of Evanston; or (b) have a prior cannabis-related arrest, conviction, or adjudication of delinquency. Social equity applicants must complete the Social Equity Documentation requirements described in Checklist Item 18 and the detailed disclosure sections of the Application Form.

**[Section 3: Required Submission Components --- Master Checklist]{.underline}**

The following checklist identifies every document and item that must be included in the application package. Items marked with an asterisk (**\\***) must be submitted at the time of application. Items marked with a dagger (†*\*) must be submitted prior to permit issuance but are not required at the time of application. Applicants should include a cover sheet indexing all submitted materials by checklist item number.

**Item 1 --- Completed Application Form (\\\*)** Completed City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form (all sections, signed and notarized by an authorized representative of the applicant entity). Reference: Application Form Sections 1--10.

**Item 2 --- Application Fee (\\\*)** Non-refundable application fee of \$5,000.00, payable by cashier\'s check or money order to \"City of Evanston.\" Personal checks and credit cards are not accepted.

**Item 3 --- State Cannabis License (\\\*)** Certified copy of the applicant\'s State of Illinois adult-use dispensing organization license (conditional or permanent), including the license number, date of issuance, and expiration date. If the license is conditional, the applicant must also provide documentation of the timeline and conditions for conversion to a permanent license. Applicants holding conditional licenses should be aware that the City\'s permit review and approval process may take 120 to 150 days. It is the applicant\'s sole responsibility to ensure that its State license remains valid throughout the local permitting process and that all State deadlines for license conversion are met.

**Item 4 --- Entity Formation and Organizational Documents (\\\*)** Articles of Organization or Articles of Incorporation; Operating Agreement or Bylaws; Certificate of Good Standing from the Illinois Secretary of State (dated within 60 days of application submission); EIN verification letter from the IRS; and an ownership chart identifying all members, managers, officers, and directors with their respective ownership percentages.

**Item 5 --- Principal Officer and Owner Disclosures (\\\*)** For each principal officer, board member, and person holding 5% or more ownership interest: (a) completed Individual Disclosure Form (Appendix A to the Application Form); (b) government-issued photo identification; (c) proof of residency (utility bill, bank statement, or lease agreement dated within 90 days); (d) authorization for fingerprint-based background check (Illinois State Police and FBI); and (e) completed criminal history disclosure as required by Application Form Question 14B. All individuals meeting the ownership threshold must complete this disclosure regardless of whether they hold an active management role.

**Item 6 --- Proof of Right to Occupy Proposed Location (\\\*)** Executed commercial lease agreement or deed showing ownership of the proposed dispensary location. The lease must have a remaining term of at least three (3) years from the date of application submission (inclusive of renewal options) and must contain an express provision permitting the operation of a cannabis dispensary on the premises. If leased, include a signed Landlord Acknowledgment and Consent Form (Appendix B to the Application Form).

**Item 7 --- Site Plan and Floor Plan (\\\*)** Professionally prepared site plan and floor plan drawn to scale, showing: (a) full property boundaries and proposed dispensary footprint; (b) interior layout identifying the retail sales floor area, secure storage area, office/employee area, vestibule/customer waiting area, and any other distinct spaces with square footage for each; (c) all entry and exit points; (d) location of all security cameras; (e) location of the point-of-sale area; and (f) location of the secure storage vault or room. Plans must be prepared or certified by a licensed architect or engineer.

**Item 8 --- Buffer Distance Certification (\\\*)** A certified survey or professional measurement report demonstrating compliance with all required buffer distances from sensitive uses, including: (a) minimum 1,000 feet from any K-12 school; (b) minimum 500 feet from any licensed daycare center; and (c) minimum 1,500 feet from any other licensed cannabis dispensary. Distances must be measured as set forth in the Application Form, Section 3, Question 7. The certification must identify each sensitive use by name and address and state the measured distance to each.

**Item 9 --- Security Plan (\\\*)** Comprehensive Security Plan addressing: (a) 24/7 video surveillance system with minimum 1080p resolution and minimum 30-day recording retention; (b) camera placement map; (c) panic alarm system with direct connection to the Evanston Police Department; (d) secure vestibule entry procedures with customer ID verification protocol; (e) cash handling and transportation procedures; (f) after-hours security measures; and (g) employee security training program. The Security Plan must be prepared or reviewed by a qualified security consultant.

**Item 10 --- Odor Mitigation Plan (\\\*)** Odor Mitigation Plan including: (a) detailed HVAC system specifications for the proposed dispensary; (b) carbon filtration system details, including manufacturer, model, capacity, and maintenance schedule; (c) description of any additional odor control measures (e.g., negative air pressure, sealed storage); and (d) a professional engineer\'s certification that the proposed odor mitigation system is adequate to prevent detectable cannabis odors at the property line and in adjacent units or buildings. The certification must be signed and sealed by a licensed Professional Engineer (PE) registered in the State of Illinois. This certification must be included with the application at the time of submission.

**Item 11 --- Business Plan (\\\*)** Detailed Business Plan including: (a) executive summary; (b) description of proposed operations; (c) staffing plan with projected number of employees, roles, and hiring timeline; (d) financial projections for the first three (3) years of operations, including projected gross revenue, operating expenses, and capital expenditures; (e) inventory management and seed-to-sale tracking plan demonstrating integration with the Illinois COMPASS system; (f) customer flow and queue management plan; and (g) employee training program overview.

**Item 12 --- Community Engagement Documentation (\\\*)** Documentation of community engagement sessions, including: (a) evidence that the applicant held a minimum of two (2) community engagement sessions within the City of Evanston prior to application submission; (b) for each session: the date, time, and location; (c) proof that notice of each session was published in a newspaper of general circulation within Evanston (such as the *Evanston Roundtable*) and posted on the City\'s website at least **fourteen (14) calendar days** prior to the session date; (d) signed attendance sheets; (e) summary minutes of each session, including a description of community questions, concerns, and the applicant\'s responses; and (f) a narrative description of how community feedback has been incorporated into the applicant\'s plans.

**Item 13 --- Community Benefits Plan (\\\*)** Community Benefits Plan describing: (a) the applicant\'s proposed annual community benefit contribution (minimum \$25,000 or 1% of gross annual revenue, whichever is greater); (b) proposed beneficiary organizations or programs within Evanston; and (c) any additional community benefits the applicant proposes to provide (e.g., local hiring initiatives, educational programs, neighborhood improvement projects).

**Item 14 --- Diversity and Inclusion Plan (\\\*)** Diversity and Inclusion Plan describing: (a) the applicant\'s commitment to workforce diversity; (b) specific hiring goals and strategies; (c) partnerships with local workforce development organizations; and (d) for social equity applicants: a detailed plan demonstrating how the applicant will meet the requirement that at least 50% of its workforce be hired from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston, including identification of hiring pipelines and recruitment partners.

**Item 15 --- Environmental Sustainability Plan (\\\*)** Environmental Sustainability Plan addressing: (a) energy efficiency measures; (b) waste reduction and recycling program; (c) cannabis waste disposal procedures consistent with IEPA regulations and Illinois cannabis regulations; and (d) water conservation measures, if applicable.

**Item 16 --- Insurance Documentation (\\\* / †)** Evidence of insurance coverage meeting the following minimums, or a binding commitment letter from an insurance carrier confirming that the following coverages will be bound prior to permit issuance:

> • Commercial General Liability: \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> • Product Liability: \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> • Workers\' Compensation: Illinois statutory limits
>
> • Cannabis-Specific Crime/Theft Coverage: \$500,000

At the time of application (\\\*), the applicant may submit certificates of insurance for policies already in force, or binding commitment letters for policies to be bound prior to permit issuance. Final certificates of insurance for all required coverages (†) must be submitted prior to permit issuance. Final certificates must name the City of Evanston as an additional insured (except for Workers\' Compensation).

**Item 17 --- Financial Viability Documentation (\\\* / †)** Documentation of financial viability, including: (a) proof of capitalization (bank statements, investment account statements, or member/shareholder contribution documentation); (b) sources and uses of funds statement; (c) evidence of secured bonding capacity --- a commitment letter or preliminary approval from a surety company or financial institution confirming the applicant\'s ability to obtain a \$50,000 performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit; and (d) financial projections as included in the Business Plan. Items (a) through (d) are required at the time of application submission (\\\*). The \$50,000 performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit itself (†) must be submitted prior to permit issuance, but evidence of bonding capacity must be submitted with the application.

**Item 18 --- Social Equity Documentation (\\\*) (if applicable)** If claiming social equity applicant status: (a) documentation of qualifying individual\'s residency in an R3-designated census tract (e.g., utility bills, lease, or government-issued ID showing address, with census tract verification); (b) documentation of prior cannabis-related arrest or conviction, if applicable, including any records of expungement, sealing, or pardon; (c) signed commitment to participate in the City\'s Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum of two (2) years; and (d) signed commitment to hire at least 50% of workforce from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston.

**Item 19 --- Agent-in-Charge Designation (\\\*)** Designation of the Agent-in-Charge for the dispensary, including: (a) the individual\'s name and title; (b) proof that the individual holds or has applied for an Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent ID Card; and (c) a signed statement accepting responsibility for day-to-day compliance with all City and State regulations.

**Item 20 --- Zoning Confirmation (\\\* / †)** Confirmation of zoning compliance from the City\'s Zoning Division, or a pending application for special use approval if required. Cannabis dispensaries in the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use district require special use approval from the Zoning Board of Appeals. If special use approval has not yet been obtained, the applicant must submit evidence that a special use application has been filed or will be filed concurrently (\\\*). The dispensary permit will not be issued until special use approval is granted by the City Council (†).

**[Section 4: Application Review and Scoring Process]{.underline}**

Applications are first reviewed for completeness against the Master Checklist in Section 3 above. The completeness review targets a 30-day turnaround. If deficiencies are identified, the applicant receives a written deficiency notice and has fourteen (14) calendar days to cure. Once an application is deemed complete, it proceeds to substantive review.

Substantive review is conducted by a review panel composed of City staff from the Department of Community Development, the City Manager\'s Office, and the Evanston Police Department. Applications are scored according to the following rubric:

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Category**                          **Maximum Points**
  ------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Business Plan                         60

  Security Plan                         50

  Community Benefits Plan               40

  Financial Viability                   30

  Diversity & Inclusion Plan            25

  Experience & Qualifications           25

  Environmental Sustainability Plan     20

  **Total Base Score**                  **250**

  Social Equity Bonus (if applicable)   20

  **Maximum Possible Score**            **270**
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Business Plan (60 points):** Evaluates operational feasibility, staffing adequacy, financial projections, inventory management, and customer experience planning.

**Security Plan (50 points):** Evaluates comprehensiveness, compliance with all City and State security requirements, and the qualifications of any security consultant involved in plan preparation.

**Community Benefits Plan (40 points):** Evaluates the scope and commitment of the community benefits plan, the proposed annual contribution amount, and the quality and feasibility of proposed community programs.

**Financial Viability (30 points):** Evaluates the applicant\'s demonstrated financial capacity, including adequacy of capitalization, realistic financial projections, evidence of secured bonding capacity, and evidence of banking relationships. Applications that include a commitment letter or preliminary approval for the required performance bond from a surety company or financial institution will receive stronger consideration in this category.

**Diversity & Inclusion Plan (25 points):** Evaluates the applicant\'s commitment to workforce diversity, specificity of hiring goals, and quality of workforce development partnerships.

**Experience & Qualifications (25 points):** Evaluates the relevant experience and professional qualifications of the applicant\'s management team and key personnel.

**Environmental Sustainability Plan (20 points):** Evaluates the scope and feasibility of the applicant\'s environmental commitments.

Scoring is completed during the substantive review period. The public hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board is a separate process that occurs after scoring. After the hearing, the Zoning Board of Appeals issues its recommendation on any special use application, and the City Council takes final action on permit issuance.

**[Section 5: Post-Application Timeline and Milestones]{.underline}**

The following outlines the anticipated timeline from submission to permit issuance:

1\. **Submission.** Application and fee received by the Department of Community Development during the open application window (February 1, 2024 through May 31, 2024).

2\. **Completeness Review** (target: 30 days from submission). The City reviews the application for all required components per the Master Checklist. If deficiencies are found, the applicant is notified in writing and has 14 calendar days to cure.

3\. **Completeness Determination.** The City issues a completeness determination letter. The substantive review period and public hearing scheduling process begin.

4\. **Substantive Review** (target: 60 days from completeness determination). The review panel scores the application. During this period, the City may request additional information or clarification from the applicant.

5\. **Public Hearing --- Liquor & Cannabis Board** (within 45 days of completeness determination). Public hearing on the application. The applicant or its authorized representative must attend. Notice of the hearing is published at least 15 days prior to the hearing date.

6\. **Zoning Board of Appeals Hearing** (if special use required). Scheduled following the Liquor & Cannabis Board hearing. Requires mailed notice to all property owners within 500 feet of the proposed location at least 15 days prior to the hearing date.

7\. **City Council Action.** Final vote on permit issuance, incorporating scoring results, public hearing testimony, and the ZBA recommendation.

8\. **Permit Issuance.** Upon City Council approval, the applicant must submit all pre-issuance items (performance bond, final insurance certificates, annual permit fee of \$15,000) within 30 days. The permit is issued upon receipt of all pre-issuance items.

The City estimates a total processing timeline of approximately 120 to 150 days from submission to City Council action. Additional time may be required for scheduling, requests for additional information, hearing continuances, or other unforeseen circumstances. **The City is not responsible for any deadlines imposed by the State of Illinois or any other governmental authority. It is the applicant\'s sole responsibility to manage all concurrent licensing timelines.**

**[Section 6: Pre-Issuance Requirements]{.underline}**

The following items must be submitted after City Council approval but before the permit is physically issued. Applicants have **thirty (30) days** from the date of City Council approval to submit all pre-issuance items. Failure to submit within 30 days may result in forfeiture of the permit approval.

1\. **Annual Local Cannabis Dispensary Permit Fee.** \$15,000.00, payable by cashier\'s check or money order to \"City of Evanston.\" Due annually thereafter on the anniversary of permit issuance.

2\. **Performance Bond or Irrevocable Letter of Credit.** \$50,000, in a form acceptable to the City Attorney, naming the City of Evanston as obligee or beneficiary. Must be maintained for the duration of the permit.

3\. **Final Certificates of Insurance.** For all required coverages (Commercial General Liability, Product Liability, Workers\' Compensation, and Cannabis-Specific Crime/Theft) meeting the minimum limits stated in Checklist Item 16. Certificates must name the City of Evanston as an additional insured (except Workers\' Compensation).

4\. **Proof of Agent ID Cards.** Evidence that all employees who will handle cannabis products hold valid Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent ID Cards or have applications pending with the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation.

5\. **Final Special Use Approval.** If not already obtained, evidence of final special use approval granted by the City Council.

6\. **Completion of Build-Out.** Certificate of occupancy or evidence that the proposed dispensary location has been built out in conformance with the approved site plan and floor plan and has passed all required inspections (fire, building, and health).

**[Section 7: Post-Issuance Ongoing Compliance Obligations]{.underline}**

The following are continuing conditions of the Cannabis Dispensary Permit. Failure to maintain compliance may result in suspension or revocation of the permit.

1\. **Quarterly Compliance Self-Audits.** The permittee must conduct self-audits at the end of each calendar quarter (Q1: January--March; Q2: April--June; Q3: July--September; Q4: October--December). Audit results must be submitted to the Department of Community Development within fifteen (15) calendar days of the end of each quarter. Self-audits must cover security system functionality, video surveillance compliance, seed-to-sale tracking accuracy, operating hours compliance, employee Agent ID Card status, insurance maintenance, odor control system maintenance, and waste disposal compliance.

2\. **Annual Permit Renewal.** Permits must be renewed annually. Renewal applications must be submitted at least 60 days before permit expiration. Renewal fee: \$15,000. The City will re-verify social equity status at each renewal for applicants who received the social equity scoring bonus.

3\. **Insurance Maintenance.** All required insurance coverages must be maintained continuously. The City must be provided with 30 days\' written notice of any cancellation or material change in coverage.

4\. **Community Benefit Contributions.** Annual contribution of the greater of \$25,000 or 1% of gross annual revenue, due within 90 days of the end of each fiscal year. The permittee must submit an annual report documenting contributions and beneficiary organizations.

5\. **Social Equity Continuing Obligations** (if applicable). The permittee must: (a) maintain at least 50% of its workforce hired from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston, verified annually; and (b) participate in the City\'s Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum of two (2) years from the date of initial permit issuance. Failure to maintain social equity compliance may result in loss of social equity status at renewal and may affect continued permitting.

6\. **Odor Control Maintenance.** The odor mitigation system must be maintained in accordance with the approved Odor Mitigation Plan. Any material changes to HVAC or filtration systems require prior written approval from the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator.

7\. **Operating Hours.** 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily. No exceptions without prior written approval from the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator.

8\. **Maximum Occupancy.** The permittee must maintain customer occupancy at or below the maximum specified in the permit, calculated based on retail floor area per the ordinance.

9\. **State License Maintenance.** The permittee must maintain a valid State of Illinois adult-use dispensing organization license at all times. Lapse of the State license automatically suspends the City permit.

**[Section 8: Contact Information and Resources]{.underline}**

**Cannabis Licensing Coordinator** Renata Vasquez-Morris Department of Community Development Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center, 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, IL 60201

Phone: (847) 448-4311 \| Email: rvasquezmorris@cityofevanston.org

**Zoning Division** (Special Use Inquiries)

Phone: (847) 448-4350 \| Email: zoning@cityofevanston.org

**Evanston Police Department** (Security Plan Consultation)

Phone: (847) 866-5000 \| Email: policecommunity@cityofevanston.org

**Additional Resources:**

> • Evanston Municipal Code, Chapter 9, Article 5: Available at www.cityofevanston.org/government/city-code
>
> • State of Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/): Available at www.ilga.gov
>
> • Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, Cannabis Division: www.idfpr.com/profs/adultusecan.asp

Applicants with questions about specific submission requirements or the review process are encouraged to contact the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator. The City may hold pre-application conferences by appointment.

**The City of Evanston is not responsible for advising applicants regarding State licensing requirements, timelines, or deadlines. Applicants are solely responsible for managing compliance with all applicable State and local requirements concurrently.**

**[Appendix A: Checklist Summary Table]{.underline}**

The following table is provided for quick reference. Applicants may use the \"Status\" column to track their own preparation. See Section 3 of this Guide for full descriptions of each item.

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**          **Description**                                                       **Timing**        **Status**
  ----------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------- -----------------

  2                 Application Fee (\$5,000 --- cashier\'s check/money order)            \\\*              

  3                 State Cannabis License (certified copy with expiration date)          \\\*

  7                 Site Plan and Floor Plan (architect/engineer certified)               \\\*

  10                Odor Mitigation Plan (with PE certification)                          \\\*              

  11                Business Plan (3-year projections, COMPASS integration)               \\\*              

  12                Community Engagement Documentation (2 sessions, 14-day notice)        \\\*              

  13                Community Benefits Plan (min. \$25,000 or 1% revenue)                 \\\*              

  14                Diversity and Inclusion Plan                                          \\\*              

  15                Environmental Sustainability Plan                                     \\\*              

  16                Insurance Documentation (certificates or commitment letters)          \\\* / †          

  17                Financial Viability Documentation (incl. bonding capacity evidence)   \\\* / †          

  18                Social Equity Documentation (if applicable)                           \\\*

*End of Application Checklist and Submission Guide* *City of Evanston --- Department of Community Development --- Cannabis Licensing Division* *Publication Date: January 15, 2024*

Form Version 3.2 \| Revised January 12, 2024

**This application must be completed in its entirety. Incomplete applications will not be accepted. All information provided is subject to verification by the City of Evanston Department of Community Development, Cannabis Licensing Division, and such other City departments and agencies as may be necessary to evaluate the application.**

**[GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS AND IMPORTANT NOTICES]{.underline}**

**READ CAREFULLY BEFORE COMPLETING THIS APPLICATION**

All applicants for a City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit must carefully review and comply with the following instructions. Failure to comply with any instruction may result in the application being deemed incomplete or rejected.

1\. **Completeness Requirement.** All fields in this application must be completed or marked \"N/A\" if a particular field is not applicable to the applicant. Do not leave any field blank. Applications with blank fields will be returned without processing.

2\. **Submission Format.** This application must be submitted in hard copy (three (3) complete, bound copies, including all attachments and exhibits) and one (1) electronic copy on a USB flash drive in PDF format. All documents must be clearly legible and organized with tabs corresponding to each section and exhibit of the application. Hard copies and the USB drive must be delivered in person to the Cannabis Licensing Division at the Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center, 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, Illinois 60201, during regular business hours (Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM).

3\. **Application Fee.** A non-refundable application fee of Five Thousand Dollars (\$5,000.00) must accompany the submission of this application. Payment must be made by certified check, cashier\'s check, or wire transfer payable to the \"City of Evanston.\" Personal checks, credit cards, and cash payments will not be accepted. The application fee covers the administrative costs of processing, reviewing, and evaluating the application and is non-refundable regardless of whether the application is approved, denied, or withdrawn.

4\. **Annual Permit Fee.** Upon issuance of a cannabis dispensary permit, the permittee must pay an annual local permit fee of Fifteen Thousand Dollars (\$15,000.00). The annual permit fee is due and payable at the time of permit issuance and at each annual renewal thereafter.

5\. **Performance Bond or Letter of Credit.** A performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit in the amount of Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$50,000.00) must be submitted prior to permit issuance. The bond or letter of credit must name the City of Evanston as the obligee or beneficiary and must remain in full force and effect for the duration of the permit term.

6\. **Processing Timeline.** The City will process applications in the order received. The City\'s target is to complete the initial review of each application within ninety (90) days from the date the application is determined to be complete. The 90-day review period begins upon the date of the formal completeness determination, not the date of submission. Applicants will receive written notice of the completeness determination. The City does not guarantee processing within the 90-day target period and shall not be liable for any delays.

7\. **Public Hearing.** A public hearing before the Evanston Liquor and Cannabis Board is required for each application. The public hearing will be scheduled within forty-five (45) days of the completeness determination. Applicants will receive written notice of the hearing date and must attend or send an authorized representative. The Liquor and Cannabis Board will make a recommendation to the City Council regarding approval or denial of the application.

8\. **Special Use Approval.** Cannabis dispensaries proposed for locations within C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning districts require special use approval from the Zoning Board of Appeals and a final vote of approval by the Evanston City Council. Applicants proposing locations in C1a districts should apply for special use approval concurrent with this permit application to avoid processing delays. Special use applications are filed separately with the Zoning Division of the Department of Community Development.

9\. **State License Requirement.** All applicants must hold a valid State of Illinois conditional or permanent adult-use dispensing organization license issued by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation at the time of application submission. Applications from entities that do not hold a valid state license will not be accepted.

10\. **Municipal Code Reference.** Applicants are strongly encouraged to review City of Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5, which contains the full regulatory requirements governing cannabis dispensaries within the City. The Municipal Code is available online at the City\'s website or for review in person at the City Clerk\'s office. This application form is a supplement to, and not a replacement for, the requirements set forth in the Municipal Code.

11\. **Public Records Disclosure.** This application and all materials submitted with it are public records subject to disclosure under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. Information specifically exempt from disclosure under state law (e.g., Social Security numbers, personal financial account numbers) will be redacted prior to public disclosure. Applicants who believe that specific materials qualify for a FOIA exemption must clearly mark such materials as \"CONFIDENTIAL --- FOIA EXEMPTION CLAIMED\" and identify the applicable statutory exemption at the time of submission.

12\. **Supplemental Information.** The City reserves the right to request supplemental information, documentation, or clarification from the applicant at any time during the review period. Failure to respond to a request for supplemental information within the time period specified by the City may result in the application being deemed abandoned.

**Question 21: Social Equity Commitments**

Social Equity Applicants must commit to the following conditions as part of the cannabis dispensary permit. These conditions are ongoing obligations that will be monitored and enforced by the Department of Community Development throughout the permit term. Failure to maintain these conditions may result in loss of social equity status, fines, permit suspension, and/or permit revocation.

> \(a\) **R3 Workforce Hiring Requirement.** Hire at least fifty percent (50%) of the dispensary workforce from residents of R3-designated census tracts within the City of Evanston. This requirement applies to both full-time and part-time employees and will be audited annually by the Department of Community Development. For purposes of this requirement, \"dispensary workforce\" means all individuals employed by or engaged as independent contractors at the dispensary location, excluding the applicant\'s principal officers and legal counsel.
>
> \(b\) **Mentorship Program Participation.** Participate in the City of Evanston Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum of two (2) consecutive years from the date of permit issuance. Participation includes attending all scheduled mentorship sessions, completing assigned program milestones, and cooperating with program administrators.
>
> \(c\) **Annual Compliance Reporting.** Submit an annual Social Equity Compliance Report documenting workforce demographics, R3 hiring statistics, mentorship program participation, and community benefit activities. The annual report is due at the time of permit renewal.
>
> \(d\) **Re-Verification at Renewal.** Social Equity Applicant status will be re-verified at each annual permit renewal. Failure to meet social equity conditions at the time of renewal may result in loss of the social equity scoring designation and could affect permit renewal. The 20-point Social Equity scoring bonus was applied at the initial scoring stage; however, ongoing failure to maintain social equity commitments may constitute a violation of permit conditions and may be grounds for non-renewal or revocation.
