**APPLICATION SCORING RUBRIC --- FOR INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES**

Applications will be evaluated and scored by the Cannabis Licensing Review Panel using the following rubric. The Cannabis Licensing Review Panel is composed of representatives from the Department of Community Development, the Evanston Police Department, the City Manager\'s Office, and two community members appointed by the Mayor. The maximum base score is 250 points. Social Equity Applicants who have been verified as meeting the requirements of Section 9-5-19 of the Municipal Code receive a 20-point bonus, for a maximum possible score of 270 points.

The scoring rubric is provided for informational purposes to assist applicants in preparing their applications. The Cannabis Licensing Review Panel retains discretion in the application of the rubric and in the assignment of point values within each category.

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Category**                  **Maximum Points**      **Scoring Criteria**
  ----------------------------- ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Business Plan                 60                      Comprehensiveness of business plan; quality of market analysis; operational detail and feasibility; management team experience and qualifications; financial sustainability and cash flow adequacy; contingency planning

  Security Plan                 50                      Surveillance system coverage and quality; access control adequacy; alarm system design and monitoring; cash handling and transportation protocols; employee security training; overall compliance with all City and State security requirements

  Community Benefits            40                      Community benefit contribution plan and projected amount; local hiring commitments; neighborhood engagement strategy; partnerships with community organizations; responsiveness to community feedback from engagement sessions

  Financial Viability           30                      Capitalization adequacy; reliability and diversity of funding sources; **evidence of secured bonding capacity (10 pts)**; realism and conservatism of financial projections; cash reserve adequacy for first 12 months of operations

  Diversity Plan                25                      Workforce diversity goals and specificity; hiring commitments from R3-designated areas; vendor diversity initiatives; inclusive business practices; cultural competency training

  Environmental Plan            20                      Odor mitigation system effectiveness and P.E. certification; energy efficiency measures; waste reduction and recycling programs; sustainable practices and packaging initiatives

  Experience / Qualifications   25                      Cannabis industry experience of ownership and management team; relevant professional credentials and certifications; management track record in regulated industries; regulatory compliance history

  **Subtotal**                  **250**                 

  Social Equity Bonus           20                      Verified Social Equity Applicant status per Section 9-5-19 of the Municipal Code

  **Maximum Total**             **270**                 
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Note Regarding Financial Viability --- Bonding Capacity (10 Points):** Evidence of secured bonding capacity includes a commitment letter from a licensed surety company or financial institution confirming the applicant\'s ability to obtain a performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit in the amount of \$50,000 naming the City of Evanston as obligee or beneficiary. The commitment letter must be on the surety company\'s or financial institution\'s letterhead, signed by an authorized officer, and dated within sixty (60) days of the application submission date. **Applicants who do not provide such evidence at the time of application will receive zero (0) points in this sub-category.** The remaining 20 points in the Financial Viability category will be scored based on the other criteria listed above.

**[SECTION 9: COMPLIANCE AND REPORTING OBLIGATIONS]{.underline}**

**Question 29: Quarterly Compliance Self-Audits**

Permittees must conduct quarterly compliance self-audits and submit written audit results to the Department of Community Development, Cannabis Licensing Division, within fifteen (15) days of the end of each calendar quarter. Calendar quarters are defined as follows:

> • Q1: January 1 through March 31
>
> • Q2: April 1 through June 30
>
> • Q3: July 1 through September 30
>
> • Q4: October 1 through December 31

**Note:** The first audit period begins on the date of permit issuance and ends on the last day of that calendar quarter, regardless of whether a full quarter has elapsed. Audit results for this initial period are due within fifteen (15) days of the quarter end. For example, if a permit is issued on November 15, the first audit period would cover November 15 through December 31, and the audit report would be due on or before January 15 of the following year.

Self-audits must cover, at a minimum, the following areas:

> \(a\) Inventory reconciliation --- comparison of physical inventory counts with records maintained in the COMPASS system;
>
> \(b\) Security system functionality verification --- confirmation that all surveillance cameras, alarm systems, access control systems, and panic alarms are operational;
>
> \(c\) Employee credential verification --- confirmation that all dispensary agents hold valid Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Cards;
>
> \(d\) Customer complaint log review --- summary of all customer complaints received during the quarter and actions taken in response;
>
> \(e\) Signage and occupancy compliance --- confirmation that all required signage is in place and that the posted occupancy limit has not been exceeded; and
>
> \(f\) Odor control system maintenance verification --- confirmation that the carbon filtration system and all odor control equipment have been inspected and maintained in accordance with the manufacturer\'s recommended maintenance schedule.

Applicant understands and will comply with quarterly self-audit requirements.

**Question 30: Annual Reporting**

In addition to the quarterly self-audits described above, permittees must submit the following documents annually at the time of permit renewal:

> \(a\) Financial statements (audited or reviewed by a Certified Public Accountant licensed in the State of Illinois);
>
> \(b\) Community benefit contribution documentation, including proof of payment and a summary of how contribution funds were allocated;
>
> \(c\) Social Equity Compliance Report (if the permittee holds Social Equity Applicant status), documenting workforce demographics, R3 hiring statistics, and mentorship program participation;
>
> \(d\) Updated certificates of insurance demonstrating that all required coverages remain in force; and
>
> \(e\) Current employee roster with verification that all dispensary agents hold valid Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Cards.

result:
**[GREENLEAF THERAPEUTICS LLC]{.underline}**

**[Business Plan: Executive Summary and Operations Plan]{.underline}**

**Prepared in Support of City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application**

*\[Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC Logo\]*

**Proposed Dispensary Location:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B Evanston, IL 60201

**Date:** April 2024

**CONFIDENTIAL AND PROPRIETARY**

**Contact:** Dara Okonkwo, Managing Member Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC dokonkwo@greenleaftherapeuticsllc.com (872) 555-0193

**[1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY]{.underline}**

Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC (\"Greenleaf\" or the \"Company\") intends to open and operate a retail adult-use cannabis dispensary at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201, serving both adult-use recreational customers and registered medical cannabis patients in full compliance with all applicable State of Illinois and City of Evanston regulations. The Company\'s mission is to establish a safe, welcoming, and community-oriented dispensary that provides high-quality cannabis products while reinvesting meaningfully in the communities most impacted by cannabis prohibition.

**Company Formation.** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC is an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2023, under the Illinois Limited Liability Company Act. The Company\'s Employer Identification Number is 88-3921047. Greenleaf\'s registered agent is Northwest Statutory Services, located at 205 N. Michigan Ave., Suite 810, Chicago, IL 60601.

**Ownership.** Greenleaf is owned by two members: Dara Okonkwo, who holds a 60% membership interest and serves as Managing Member, and Felix Ramachandran, who holds a 40% membership interest as a Silent Member. Total initial capitalization of the Company is \$1,850,000, comprising \$1,110,000 contributed by Okonkwo and \$740,000 contributed by Ramachandran. All capital has been contributed as equity, and the Company carries no debt financing.

**State Licensing Status.** Greenleaf holds State of Illinois Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License No. ADC-285-0147, issued September 22, 2023, with a one-year conditional term expiring September 21, 2024. Pursuant to State requirements, conditional licenses must be converted to permanent status within 180 days of local permit issuance. The Company anticipates submitting its City application by May 15, 2024, and based on the City\'s target 90-day review period, expects to receive its local permit in time to commence the 180-day conversion process well before the conditional license expiration. Greenleaf is prepared to fulfill all State conversion requirements promptly upon receipt of the local permit.

**Local Application.** Greenleaf is filing its application under the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit program. The City\'s application window opened on February 1, 2024, and the deadline for submission is May 31, 2024. Greenleaf is targeting submission on or before May 15, 2024, to allow maximum processing time within the City\'s review cycle.

**Social Equity Designation.** Greenleaf\'s Managing Member, Dara Okonkwo, qualifies as a social equity applicant under the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act. Okonkwo is a resident of a census tract (8092.02) within Evanston that has been designated as an R3 (Restorative, Reinvest, Renew) area. Additionally, Okonkwo has a prior cannabis-related conviction --- a 2016 misdemeanor for possession of cannabis in an amount less than 30 grams, adjudicated in Cook County, Illinois, which was subsequently expunged in 2020. As a social equity applicant holding a majority ownership position, Greenleaf intends to claim the 20-point social equity bonus on the City\'s 250-point scoring rubric, for a maximum possible score of 270 points.

**Market Opportunity.** The City of Evanston currently has one licensed cannabis dispensary: Prairie Wellness Dispensary, located at 807 Church St., approximately 2,100 feet from Greenleaf\'s proposed location. Greenleaf\'s position on Sherman Avenue --- a high-foot-traffic commercial corridor --- provides meaningful geographic differentiation. The Company projects Year 1 gross revenue of \$3,200,000, based on conservative assumptions regarding customer traffic, average transaction value, and product mix that are detailed in Section 4 of this business plan.

**Key Advisors.** Greenleaf has assembled an experienced team of professional advisors to support its application and operations:

> • **Legal Counsel:** Hargrove & Sinclair LLP, 77 W. Wacker Dr., Suite 3200, Chicago, IL 60601 (partner Nadia Petrova, IL Bar #6312441; associate Jordan Mackey, IL Bar #6425098)
>
> • **Security and Compliance Consultant:** Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC (principal Marcus Wren)
>
> • **Cannabis Banking Partner:** Ridgeline Community Bank, 820 Davis St., Evanston, IL 60201

**Build-Out and Opening.** Greenleaf estimates a total build-out cost of \$425,000 for the proposed dispensary location. The build-out scope includes construction of a secure vestibule entry, hardened secure storage room, retail floor installations, office and employee areas, HVAC system upgrades, surveillance system installation, and ADA-compliant accessibility throughout. The Company targets a build-out period of approximately 90 days following local permit issuance, with an additional 30 days allocated for systems testing, staff training, and final regulatory inspections. Greenleaf anticipates opening for business within 120 days of receiving its City of Evanston dispensary permit.

This business plan sets forth in detail the Company\'s organizational structure, proposed facility, market analysis, operations plan, financial plan, social equity and diversity commitments, community engagement activities, regulatory compliance framework, and risk mitigation strategies. Greenleaf is committed to operating a best-in-class dispensary that serves the Evanston community responsibly and contributes to the City\'s goals for equitable cannabis commerce.

**[2. COMPANY OVERVIEW AND ORGANIZATIONAL STRUCTURE]{.underline}**

**[2.1 Entity Information]{.underline}**

Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC is organized under the laws of the State of Illinois as a limited liability company. The following table summarizes the Company\'s key entity information:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**                                         **Detail**
  ------------------------------------------------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Full Legal Name                                  Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC

  Formation Date                                   March 14, 2023

  State of Formation                               Illinois

  Governing Statute                                Illinois Limited Liability Company Act (805 ILCS 180/)

  EIN                                              88-3921047

  Principal Office / Proposed Dispensary Address   1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201

  Registered Agent                                 Northwest Statutory Services, 205 N. Michigan Ave., Suite 810, Chicago, IL 60601

  State Conditional License                        ADC-285-0147, issued September 22, 2023; expires September 21, 2024

  ILSOS File Number                                LLC-07842319
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Company is in good standing with the Illinois Secretary of State and has filed all required annual reports and documentation. The Company\'s Articles of Organization and Operating Agreement are on file and available for regulatory review upon request. The principal office address listed above is also the proposed dispensary location, which will serve as both the Company\'s primary place of business and retail cannabis establishment following build-out and permit issuance.

**[2.2 Ownership and Management]{.underline}**

Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC has two members, whose respective ownership interests, capital contributions, and roles are described below.

**Dara Okonkwo --- Managing Member (60% Ownership Interest)**

Dara Okonkwo, age 38, is Greenleaf\'s Managing Member and the Company\'s designated Agent-in-Charge for purposes of the State of Illinois dispensary licensing requirements. Okonkwo is a resident of Evanston, Illinois, residing within census tract 8092.02, which has been designated as an R3 (Restorative, Reinvest, Renew) area by the State of Illinois. Okonkwo is a licensed pharmacist in the State of Illinois, holding active Pharmacist License No. 051-302887. Her pharmaceutical background provides her with extensive knowledge of controlled substance handling, patient consultation, regulatory compliance, and inventory management --- all directly transferable to cannabis dispensary operations.

Okonkwo qualifies as a social equity applicant under the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act based on two independent qualifying criteria: (a) her residency in an R3-designated census tract, and (b) her prior cannabis-related conviction --- a 2016 misdemeanor for possession of cannabis in an amount less than 30 grams, adjudicated in Cook County, Illinois. This conviction was expunged in 2020 through the Cook County expungement process. Okonkwo\'s social equity status, combined with her majority ownership position of 60%, establishes Greenleaf\'s eligibility for the social equity designation and associated scoring bonus.

As Managing Member, Okonkwo is responsible for all day-to-day management and operational decisions of the dispensary. Her duties include regulatory compliance oversight, personnel management, vendor relationships, financial management, and community engagement. Okonkwo has contributed \$1,110,000 in equity capital to the Company, representing 60% of the Company\'s total initial capitalization.

**Felix Ramachandran --- Silent Member (40% Ownership Interest)**

Felix Ramachandran, age 44, is a Silent Member of Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC, holding a 40% membership interest. Ramachandran is a resident of Wilmette, Illinois, and is currently employed as Vice President of Operations at Lakefront Distribution Co., a non-cannabis logistics and distribution company based in the greater Chicago area. Ramachandran brings significant operational and business management expertise to the Company, including experience with supply chain logistics, warehouse management, regulatory compliance in the food and beverage distribution industry, and corporate finance.

As a Silent Member, Ramachandran does not participate in the day-to-day management of the dispensary. His role is limited to providing capital investment and strategic operational advice as requested by the Managing Member. Ramachandran has contributed \$740,000 in equity capital to the Company, representing 40% of the total initial capitalization. Ramachandran has no criminal history and does not hold professional licenses in the healthcare or pharmaceutical fields. As a person holding more than 5% ownership interest in a cannabis dispensary applicant, Ramachandran will undergo all required background checks and disclosure procedures mandated by both the City of Evanston and the State of Illinois.

**Organizational Structure**

The Company\'s organizational structure is designed to ensure clear lines of authority, accountability, and regulatory compliance. The organizational chart (see Appendix A for the full chart) is structured as follows:

> • **Managing Member / Agent-in-Charge** (Dara Okonkwo) --- Ultimate authority over all dispensary operations, compliance, and personnel decisions.
>
> • Reporting directly to the Managing Member:
>
> • **Dispensary Manager** --- Responsible for day-to-day retail floor operations, customer service standards, staffing schedules, and front-of-house management. (Position to be hired.)
>
> • **Inventory Manager** --- Responsible for product receiving, storage, tracking, inventory reconciliation, and integration with the COMPASS seed-to-sale platform. (Position to be hired.)
>
> • **Compliance Officer** --- Responsible for maintaining all regulatory filings, audit preparation, insurance and bonding documentation, employee Agent ID card tracking, and liaison with City and State regulators. (Position to be hired.)
>
> • Reporting to the Dispensary Manager:
>
> • **Dispensary Agents (Budtenders)** --- 6 positions; responsible for customer consultation, product recommendations, and point-of-sale transactions.
>
> • **Security / Reception Staff** --- 2 positions; responsible for vestibule operations, customer ID verification, and facility security monitoring.
>
> • **Administrative Assistant** --- 1 position; responsible for administrative support, scheduling, and record-keeping.

Greenleaf\'s social equity status flows from Okonkwo\'s 60% qualifying ownership interest. The Company is committed to meeting all social equity conditions imposed by both the State and the City of Evanston, including workforce hiring requirements. Greenleaf has committed to hiring at least 50% of its workforce from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston, and details of the social equity hiring plan are set forth in Section 7 below.

**[2.3 Professional Advisors and Consultants]{.underline}**

Greenleaf has engaged the following professional advisors and consultants to support its application, build-out, and ongoing operations:

**Legal Counsel --- Hargrove & Sinclair LLP** 77 W. Wacker Dr., Suite 3200, Chicago, IL 60601. Partner Nadia Petrova (IL Bar #6312441) and associate Jordan Mackey (IL Bar #6425098) serve as Greenleaf\'s outside counsel for all matters related to cannabis regulatory compliance, local permitting, state license conversion, corporate governance, and ongoing legal advisory services. Hargrove & Sinclair has represented multiple cannabis licensees in Illinois and brings deep expertise in the State\'s cannabis regulatory framework and the City of Evanston\'s local permitting process.

**Security and Compliance Consultant --- Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC** Principal: Marcus Wren. Pinnacle Compliance Advisors has been engaged to design Greenleaf\'s comprehensive security plan, including the surveillance system layout, access control systems, panic alarm integration, and the dispensary\'s seed-to-sale tracking system integration with the Illinois COMPASS platform. Pinnacle has experience designing security and compliance systems for multiple cannabis dispensaries and cultivation facilities throughout Illinois and brings a thorough understanding of both State and local security requirements.

**Cannabis Banking Partner --- Ridgeline Community Bank** 820 Davis St., Evanston, IL 60201. Ridgeline Community Bank is a participating institution in a cannabis-specific banking program that provides deposit, checking, and cash management services to licensed cannabis businesses in Illinois. Ridgeline requires quarterly compliance attestations from its cannabis business clients as a condition of maintaining the banking relationship. Greenleaf has established its primary banking relationship with Ridgeline and intends to maintain all financial accounts through this institution.

**Surety and Bonding --- Thornfield Surety Group** Greenleaf has initiated preliminary discussions with Thornfield Surety Group regarding the \$50,000 performance bond required for dispensary permit issuance under the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit program. A formal commitment letter is expected prior to application submission. Thornfield specializes in surety products for regulated industries, including cannabis and alcohol licensing.

**Insurance --- Canopy Risk Underwriters** Greenleaf is working with Canopy Risk Underwriters, a cannabis specialty insurance carrier, to secure the comprehensive insurance coverage required for dispensary operations. Canopy Risk provides tailored coverage lines for cannabis businesses, including general liability, product liability, cannabis-specific crime and theft coverage, and property insurance. Policy placement is in progress and is expected to be completed prior to permit issuance.

**[3. PROPOSED LOCATION AND FACILITY]{.underline}**

**[3.1 Site Description]{.underline}**

Greenleaf\'s proposed dispensary is located at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201. The site is situated within the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning district, in which cannabis dispensaries are permitted as a special use. The property is owned by Kimball Properties Inc., an Illinois corporation, which serves as the landlord.

Greenleaf executed a commercial lease agreement with Kimball Properties Inc. on January 8, 2024. The lease provides for an initial term of seven (7) years, with two (2) three-year renewal options exercisable at Greenleaf\'s election. Kimball Properties has acknowledged and expressly consented to the use of the premises as a licensed cannabis dispensary, and the lease contains provisions specifically contemplating cannabis retail operations. A summary of the lease terms is included as a separate submission document and is referenced in Appendix C to this business plan.

The premises comprise a total of 3,200 square feet, allocated as follows:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Area**                   **Square Footage**      **Percentage**
  -------------------------- ----------------------- -----------------------
  Retail Floor Area          1,600 sq. ft.           50.0%

  Secure Storage Area        800 sq. ft.             25.0%

  Office and Employee Area   600 sq. ft.             18.75%

  Vestibule / Waiting Area   200 sq. ft.             6.25%

  **Total**                  **3,200 sq. ft.**       **100%**
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------

The retail floor area provides ample space for product display, point-of-sale stations, customer consultation areas, and accessible circulation. The secure storage area will be constructed as a hardened, access-controlled room for all cannabis product inventory. The office and employee area will include management offices, a break room, and training space. The vestibule and waiting area will serve as the controlled entry point where all customers are verified before admission to the retail floor.

**[3.2 Proximity to Sensitive Uses]{.underline}**

Greenleaf has conducted a thorough analysis of the proposed dispensary location\'s proximity to sensitive uses as defined by the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit ordinance. All distances referenced below are measured as straight-line distances from the nearest property boundary of the dispensary premises to the nearest property boundary of the sensitive use location. The following table summarizes the proximity analysis:

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Sensitive Use**                      **Name and Address**                                        **Distance (Straight-Line)**   **City Buffer Requirement**   **Status**
  -------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------ ----------------------------- ---------------
  Nearest K-12 School                    Chute Middle School, 1400 Oakton St., Evanston              \~1,180 feet                   1,000 feet minimum            **COMPLIANT**

  Nearest Daycare Center                 Tiny Acorns Learning Center, 1635 Hinman Ave., Evanston     \~890 feet                     500 feet minimum              **COMPLIANT**

  Nearest Licensed Cannabis Dispensary   Prairie Wellness Dispensary, 807 Church St., Evanston       \~2,100 feet                   1,500 feet minimum            **COMPLIANT**

  Nearest Religious Institution          First United Methodist Church of Evanston, 516 Church St.   \~1,450 feet                   No buffer required            N/A
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The proposed location at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, satisfies all applicable buffer distance requirements established by the City of Evanston. The nearest K-12 school, Chute Middle School, is located approximately 1,180 feet away, comfortably exceeding the City\'s 1,000-foot minimum buffer. The nearest daycare facility, Tiny Acorns Learning Center, is approximately 890 feet distant, well in excess of the 500-foot minimum. The nearest existing licensed cannabis dispensary, Prairie Wellness Dispensary, is approximately 2,100 feet from the proposed location, exceeding the 1,500-foot inter-dispensary buffer by a substantial margin. The City of Evanston ordinance does not impose a buffer requirement for religious institutions; however, Greenleaf notes that the nearest church is approximately 1,450 feet from the proposed location.

Detailed site maps and measurement documentation are included in the site plan submitted with the application (see Appendix D).

**[3.3 Build-Out Plan]{.underline}**

Greenleaf has budgeted \$425,000 for the complete build-out and tenant improvement of the premises at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B. The build-out scope encompasses all physical construction, systems installation, and finishing work necessary to prepare the facility for cannabis dispensary operations in compliance with all City and State requirements. The major components of the build-out include:

**Secure Vestibule Entry.** Construction of a controlled-access vestibule at the front entrance of the facility, equipped with an ID-verification station, a controlled door-release system, and security cameras. The vestibule will serve as the sole customer entry point, ensuring that all persons entering the retail floor have been verified as 21 years of age or older (or as registered medical cannabis patients) before admission.

**Secure Storage Room.** Construction of an 800-square-foot hardened secure storage room within the facility for the storage of all cannabis product inventory. The storage room will feature reinforced walls, a steel-core door with electronic access control (key-card or biometric), and dedicated surveillance cameras covering all entry points and interior areas. Access will be restricted to authorized personnel only, and all access events will be logged electronically.

**Retail Floor.** The 1,600-square-foot retail floor will be fitted with product display cases, consultation counters, and a minimum of four (4) point-of-sale stations. The design will prioritize customer flow, accessibility, and a professional retail environment. All display cases will feature locked, tamper-resistant construction, and products will be accessible only to dispensary agents.

**Office and Employee Area.** The 600-square-foot office and employee area will include a management office, a Compliance Officer workstation, an employee break room, and a training area. This space will also house the on-site server equipment for the surveillance and seed-to-sale tracking systems.

**HVAC System Upgrades.** The existing HVAC system will be upgraded to support the environmental control requirements of a cannabis dispensary, including temperature and humidity management in the secure storage area and air quality controls throughout the facility.

**Odor Control and Environmental Considerations.** Greenleaf will install a commercial-grade carbon filtration system integrated with the facility\'s HVAC system to mitigate odor migration to adjacent units and public areas. The system will include activated carbon filters on all exhaust vents and negative pressure maintenance in the secure storage area. Greenleaf has identified several commercial carbon filtration vendors and will finalize specifications during the build-out engineering phase. The Company is committed to being a responsible neighbor and will ensure that odor controls meet or exceed industry standards for cannabis retail facilities.

**Surveillance System.** A comprehensive surveillance system will be installed throughout the facility, including high-definition cameras at all entry and exit points, throughout the retail floor, in the secure storage room, in the vestibule, and at all point-of-sale stations. The surveillance system specifications are detailed in the separate security plan prepared by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC.

**ADA Compliance.** All build-out work will be designed and constructed in compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and all applicable local accessibility codes. Accessible pathways, counters, and restroom facilities will be incorporated into the design.

**Timeline and Contractor Selection.** Greenleaf targets completion of the build-out within 90 days of local permit issuance. An additional 30 days will be allocated for systems testing, staff training, and pre-opening regulatory inspections, for a target opening within 120 days of permit issuance. The general contractor for the build-out will be selected through a competitive bidding process, with preference given to qualified local and minority-owned contracting firms consistent with Greenleaf\'s diversity procurement commitments. All construction work will be subject to City of Evanston building permits and inspections.

**[4. MARKET ANALYSIS AND COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE]{.underline}**

**[4.1 Illinois Cannabis Market Overview]{.underline}**

The State of Illinois legalized adult-use cannabis through the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (CRTA), which took effect on January 1, 2020. Since legalization, the Illinois cannabis market has experienced significant and sustained growth, establishing itself as one of the largest and most dynamic cannabis markets in the Midwest. Statewide adult-use sales have grown steadily year over year, driven by expanding consumer awareness, increasing product diversity, and the gradual build-out of the licensed dispensary network across the state.

The Illinois market operates as a dual-track system, with both medical cannabis (available since the Compassionate Use of Medical Cannabis Program Act of 2014) and adult-use recreational cannabis served by licensed dispensing organizations. This dual-track structure creates cross-selling opportunities for dispensaries licensed to serve both customer segments, as registered medical patients benefit from lower tax rates and broader product access, while adult-use customers drive volume sales. Greenleaf intends to operate under both the adult-use and medical dispensary frameworks to maximize its addressable market.

The CRTA also established a groundbreaking social equity licensing initiative designed to expand market access for individuals and communities disproportionately affected by the enforcement of cannabis prohibition. Social equity applicants receive priority consideration in the licensing process, and the State has committed substantial resources to mentorship, technical assistance, and reduced-fee programs for social equity licensees. Greenleaf\'s social equity designation positions the Company to benefit from these programs while fulfilling the legislative intent of equitable market participation.

Industry analysts project continued market expansion in Illinois through at least 2028, driven by population density in the greater Chicagoland area, tourism, and the ongoing normalization of cannabis consumption. Greenleaf\'s entry into the Evanston market aligns with this favorable trajectory and positions the Company to capture meaningful market share during a period of sustained demand growth.

**[4.2 Evanston Local Market]{.underline}**

The City of Evanston, with a population of approximately 78,000 residents, represents a highly attractive local market for cannabis retail. Evanston is a university town --- home to Northwestern University --- with a progressive local government, a diverse and educated population, and a demonstrated consumer demand for cannabis products. The City\'s decision to establish a local cannabis dispensary permit program reflects both broad community acceptance of regulated cannabis commerce and a desire to capture the economic and social benefits of local cannabis businesses, including tax revenue, employment, and community benefit contributions.

At present, the City of Evanston has only one licensed cannabis dispensary: Prairie Wellness Dispensary, located at 807 Church St. Given Evanston\'s population size and consumer demographics, the market appears significantly underserved by a single dispensary. Greenleaf\'s proposed location at 1420 Sherman Ave. is situated in a high-foot-traffic commercial corridor in central Evanston, approximately 2,100 feet from Prairie Wellness, providing meaningful geographic differentiation and convenient access for customers in the northern and central portions of the City.

Greenleaf expects to capture market share through several competitive advantages: a superior customer experience featuring knowledgeable, well-trained dispensary agents; a strong social equity narrative that resonates with Evanston\'s community values; an accessible and welcoming retail environment; and extended operating hours of 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily, the maximum permitted under both City ordinance and State law. The Company\'s pharmacist-led management structure also distinguishes Greenleaf from competitors, as Okonkwo\'s pharmaceutical expertise informs a consultative, patient-centered approach to cannabis retail that appeals to both medical patients and recreational consumers seeking guidance and product education.

Greenleaf\'s revenue projections are based on conservative assumptions derived from comparable single-location dispensary operations in suburban Chicagoland markets, adjusted for Evanston\'s specific demographics and competitive landscape. The following table summarizes projected gross revenue for the first three years of operations:

These projections are conservative relative to published data for comparable single-location dispensaries in the suburban Chicago market, and they do not assume the capture of any disproportionate share of the existing market currently served by Prairie Wellness. Rather, projections anticipate that Greenleaf\'s entry into the market will expand overall market demand through improved access, extended hours, and a differentiated customer experience.

**[5. OPERATIONS PLAN]{.underline}**

**[5.1 Operating Hours and Customer Flow]{.underline}**

**Operating Hours.** Greenleaf will operate daily from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, which represents the maximum operating hours permitted under both the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit ordinance and the State of Illinois regulations governing adult-use cannabis dispensing organizations. These extended hours are designed to maximize customer convenience and accommodate a wide range of schedules, including early-morning medical patients, daytime shoppers, and evening customers.

**Customer Flow Process.** Greenleaf has designed a structured customer flow process to ensure safety, regulatory compliance, and a positive customer experience:

> 1\. **Entry and Vestibule.** Customers enter the facility through the front entrance into the vestibule/waiting area (200 sq. ft.), which serves as a controlled access point. The vestibule is designed to accommodate a reasonable queue during peak hours and is equipped with seating, signage regarding dispensary rules and age requirements, and security cameras.
>
> 2\. **ID Verification.** A trained dispensary agent stationed in the vestibule verifies each customer\'s government-issued identification to confirm that the customer is at least 21 years of age. Medical cannabis patients present their valid Illinois medical cannabis patient card in addition to government-issued ID. The verification system is equipped with technology capable of detecting fraudulent identification documents. No customer is admitted to the retail floor without completed verification.
>
> 3\. **Retail Floor Admission.** Upon successful ID verification, the customer is admitted through a controlled door-release system into the retail floor area (1,600 sq. ft.). The retail floor features product display cases, consultation counters, and point-of-sale stations staffed by trained dispensary agents.
>
> 4\. **Consultation and Purchase.** Customers consult with dispensary agents regarding available products, receive recommendations and product education as desired, and complete their purchase at one of the point-of-sale stations. All transactions are recorded in the seed-to-sale tracking system in real time.
>
> 5\. **Exit.** After completing their purchase, customers exit the retail floor through the vestibule and out the front entrance. The one-way traffic flow ensures orderly customer movement and supports security monitoring.

**Maximum Retail Occupancy.** The maximum number of customers permitted on the retail floor at any one time is 40, based on the retail floor area of 1,600 square feet and the standard occupancy calculation of one customer per 40 square feet of retail floor area (1,600 ÷ 40 = 40). Greenleaf will implement a queue management system to ensure that retail floor occupancy does not exceed this limit. During peak periods, additional customers will be held in the vestibule/waiting area on a first-come, first-served basis.

**Peak Hours and Staffing.** Based on industry data for comparable dispensaries, Greenleaf anticipates peak customer traffic during the hours of 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM and 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM on weekdays, with more sustained traffic on weekends. Staffing schedules will be adjusted to ensure adequate coverage during peak periods, with a minimum of four dispensary agents on the retail floor during high-traffic hours and a minimum of two during lower-traffic periods.

**[5.2 Inventory Management and Seed-to-Sale Tracking]{.underline}**

Greenleaf will implement a comprehensive inventory management system fully integrated with the Illinois COMPASS platform, as required by the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act and the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR). The integration is being designed and configured by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC under the direction of principal Marcus Wren.

**Seed-to-Sale Tracking.** All cannabis products received, stored, displayed, and sold by Greenleaf will be tracked in real time through the COMPASS-integrated tracking system. Each product unit will be assigned a unique tracking identifier upon receipt, which follows the product through storage, display, sale, and --- in the case of unsold or defective product --- return or destruction. This comprehensive tracking enables Greenleaf and State regulators to maintain a complete chain-of-custody record for every cannabis product that enters the dispensary.

**Inventory Reconciliation Procedures.** Greenleaf will implement a tiered inventory reconciliation protocol as follows:

> • **Daily:** Physical count of all high-value items (concentrates, high-potency products, and any products exceeding \$100 per unit retail value) and reconciliation against system records. Any discrepancies reported to the Inventory Manager and Compliance Officer immediately.
>
> • **Weekly:** Full physical inventory count of all cannabis products in both the retail floor and secure storage area. Reconciliation against COMPASS system records. Discrepancy reports generated and resolved within 24 hours.
>
> • **Monthly:** Comprehensive inventory reconciliation covering all products, accessories, and paraphernalia. Reconciliation report submitted to the State via the COMPASS platform as required. Internal reconciliation report retained in the compliance file.

**Receiving Protocols.** All cannabis product deliveries will be received exclusively in the secure storage area (800 sq. ft.). No deliveries will be accepted or processed on the retail floor or in any area accessible to customers. Upon delivery, the receiving agent will verify the delivery against the accompanying manifest, confirm product identity, quantity, and packaging integrity, and enter all received products into the COMPASS tracking system before any product is moved to the retail floor for display or sale.

**Product Sourcing.** Greenleaf will source all cannabis products exclusively from licensed Illinois cultivators and processors. The Company intends to establish relationships with multiple licensed suppliers to ensure product diversity, supply chain resilience, and competitive pricing. Greenleaf will maintain documentation of all supplier licenses, COAs (certificates of analysis), and manifests as required by State regulations.

**[5.3 Security Plan Overview]{.underline}**

Greenleaf\'s security plan has been designed by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC, a specialized cannabis security and compliance consulting firm, under the direction of principal Marcus Wren. The security plan is designed to meet or exceed all requirements of the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit ordinance and the State of Illinois cannabis regulations. This section provides an overview of the security plan\'s key components; the full security plan, including detailed specifications, camera placement maps, equipment models, and emergency response protocols, is prepared as a standalone submission document by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC and is submitted separately as part of the City application.

**24/7 Video Surveillance System.** Greenleaf will install and maintain a comprehensive video surveillance system operating 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year. The system will include high-definition cameras (minimum 1080p resolution) positioned to provide complete coverage of all facility areas, including:

> • All exterior entrances and exits, including the front entrance, any rear or side exits, and the delivery entrance
>
> • The vestibule/waiting area
>
> • The entire retail floor, including all point-of-sale stations and product display areas
>
> • The secure storage area, including all entry and exit points
>
> • The office and employee area
>
> • All hallways and corridors

All surveillance footage will be retained for a minimum of 30 days, as required by City and State regulations. Recordings will be stored on a secure on-site server with redundant backup to an encrypted cloud storage system. Footage will be made available to City and State regulators and law enforcement upon request.

**Panic Alarm System.** The facility will be equipped with a panic alarm system connected directly to the Evanston Police Department. Panic alarm activation points will be located at each point-of-sale station, at the ID verification station in the vestibule, in the Dispensary Manager\'s office, and in the secure storage area.

**Access Control.** The secure vestibule entry system provides controlled access to the retail floor, with admission granted only after successful ID verification. The secure storage area is accessible only by authorized personnel via key-card or biometric access control. All access events --- including the identity of the accessing individual, date, and time --- are logged electronically and retained for audit purposes. Employee access permissions are assigned based on role and are reviewed and updated on a quarterly basis by the Compliance Officer.

**ID Verification.** All customers are verified in the vestibule before admission to the retail floor using government-issued identification. The verification system includes technology capable of scanning and validating identification documents and detecting common types of fraudulent identification.

**Cash Handling.** Given the cash-intensive nature of cannabis retail operations, Greenleaf will implement rigorous cash handling protocols. Cash on the retail floor will be limited; excess cash will be secured in time-delay safes located in the secure storage area. Greenleaf will contract with a licensed armored car service for regular cash pickups and deposits to Ridgeline Community Bank. Cash handling procedures are designed to minimize the risk of theft and ensure the safety of employees and customers.

The detailed security plan submitted as a separate exhibit to the City application includes complete specifications for all equipment, camera placement diagrams overlaid on the architectural floor plan, access control system configurations, alarm monitoring protocols, and written standard operating procedures for security incidents.

**[5.4 Odor Mitigation and Environmental Controls]{.underline}**

Greenleaf recognizes the importance of odor control as both a regulatory requirement and a fundamental good-neighbor obligation. Cannabis retail facilities, while not engaged in cultivation or processing, may still generate noticeable odors from stored product inventory and from product display areas on the retail floor. Greenleaf is committed to implementing robust odor mitigation measures to prevent odor migration to adjacent commercial units, common areas, and the surrounding neighborhood.

**HVAC and Carbon Filtration System.** Greenleaf\'s primary odor mitigation strategy centers on a commercial HVAC system equipped with activated carbon filtration on all exhaust pathways. The HVAC system will be designed to serve the entire 3,200-square-foot facility, with particular attention to the secure storage area (800 sq. ft.) and the retail floor product display zones, where the highest concentration of cannabis products will be present.

The system will incorporate the following features:

> • Activated carbon filters installed on all exhaust vents throughout the facility, including the secure storage area, retail floor, and any areas where cannabis products are handled or stored
>
> • Negative air pressure maintained in the secure storage area relative to adjacent spaces, ensuring that air flows into the storage area rather than out, thereby containing odors within the controlled environment
>
> • Negative air pressure also maintained in retail product display zones where open or semi-open product samples may be available for customer inspection
>
> • Regular filter replacement on a scheduled basis, estimated at every 6 to 12 months depending on usage load and manufacturer recommendations

Greenleaf intends to install a carbon filtration system rated to handle at least 3,200 square feet of combined airspace, with carbon filter replacement every 6 to 12 months depending on usage load. The Company has identified several commercial carbon filtration vendors with experience in cannabis retail and storage applications. Detailed HVAC specifications and filtration system designs will be finalized during the build-out engineering phase in consultation with the selected HVAC contractor. Greenleaf will select the system that provides the highest level of odor control effectiveness while maintaining energy efficiency and operational reliability.

**Sealed Product Packaging.** As an additional odor control measure, all cannabis products on the retail floor will be maintained in sealed, child-resistant packaging as required by State law. Products will not be removed from sealed packaging except in designated consultation areas where odor containment measures are in place.

**Waste Disposal.** Cannabis waste generated by the dispensary --- including unsold, expired, damaged, or returned products --- will be rendered unusable and unrecognizable in accordance with Illinois Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) regulations and State cannabis waste disposal rules. Greenleaf will maintain a waste disposal log documenting all waste events, including the date, product description, quantity, method of rendering unusable, and final disposition. The Company will contract with a licensed waste disposal vendor for the transportation and destruction of rendered cannabis waste. All waste disposal activities will be recorded in the COMPASS tracking system.

Greenleaf is committed to maintaining its odor mitigation and environmental control systems at the highest standards throughout the life of its operations, and will respond promptly to any odor-related complaints from adjacent tenants, neighboring properties, or the City of Evanston.

**[5.5 Employee Requirements and Training]{.underline}**

All individuals employed by Greenleaf in any capacity involving access to cannabis products or the dispensary\'s restricted areas must hold a valid Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Card (\"Agent ID Card\") issued by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation prior to commencing work. Greenleaf will verify the valid Agent ID Card status of each employee upon hire and will maintain copies of all Agent ID Cards in the employee compliance file.

**Agent-in-Charge.** Dara Okonkwo, Managing Member, is designated as the dispensary\'s Agent-in-Charge, as identified in the State and City applications. As Agent-in-Charge, Okonkwo bears ultimate responsibility for all regulatory compliance, employee conduct, and dispensary operations.

**Pre-Opening Training Program.** All employees will be required to complete a minimum of 40 hours of comprehensive pre-opening training before the dispensary opens for business. The training curriculum will cover the following modules:

> • **Product Knowledge:** Overview of cannabis product types (flower, edibles, concentrates, tinctures, topicals, accessories), effects, dosing, and responsible consumption guidance
>
> • **Regulatory Compliance:** Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act requirements, City of Evanston ordinance provisions, operating hour restrictions, customer age verification, and prohibited activities
>
> • **Seed-to-Sale System Operation:** Hands-on training with the COMPASS-integrated tracking system for product receiving, inventory management, sales transactions, and waste documentation
>
> • **Customer Service:** Professional communication, customer consultation techniques, handling of difficult situations, and medical patient interaction protocols
>
> • **Security Protocols:** Vestibule and access control procedures, cash handling, emergency response, active threat scenarios, and panic alarm activation
>
> • **Health and Safety:** Workplace safety, product handling, sanitization, and emergency first aid

**Ongoing Training.** Following the initial pre-opening training, all employees will participate in quarterly refresher training sessions to reinforce core competencies and address any regulatory updates, new product introductions, or operational procedure changes. Quarterly training sessions will be documented and tracked in the employee compliance file.

Greenleaf will maintain complete employee records for each dispensary agent, including copies of Agent ID Cards, training completion certificates, signed acknowledgment of company policies and procedures, background check documentation, and performance evaluations. All employee records will be maintained at the dispensary and will be available for inspection by City and State regulators upon request.
