**[7. SOCIAL EQUITY AND DIVERSITY PLAN]{.underline}**

**[7.1 Social Equity Qualification]{.underline}**

Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC qualifies for social equity designation under the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act through its Managing Member, Dara Okonkwo, who meets the qualifying criteria on two independent bases:

> **a. R3 Area Residency.** Okonkwo is a current resident of Evanston, Illinois, within census tract 8092.02. This census tract has been designated by the State of Illinois as an R3 (Restorative, Reinvest, Renew) area --- a geographic area identified as having been disproportionately impacted by the enforcement of cannabis prohibition through elevated rates of arrest, conviction, and incarceration for cannabis offenses.
>
> **b. Prior Cannabis-Related Conviction.** Okonkwo has a prior cannabis-related criminal record consisting of a 2016 misdemeanor conviction for possession of cannabis in an amount less than 30 grams, adjudicated in Cook County, Illinois. This conviction was subsequently expunged in 2020 through Cook County\'s expungement process. Although the conviction has been expunged from the public record, it remains a qualifying basis for social equity status under the CRTA, which recognizes the lasting impact of cannabis-related criminal records on affected individuals\' lives and opportunities.

Okonkwo holds a 60% membership interest in Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC, constituting a majority ownership position by the social equity qualifying individual. This ownership structure satisfies the State and City requirements that a qualifying individual hold at least a 51% ownership interest in the applicant entity for social equity designation purposes.

Social equity applicants receive a 20-point bonus on the City of Evanston\'s 250-point scoring rubric, for a maximum possible score of 270 points. Greenleaf intends to claim this bonus and is committed to fulfilling all social equity conditions not merely as an application scoring advantage but as a continuing obligation integral to the Company\'s mission and values.

**[7.2 Workforce Hiring Commitment]{.underline}**

The City of Evanston social equity provisions require that at least 50% of Greenleaf\'s workforce be hired from R3-designated census tracts within the City of Evanston. This requirement is a binding condition of Greenleaf\'s social equity designation and is subject to annual audit by the City. Greenleaf embraces this requirement as both a regulatory obligation and a core element of its commitment to equitable community development.

**Staffing Model and Threshold Calculation.** Greenleaf\'s staffing model projects 12 full-time employees (FTEs) at full operational capacity. The 50% hiring requirement therefore means that a minimum of 6 employees must be residents of R3-designated census tracts at all times. The planned positions are as follows:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Position**                        **Number of FTEs**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Dispensary Manager                  1

  Inventory Manager                   1

  Compliance Officer                  1

  Dispensary Agents (Budtenders)      6

  Security / Reception Staff          2

  Administrative Assistant            1

  **Total**                           **12**
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

To meet the 50% threshold, at least 6 of these 12 positions must be filled by individuals who reside in R3-designated census tracts within Evanston at the time of hire and throughout their employment.

**Hiring Pipeline Strategy.** Greenleaf will implement a proactive recruitment strategy focused on R3-designated communities. The Company plans to partner with local workforce development organizations in Evanston to recruit qualified candidates from these areas. Planned recruitment activities include:

> • Hosting community job fairs in R3-designated neighborhoods prior to opening
>
> • Partnering with local workforce agencies and community organizations to publicize job openings and provide application assistance
>
> • Posting all open positions through community bulletin boards, local media, and neighborhood organizations in R3 areas
>
> • Offering competitive wages and benefits to attract and retain R3-area residents in a competitive retail labor market

While Greenleaf has identified these recruitment strategies and is actively developing relationships with potential community partners, specific partnerships with workforce development organizations have not yet been formalized. The Company anticipates formalizing these partnerships during the pre-opening phase following permit issuance.

Greenleaf recognizes that the 50% R3-area hiring requirement is a binding condition of its social equity status and will be subject to annual audit by the City. Greenleaf is developing a recruitment and retention strategy to ensure ongoing compliance. Given the nature of retail employment, maintaining the 50% threshold on a continuous basis will require proactive recruitment and retention efforts, particularly in the event of employee departures. The Company will monitor its workforce composition on a monthly basis through internal reporting, and will take prompt corrective action if the R3-area hiring ratio falls below the required threshold at any time.

Greenleaf is committed to investing in employee development, providing competitive compensation, and creating a positive work environment that encourages long-term retention among all employees, with particular focus on supporting employees from R3-designated communities.

**[7.3 Cannabis Business Mentorship Program]{.underline}**

As a social equity applicant, Greenleaf\'s Managing Member, Dara Okonkwo, will participate in the City of Evanston\'s Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum period of two (2) years. The Cannabis Business Mentorship Program is designed to pair new cannabis business operators with experienced mentors who can provide guidance on business operations, regulatory compliance, financial management, and community engagement. The program includes structured workshops, one-on-one mentorship sessions, and periodic check-ins to ensure that social equity licensees have the support and resources needed to establish and sustain successful cannabis businesses.

Okonkwo is committed to fulfilling the full two-year mentorship participation requirement. Participation in the mentorship program is a continuing condition of the social equity designation, and Greenleaf understands that compliance will be monitored. The annual permit renewal process includes re-verification of social equity status, including confirmation of ongoing participation in required programs such as the mentorship program. Okonkwo welcomes the opportunity to learn from experienced operators and to contribute her own professional expertise --- particularly her pharmaceutical background --- to the mentorship community.

**[7.4 Community Benefit Contributions]{.underline}**

Under the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit ordinance, permitted dispensaries are required to make an annual community benefit contribution equal to the greater of \$25,000 or 1% of gross annual revenue. Greenleaf views this contribution not merely as a regulatory requirement but as integral to its mission of reinvesting in the communities most impacted by cannabis prohibition.

Based on the Company\'s revenue projections, the estimated community benefit contributions for the first three years of operations are as follows:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Year**       **Projected Gross Revenue**   **Community Benefit Contribution (1% of Revenue)**   **Minimum Requirement**   **Amount Due**
  -------------- ----------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------- ------------------------- ----------------
  Year 1         \$3,200,000                   \$32,000                                             \$25,000                  \$32,000

  Year 2         \$4,100,000                   \$41,000                                             \$25,000                  \$41,000

  Year 3         \$4,800,000                   \$48,000                                             \$25,000                  \$48,000
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Greenleaf intends to direct its community benefit contributions toward programs and organizations that align with the restorative purposes of the CRTA and the City\'s social equity goals. Planned beneficiary categories include:

> • Youth development and education programs in R3-designated communities
>
> • Substance abuse prevention and treatment programs
>
> • R3-area community development initiatives, including job training, small business support, and neighborhood improvement projects
>
> • Local nonprofit organizations serving communities disproportionately impacted by cannabis prohibition

The Company will work with the City to identify specific beneficiary organizations and will provide annual documentation of all community benefit contributions as part of the permit renewal process. Greenleaf anticipates that its community benefit contributions will grow over time as the dispensary\'s revenue increases, creating a sustainable and expanding source of reinvestment in the Evanston community.

**[7.5 Diversity in Operations and Procurement]{.underline}**

Greenleaf is committed to diversity and inclusion not only in its ownership and workforce but throughout its operations and procurement practices. The Company will prioritize partnerships with minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, and Evanston-based businesses for build-out construction, ongoing operational services, and supply chain procurement wherever possible and consistent with quality, cost, and regulatory requirements.

In selecting its general contractor for the build-out, Greenleaf will include minority-owned and women-owned contracting firms in the competitive bid process and will give meaningful weight to diversity credentials in the evaluation process. Similarly, the Company will seek to engage local and diverse vendors for ongoing services such as cleaning, maintenance, marketing, and professional services.

Greenleaf\'s diversity procurement commitment supports the Diversity Plan scoring category, which carries 25 points on the City\'s 250-point scoring rubric. The Company believes that a genuine commitment to diverse procurement strengthens the local economy, supports underrepresented business owners, and reflects the values of the Evanston community.

**[8. COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT]{.underline}**

**[8.1 Community Sessions Held]{.underline}**

Greenleaf held two community engagement sessions as required by the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit ordinance, providing residents and community members with the opportunity to learn about the proposed dispensary, ask questions, and provide feedback.

**First Community Engagement Session**

> • **Date:** March 12, 2024
>
> • **Location:** Evanston Public Library, 1703 Orrington Ave., Evanston, IL 60201
>
> • **Public Notice:** Published in the *Evanston Roundtable* on February 25, 2024, providing 15 days of advance notice prior to the session
>
> • **Attendance:** 22 community members

**Second Community Engagement Session**

> • **Date:** April 9, 2024
>
> • **Location:** Evanston Public Library, 1703 Orrington Ave., Evanston, IL 60201
>
> • **Public Notice:** Published in the *Evanston Roundtable* on March 28, 2024
>
> • **Attendance:** 31 community members

Both sessions included formal presentations by Dara Okonkwo on Greenleaf\'s business model, proposed dispensary location, security plan, social equity commitments, and community benefit plans. Each presentation was followed by an open question-and-answer period during which community members were encouraged to raise concerns, ask questions, and provide suggestions. Okonkwo and representatives from the Company\'s advisory team were available to address technical and operational questions.

Full attendance sheets and summary minutes for both sessions have been prepared and are maintained in the Company\'s records. These documents, along with copies of the published public notices, will be submitted with the City application as part of the community engagement documentation package .

**[8.2 Community Feedback Summary]{.underline}**

The two community engagement sessions generated constructive and substantive feedback from Evanston residents. The most commonly raised themes and Greenleaf\'s responses are summarized below:

**Security and Public Safety.** Several community members raised concerns about the potential impact of a cannabis dispensary on neighborhood safety, including questions about loitering, traffic congestion, and crime. Greenleaf addressed these concerns by describing its comprehensive security plan, including 24/7 surveillance, controlled vestibule entry, panic alarm systems connected to the Evanston Police Department, and cash handling protocols designed to minimize risk. The Company emphasized that cannabis dispensaries are among the most heavily regulated and closely monitored retail establishments in the state.

**Local Hiring.** Multiple attendees expressed strong interest in Greenleaf\'s commitment to local hiring, particularly from R3-designated communities. Greenleaf described its 50% R3-area hiring commitment and its plans to conduct community job fairs and partner with local workforce agencies.

**Odor Control.** Several residents, particularly those familiar with the commercial corridor around 1420 Sherman Ave., asked about odor mitigation measures. Greenleaf described its planned commercial-grade carbon filtration system, negative air pressure protocols, and sealed product packaging requirements.

**Community Benefit Funding Priorities.** Attendees expressed interest in how the annual community benefit contributions would be allocated and asked for community input in the selection of beneficiary organizations. Greenleaf committed to working with the City and community stakeholders to identify funding priorities.

**Support for Social Equity Ownership.** Several attendees expressed support for the social equity ownership model and for Okonkwo\'s personal story of overcoming a cannabis-related conviction to become a business owner and community contributor.

Greenleaf has incorporated community feedback into its operational plans where feasible and will continue to engage with the Evanston community as a responsive and transparent neighbor.

**[9. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE FRAMEWORK]{.underline}**

**[9.1 State Licensing and Conversion Timeline]{.underline}**

Greenleaf holds State of Illinois Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License No. ADC-285-0147, issued by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation on September 22, 2023. The conditional license has a one-year term and expires on September 21, 2024. Pursuant to the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act and IDFPR regulations, the conditional license must be converted to a permanent Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License within 180 days of local permit issuance.

The conversion from conditional to permanent license requires the satisfaction of all of the following conditions:

> 1\. **Evidence of Local Zoning Approval and Dispensary Permit.** Greenleaf must provide the IDFPR with evidence that it has obtained all required local approvals, including the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit, zoning approvals, and any other required local authorizations.
>
> 2\. **Payment of Conversion Fee.** A conversion fee of \$100,000 is due to the IDFPR upon submission of the conversion application. Greenleaf has budgeted for this fee and has allocated funds accordingly (see Section 6.2).
>
> 3\. **Evidence of Operational Readiness.** The conversion application must include evidence that the dispensary is operationally ready to commence business, including completed build-out of the facility, final installation and testing of the security and surveillance system, integration of the seed-to-sale tracking system with COMPASS, and confirmation that all dispensary agents hold valid Agent ID Cards.

**Greenleaf\'s Projected Timeline.** Greenleaf anticipates the following timeline for the local permitting and state license conversion process:

> • **May 15, 2024:** Submit City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit application
>
> • **Mid-August 2024 (approximately 90 days after submission):** City completeness determination and staff review completion
>
> • **September -- October 2024 (within 45 days of completeness determination):** Public hearing before the Liquor and Cannabis Board
>
> • **Following public hearing:** Zoning Board of Appeals recommendation and City Council final vote
>
> • **Late Q3 or Early Q4 2024:** Anticipated local permit issuance

Upon receipt of the local permit, Greenleaf will immediately commence the build-out process (estimated 90 days) and simultaneously prepare the state license conversion application. The conversion application will be submitted to IDFPR as soon as all operational readiness requirements are met, and in no event later than 180 days after local permit issuance.

Greenleaf is confident that the local permitting timeline will allow sufficient time for state license conversion. The Company intends to work closely with both City and State regulators to ensure a smooth transition from conditional to permanent licensing status. Greenleaf\'s legal counsel at Hargrove & Sinclair LLP is actively monitoring both the City application review process and the State\'s conversion requirements to coordinate timelines and ensure that all deadlines are met.

**[9.2 Local Compliance Obligations]{.underline}**

Upon issuance of the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit, Greenleaf will be subject to a comprehensive set of ongoing local compliance obligations. The Company is committed to maintaining full compliance with all requirements at all times. A summary of key local compliance obligations follows:

**Annual Permit Renewal.** The dispensary permit must be renewed annually. The annual permit renewal fee is \$15,000. Greenleaf will submit its renewal application, along with all required documentation and the renewal fee, in advance of the permit expiration date each year.

**Quarterly Compliance Self-Audits.** Greenleaf is required to conduct and submit quarterly compliance self-audits to the City within 15 days of the end of each calendar quarter (Q1: January--March, due April 15; Q2: April--June, due July 15; Q3: July--September, due October 15; Q4: October--December, due January 15). Each self-audit will cover all aspects of the dispensary\'s operations, including security system status, surveillance system functionality, inventory reconciliation, employee compliance (including Agent ID Card verification), insurance and bonding status, financial reporting, and community benefit contribution tracking.

**Insurance and Bonding.** Greenleaf must maintain all required insurance coverages at the levels specified in the City ordinance (see Section 6.4) throughout the permit period. Updated certificates of insurance naming the City of Evanston as an additional insured must be provided to the City annually and upon any policy renewal, modification, or replacement. The \$50,000 performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit must remain in effect for the entire duration of the permit period.

**Operating Hours.** The dispensary must operate only during the permitted hours of 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily.

**Surveillance.** Greenleaf must maintain 24/7 video surveillance with a minimum of 30 days\' footage retention and minimum 1080p resolution throughout the facility, as detailed in Section 5.3.

**Unannounced Inspections.** Greenleaf must submit to unannounced inspections by City regulators at any time during operating hours. The Company will maintain an audit-ready compliance file at all times to facilitate prompt and cooperative responses to any inspection.

**Community Benefit Contribution.** The annual community benefit contribution --- the greater of \$25,000 or 1% of gross annual revenue --- must be paid within the timeframe specified by the City ordinance, with documentation submitted to the City.

**Social Equity Conditions.** Greenleaf must maintain all conditions of its social equity designation on an ongoing basis, including the 50% R3-area workforce hiring requirement and Managing Member Okonkwo\'s participation in the Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum of 2 years.

Greenleaf will designate an internal Compliance Officer to manage all regulatory reporting, maintain the compliance file, coordinate audit preparation, and serve as the primary point of contact for City and State regulators. The Compliance Officer will report directly to the Managing Member and will have the authority and resources necessary to ensure full regulatory compliance at all times.

**[9.3 Banking Compliance]{.underline}**

Greenleaf\'s banking relationship with Ridgeline Community Bank is subject to the bank\'s cannabis banking program requirements, which reflect the heightened regulatory scrutiny applicable to financial institutions that serve cannabis businesses. Key banking compliance requirements include:

> • **Quarterly Compliance Attestations:** Greenleaf must submit quarterly attestations to Ridgeline confirming that the Company holds all required licenses and permits, is in compliance with applicable State and local regulations, and maintains adequate internal financial controls.
>
> • **Anti-Money-Laundering (AML) Compliance:** Greenleaf must adhere to all applicable anti-money-laundering protocols, including the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) reporting requirements. The Company will maintain detailed financial records and transaction logs sufficient to satisfy both regulatory and banking requirements.
>
> • **Transparent Financial Reporting:** Greenleaf will provide Ridgeline with such financial reports and documentation as the bank may reasonably request to fulfill its own regulatory obligations.
>
> • **License and Permit Maintenance:** Greenleaf must maintain all required cannabis licenses and permits in good standing as a condition of the banking relationship. Any lapse, suspension, or revocation of a license or permit must be reported to the bank immediately.

Greenleaf is committed to maintaining the highest standards of financial transparency and regulatory compliance in all aspects of its banking relationships.

**[10. RISK FACTORS AND MITIGATION STRATEGIES]{.underline}**

**[10.1 Regulatory Risk]{.underline}**

Cannabis remains classified as a Schedule I controlled substance under the federal Controlled Substances Act. While the State of Illinois has legalized adult-use and medical cannabis under state law, and the City of Evanston has established a local cannabis permitting framework, state licensing does not provide immunity from federal enforcement. The federal regulatory posture toward state-legal cannabis businesses has been generally non-interventionist in recent years, but federal policy could change at any time.

Additionally, the local, state, and federal regulatory landscape governing cannabis is evolving rapidly. Changes in laws, regulations, or enforcement priorities at any level of government could have a material adverse effect on Greenleaf\'s operations, licensing status, or financial performance.

**Mitigation:** Greenleaf will maintain strict compliance with all applicable state and local requirements at all times. The Company has retained experienced cannabis counsel at Hargrove & Sinclair LLP and will actively monitor regulatory developments at all levels of government. Greenleaf will adapt its operations as necessary to remain in compliance with evolving regulatory requirements.

**[10.2 Operational Risk]{.underline}**

Key operational risks facing Greenleaf include:

**Employee Turnover.** Retail employee turnover is a well-documented challenge across the cannabis industry and the broader retail sector. For Greenleaf, employee turnover presents a heightened risk because of the Company\'s binding commitment to maintain at least 50% of its workforce from R3-designated census tracts. Each employee departure requires a replacement hire that may need to come from the R3-area applicant pool to maintain compliance. Maintaining the 50% R3-area hiring threshold will require dedicated recruitment and retention programs, and employee attrition in a competitive retail labor market poses a compliance risk that Greenleaf must actively manage.

**Supply Chain Disruptions.** Cannabis supply chain dynamics in Illinois are subject to variability, including cultivator production fluctuations, regulatory changes affecting product availability, and transportation logistics. Disruptions could affect product availability and revenue.

**Security Incidents.** Although Greenleaf\'s comprehensive security plan is designed to deter and respond to security incidents, no security system eliminates all risk. Theft, robbery, or vandalism could result in financial loss, employee safety concerns, and regulatory complications.

**Insurance Market Volatility.** The cannabis insurance market remains relatively immature, and premiums, coverage availability, and terms can change significantly from year to year.

**Mitigation:** Greenleaf will offer competitive wages and employee development opportunities to minimize turnover. The Company will establish relationships with multiple licensed suppliers to ensure supply chain resilience. The comprehensive security plan designed by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC provides a robust framework for incident prevention and response. Greenleaf will work with experienced insurance brokers to maintain adequate coverage and will monitor market conditions proactively.

**[10.3 Financial Risk]{.underline}**

Greenleaf\'s revenue projections are based on market assumptions and comparable data that, while reasonable, could prove optimistic or conservative depending on actual market conditions, competitive dynamics, and consumer demand. Build-out costs could exceed estimates due to construction delays, material cost increases, or unforeseen conditions at the premises. The community benefit contribution is revenue-linked, which provides a natural alignment between expense and revenue performance --- in lower-revenue scenarios, the contribution obligation decreases correspondingly (subject to the \$25,000 minimum).

The Company\'s total initial capitalization of \$1,850,000 provides a substantial financial cushion beyond estimated startup costs. The projected working capital reserve of approximately \$758,000 provides significant buffer against revenue shortfalls, cost overruns, or unexpected expenses during the critical startup phase. Greenleaf does not carry any debt financing, eliminating fixed debt service obligations that could create financial pressure during lower-revenue periods.

**[10.4 Timeline Risk]{.underline}**

The local permitting process and state license conversion are subject to regulatory timelines outside Greenleaf\'s control. Application review periods, public hearing scheduling, board and council deliberation, and state processing timelines may vary from published targets and could be affected by factors including application volume, staffing constraints at regulatory agencies, public opposition, or requests for additional information.

Greenleaf will proactively engage with regulators to manage processing timelines and avoid potential gaps. The Company\'s legal counsel at Hargrove & Sinclair LLP is actively monitoring the permitting process and is prepared to address any procedural questions or information requests promptly to minimize delays.

**[APPENDICES]{.underline}**

**[Appendix A: Organizational Chart]{.underline}**

A one-page organizational chart depicting Greenleaf\'s management structure and reporting relationships is attached. The chart reflects the organizational structure described in Section 2.2 of this business plan.

**[Appendix B: Resumes of Principal Officers]{.underline}**

Resumes for the following principal officers are attached:

> • **Dara Okonkwo** --- Managing Member, Agent-in-Charge. Licensed Pharmacist (IL #051-302887), Evanston resident, social equity qualifying individual.
>
> • **Felix Ramachandran** --- Silent Member. Vice President of Operations, Lakefront Distribution Co., Wilmette resident.

**[Appendix C: Lease Summary]{.underline}**

A summary of the commercial lease agreement between Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC and Kimball Properties Inc. for the premises at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201, is attached. The complete lease agreement is submitted as a separate application document .

**[Appendix D: Floor Plan and Site Plan]{.underline}**

The architectural floor plan depicting the 3,200-square-foot facility layout (retail floor: 1,600 sq. ft.; secure storage: 800 sq. ft.; office and employee area: 600 sq. ft.; vestibule/waiting area: 200 sq. ft.) and the site plan showing the dispensary\'s proximity to sensitive uses are attached.

**[Appendix E: Pro Forma Financial Statements]{.underline}**

Detailed Year 1 through Year 5 pro forma income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow projections are attached. These detailed projections support the summary financial information presented in Section 6.3 of this business plan.

**[Appendix F: Letters of Intent / Vendor Agreements]{.underline}**

Preliminary agreements and letters of intent with the following vendors and service providers are attached:

> • Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC (security and compliance consulting)
>
> • Canopy Risk Underwriters (cannabis insurance)
>
> • Thornfield Surety Group (performance bond)

**END OF BUSINESS PLAN**

This document is the confidential and proprietary property of Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC. It has been prepared for submission to the City of Evanston in support of the Company\'s Cannabis Dispensary Permit application and for internal use only. Unauthorized reproduction, distribution, or disclosure of this document or any of its contents is strictly prohibited. All information contained herein is believed to be accurate as of April 2024 but is subject to change. Forward-looking statements, including financial projections and timeline estimates, are based on assumptions that may not prove to be accurate. Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC makes no guarantees regarding the achievement of projected results.

**2.2 Session 1 Presentation Summary**

Dara Okonkwo opened the session by introducing herself as the Managing Member of Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC, an Evanston resident, and a licensed Illinois pharmacist (IL License No. 051-302887). She described the proposed dispensary location at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, situated within the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning district, and provided an overview of the company and its plans for serving the Evanston community. The presentation covered the following topics:

**Company Overview.** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC was formed on March 14, 2023, under the laws of the State of Illinois and is the holder of State conditional adult-use dispensary license ADC-285-0147 issued by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation.

**Proposed Operating Hours.** The dispensary will operate from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily, consistent with the operating hour restrictions established by both City of Evanston ordinance and the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act.

**Security Measures.** Greenleaf\'s security plan was designed by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC, with Marcus Wren serving as Principal. The plan includes 24/7 video surveillance with cameras positioned at all entrances, exits, point-of-sale stations, and the exterior perimeter; a secure vestibule entry system with government-issued photo identification verification required before any customer may access the retail floor; and a panic alarm system connected directly to the Evanston Police Department.

**Community Benefit Commitment.** Greenleaf commits to contributing a minimum of \$25,000 annually or one percent (1%) of gross annual revenue, whichever is greater, toward community benefit programs. Based on projected gross revenue of \$3,200,000 in Year 1, the projected community benefit contribution for the first year of operation is \$32,000.

**Social Equity Status.** Dara Okonkwo qualifies as a social equity applicant under the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act, Section 10-50, as a resident of R3-designated census tract 8092.02 within Evanston.

**Local Hiring Commitment.** Greenleaf is committed to hiring at least fifty percent (50%) of its workforce---a minimum of six (6) of twelve (12) projected full-time employees---from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston.

**Odor Mitigation.** The dispensary will employ a professional-grade carbon filtration system integrated with HVAC controls to mitigate cannabis odor. A detailed odor mitigation plan with HVAC specifications will be included in the application submission.

**Proximity to Sensitive Uses.** Okonkwo presented the distances between the proposed dispensary location and the nearest sensitive uses: Chute Middle School is located approximately 1,180 feet from the proposed location, which exceeds the 1,000-foot minimum buffer requirement; Tiny Acorns Learning Center (daycare) is located approximately 890 feet away, exceeding the 500-foot minimum buffer requirement; and Prairie Wellness Dispensary, the nearest existing dispensary at 807 Church Street, is located approximately 2,100 feet away, exceeding the 1,500-foot minimum buffer requirement between dispensary locations.

**2.3 Session 1 Community Questions and Feedback**

Following the presentation, Okonkwo and Wren opened the floor for questions from attendees. The following is a paraphrased summary of the questions raised and the responses provided during the question-and-answer period. These summaries are based on contemporaneous notes taken by Greenleaf representatives and are not verbatim transcripts.

**Traffic and Parking Concerns.** Several attendees asked about the potential impact of the dispensary on parking availability and traffic congestion along Sherman Avenue, which is a busy commercial corridor. Okonkwo explained that the commercial lease executed with Kimball Properties Inc. includes access to a shared rear parking lot with twelve (12) dedicated parking spaces allocated to the dispensary\'s use. She further noted that the dispensary\'s maximum retail occupancy of forty (40) customers at any one time is expected to produce a moderate level of traffic comparable to the existing retail businesses operating within the Sherman Avenue corridor. Greenleaf does not anticipate a material increase in traffic congestion resulting from the dispensary\'s operations.

**Security and Crime.** Three attendees expressed concern about the potential for increased criminal activity in the vicinity of a cannabis dispensary. Marcus Wren of Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC provided a detailed explanation of the comprehensive security plan designed for the facility. Key elements include 1080p minimum resolution security cameras with thirty (30) day video retention maintained on-site, a secure vestibule entry system requiring government-issued photo identification verification before any customer may enter the retail floor, and a direct panic alarm connection to the Evanston Police Department. Wren emphasized that licensed cannabis dispensaries are among the most heavily regulated and surveilled retail establishments in the State of Illinois and that data from jurisdictions with established dispensary operations does not support a correlation between licensed dispensary locations and increases in crime.

**Odor.** Two attendees asked about the potential for cannabis odor emanating from the dispensary and its impact on neighboring businesses and residences. Okonkwo stated that the dispensary will implement a professional-grade carbon filtration system designed to capture and neutralize cannabis odor before air is exhausted from the building. She noted that a detailed odor mitigation plan with specific HVAC equipment specifications will be prepared by a licensed mechanical engineer and included as part of the application submission to the City.

**Community Benefits Allocation.** One attendee asked how the community benefit funds would be specifically allocated and what mechanisms would be in place to ensure accountability. Okonkwo stated that Greenleaf is open to community input in determining the allocation of community benefit funds and is currently considering partnerships with local workforce development organizations and youth programs operating in R3-designated areas within Evanston. She invited attendees to share their suggestions and stated that Greenleaf intends to establish a community advisory process to guide benefit fund distribution.

**Hours of Operation.** One attendee asked whether the dispensary would be open late at night, expressing concern about noise and foot traffic during evening hours. Okonkwo confirmed that operating hours are limited to 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily, as mandated by both City of Evanston ordinance and the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act, and that Greenleaf has no intention of seeking any variance or extension beyond these established hours.

**General Tone.** The overall tone of Session 1 was mostly supportive, with attendees expressing interest in the social equity mission and local economic benefits of the proposed dispensary. A small number of attendees expressed cautious skepticism regarding the introduction of a cannabis business to the corridor, though no organized opposition was present and no attendees made formal objections to the proposal.

**Community Benefit Allocation Plan.** In response to feedback received at Session 1, Greenleaf has developed a more detailed community benefit allocation framework. Greenleaf proposes directing at least fifty percent (50%) of the annual community benefit contribution toward workforce development programs serving R3-designated areas within Evanston. Okonkwo specifically mentioned that Greenleaf is exploring partnerships with Evanston-based job training organizations to create a pipeline for residents in R3-designated census tracts seeking careers in the cannabis industry and related fields.

**Social Equity Hiring Pipeline.** Greenleaf intends to partner with local community organizations to actively recruit and train employees from R3-designated census tracts, targeting at least six (6) of twelve (12) full-time employees from these areas. Okonkwo described a proposed pre-employment training program that would provide prospective employees with an understanding of cannabis regulations, customer service standards, and compliance obligations prior to beginning work at the dispensary.

**Updated Security Plan Details.** Marcus Wren of Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC presented updated security plan details, including a description of the seed-to-sale tracking system integration with the Illinois Cannabis Tracking and Compliance System (COMPASS), which ensures full chain-of-custody documentation for all cannabis products from receipt through point of sale.

**Waste Disposal Plan.** Cannabis waste generated at the dispensary will be handled in full compliance with the Illinois Environmental Protection Agency (IEPA) regulations and State cannabis regulatory requirements, including documented destruction and disposal protocols.

**Cannabis Business Mentorship Program.** Greenleaf has committed to participating in the City of Evanston\'s Cannabis Business Mentorship Program for a minimum of two (2) years as part of its social equity obligations and community engagement commitments.

**Lease Information.** Okonkwo noted that the proposed location\'s landlord, Kimball Properties Inc., has executed a seven (7) year commercial lease (signed January 8, 2024) that includes cannabis-specific permitted use provisions, ensuring long-term location stability for the dispensary.

**3.3 Session 2 Community Questions and Feedback**

The question-and-answer period for Session 2 was significantly more active than Session 1, reflecting broader community awareness of the proposed dispensary and the application process. The following is a paraphrased summary of the principal questions raised and responses provided.

**Property Values.** Several attendees asked whether the presence of a cannabis dispensary would negatively affect nearby residential property values. Okonkwo cited studies conducted in other municipalities with established dispensary operations, including research from Denver, Colorado and Portland, Oregon, which suggest no measurable negative impact on residential property values in the vicinity of licensed dispensaries. She further noted that the proposed location at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, is situated within the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning district, not within a residential zone, and that the commercial character of the corridor is consistent with dispensary operations.

**Youth Access.** Four attendees raised concerns about the potential for minors to access cannabis through the dispensary. Marcus Wren provided a detailed description of the secure vestibule entry system, which requires valid government-issued photo identification verification before any customer may access the retail sales floor. He explained that all dispensing organization agents employed at the facility will hold Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Cards and are trained to verify identification and refuse entry or service to any individual who cannot present valid identification demonstrating they are twenty-one (21) years of age or older. Wren further noted the buffer distances from the nearest school (Chute Middle School, approximately 1,180 feet) and nearest daycare (Tiny Acorns Learning Center, approximately 890 feet), both of which exceed the City\'s minimum buffer requirements.

**Delivery Services.** Two attendees inquired whether Greenleaf plans to offer home delivery of cannabis products. Okonkwo stated that Greenleaf does not currently plan to operate delivery services and that any future implementation of delivery operations would require additional approvals from both the City of Evanston and the State of Illinois. She emphasized that Greenleaf\'s current application pertains exclusively to on-site retail dispensary operations.

**Employment Opportunities.** Five attendees expressed interest in potential employment at the dispensary and asked about the hiring timeline and process. Okonkwo described the plan to hire twelve (12) full-time employees, with at least fifty percent (50%) from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston, and encouraged interested Evanston residents to provide their contact information on the sign-up sheet available at the registration table for future recruitment outreach. She noted that Greenleaf anticipates beginning the formal hiring process approximately sixty (60) days before the projected opening date, pending receipt of all necessary permits and approvals.

**Relationship with Existing Dispensary.** One attendee asked about the relationship, if any, between Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC and Prairie Wellness Dispensary, the existing adult-use cannabis dispensary located at 807 Church Street in Evanston. Okonkwo stated clearly that Greenleaf has no business relationship, ownership overlap, or affiliation of any kind with Prairie Wellness Dispensary. She noted that the two locations are approximately 2,100 feet apart, well in excess of the 1,500-foot minimum buffer requirement between dispensary locations established by City ordinance.

**Banking and Financial Transparency.** One attendee asked how Greenleaf would handle banking given the ongoing federal restrictions on cannabis businesses\' access to financial services. Okonkwo stated that Greenleaf has established a banking relationship with Ridgeline Community Bank, which operates a specialized cannabis banking program in compliance with applicable FinCEN guidance. She stated that Greenleaf is committed to full financial transparency, including the preparation and submission of quarterly compliance self-audits to the City of Evanston, maintaining comprehensive financial records, and operating on a cashless or minimal-cash basis to the extent feasible.

**General Tone.** The overall tone of Session 2 was notably more engaged than Session 1, reflecting broader community awareness of both the proposed dispensary and the City\'s cannabis licensing process. Several attendees expressed explicit support for Greenleaf\'s social equity mission and its commitment to local hiring and community benefit contributions. Two attendees voiced continued opposition based on general discomfort with the presence of cannabis businesses in the community; however, neither attendee raised specific regulatory objections or cited grounds that would bear on the City\'s review of the permit application.

**[Section 4: Summary of Community Feedback Themes]{.underline}**

Across both community engagement sessions, the following recurring themes emerged from attendee questions and feedback. Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC has carefully considered this input and intends to incorporate it into the final application materials, particularly in the Business Plan, Community Benefits section, and Diversity and Inclusion Plan.

1\. **Security and Public Safety.** This was the most frequently raised concern across both sessions. Attendees sought detailed information about security infrastructure, crime prevention, and coordination with local law enforcement. The comprehensive security plan presented by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC, including 24/7 video surveillance, secure vestibule ID verification, and direct panic alarm connection to the Evanston Police Department, was responsive to these concerns.

2\. **Youth Access Prevention.** The second most common concern centered on preventing minors from accessing cannabis products. This concern was addressed through the presentation of buffer distances from schools and daycare facilities, the secure vestibule entry system requiring government-issued photo identification, and the requirement that all dispensary agents hold Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Cards.

3\. **Community Economic Benefit.** Attendees expressed strong interest in understanding how the dispensary would benefit the local community, particularly residents of R3-designated areas. The projected Year 1 community benefit contribution of \$32,000 and the commitment to directing at least fifty percent of annual contributions toward workforce development in R3-designated areas were positively received.

4\. **Traffic and Parking.** Moderate concern was expressed regarding the impact on parking availability and traffic congestion along the Sherman Avenue corridor. This was addressed by the twelve (12) dedicated parking spaces included in the lease with Kimball Properties Inc. and the forty (40) person maximum retail occupancy.

5\. **Odor.** Moderate concern about cannabis odor was addressed by Greenleaf\'s commitment to installing a professional-grade carbon filtration system integrated with HVAC controls. A detailed odor mitigation plan will be included in the application submission.

6\. **Employment Opportunities.** Significant enthusiasm was expressed by residents of R3-designated areas regarding potential employment at the dispensary. Multiple attendees at both sessions expressed interest in working at Greenleaf, and contact information was collected for future recruitment outreach.

7\. **Property Values.** Raised primarily by attendees who identified as nearby property owners, this concern was addressed by reference to available data from comparable jurisdictions indicating no measurable negative impact on residential property values from licensed dispensary operations.

Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC is grateful for the community\'s active participation in these engagement sessions and remains committed to maintaining an open and ongoing dialogue with Evanston residents throughout the permitting process and, if approved, throughout the life of the dispensary\'s operations.

**MEMORANDUM**

**TO:** Nadia Petrova, Partner, and Jordan Mackey, Associate Hargrove & Sinclair LLP 77 W. Wacker Drive, Suite 3200 Chicago, IL 60601

**FROM:** Dara Okonkwo, Managing Member, Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC

**DATE:** April 22, 2024

**RE:** Insurance and Bonding Status Summary --- Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Proposed Dispensary Location: 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201 State Conditional License No. ADC-285-0147 Target Application Submission Date: May 15, 2024

**CC:** Felix Ramachandran, Silent Member, Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC Marcus Wren, Principal, Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC

**[1. Purpose and Background]{.underline}**

This memorandum summarizes the current status of all insurance policies and bonding or surety arrangements maintained or being pursued by Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC (EIN: 88-3921047), an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2023, in connection with its application for a City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit. The City of Evanston application requires evidence of insurance meeting specified minimum coverage thresholds as a condition of permit issuance. In addition, Section 8 of the application form contains a scoring rubric that awards Financial Viability points based, in part, on evidence of secured bonding capacity.

Greenleaf\'s total capitalization stands at \$1,850,000, consisting of \$1,110,000 contributed by Dara Okonkwo and \$740,000 contributed by Felix Ramachandran. The estimated build-out cost for the dispensary premises at 1420 Sherman Avenue, Unit B, Evanston, Illinois 60201 is approximately \$425,000. Projected Year 1 gross revenue is \$3,200,000. Greenleaf\'s banking partner is Ridgeline Community Bank, located at 820 Davis Street, Evanston, IL 60201, which maintains a cannabis banking program requiring quarterly compliance attestations from participating clients.

This memorandum is being provided to outside counsel at Hargrove & Sinclair LLP and to Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC for the purpose of identifying any gaps, deficiencies, or action items in Greenleaf\'s insurance and bonding posture prior to the May 15, 2024 application submission deadline and in advance of anticipated permit issuance. We respectfully request counsel\'s guidance on the open items described herein so that Greenleaf can take timely corrective action.

**[2. City of Evanston Insurance Requirements Summary]{.underline}**

Based on our review of the City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit application materials, the following minimum insurance coverages are required of all applicants:

> • **General Liability:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> • **Product Liability:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> • **Workers\' Compensation:** Statutory limits as required under the Illinois Workers\' Compensation Act (820 ILCS 305)
>
> • **Cannabis-Specific Crime/Theft Coverage:** \$500,000 minimum

The application materials further require that certificates of insurance naming the City of Evanston as an additional insured be submitted prior to permit issuance. All policies must remain in continuous force throughout the permit period. Any lapse of required coverage constitutes grounds for immediate permit suspension or revocation by the City. Greenleaf understands these requirements and has structured its insurance procurement efforts accordingly, as detailed in the sections that follow.

**[3. Current Insurance Coverage --- Policies Obtained]{.underline}**

The following insurance policies have been obtained and are currently in force or in a compliant posture for purposes of the City of Evanston application.

**3.1 General Liability**

Greenleaf has obtained a commercial general liability policy placed through our commercial insurance broker. The policy provides the following coverage:

> **Limits:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> **Effective Date:** January 15, 2024
>
> **Named Insured:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC
>
> **Covered Location:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201
>
> **Status:** **Compliant** --- meets or exceeds City of Evanston minimums

The general liability policy includes a cannabis dispensary operations endorsement, which expressly extends coverage to the retail sale and handling of cannabis products at the insured location. A certificate of insurance is available for submission and can be endorsed to name the City of Evanston as an additional insured upon request.

**3.2 Product Liability**

Greenleaf\'s product liability coverage is bundled with the general liability policy described in Section 3.1 above.

> **Limits:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> **Effective Date:** January 15, 2024
>
> **Status:** **Compliant** --- meets or exceeds City of Evanston minimums

The product liability coverage extends to claims arising from the sale or distribution of cannabis products at the insured dispensary location. A certificate of insurance reflecting this coverage is available for submission with the application.

**3.3 Workers\' Compensation**

> **Coverage:** Statutory limits under the Illinois Workers\' Compensation Act (820 ILCS 305)
>
> **Effective Date:** Policy to be bound upon first employee hire
>
> **Status:** **Compliant in posture** --- Workers\' compensation policy has been quoted and will be bound prior to any employee onboarding; a certificate will be available for submission prior to permit issuance

Greenleaf does not currently have any employees on payroll. The company projects a workforce of approximately twelve (12) full-time employees once the dispensary becomes operational. The projected twelve-employee workforce has been underwritten by our commercial broker, and final premium figures have been quoted based on applicable classification codes for cannabis retail operations in Illinois. The policy will be bound immediately upon the first employee hire, and Greenleaf will ensure that a certificate of insurance is available for the City prior to permit issuance.

**[4. Outstanding Insurance Coverage --- Cannabis Crime/Theft Policy]{.underline}**

The City of Evanston requires a minimum of \$500,000 in cannabis-specific crime and theft coverage as a condition of permit issuance. This coverage is intended to protect against losses arising from employee dishonesty, robbery, burglary, and theft of cannabis inventory and cash on the dispensary premises.

Greenleaf has been in active negotiations with Canopy Risk Underwriters, a cannabis specialty insurance carrier, regarding a cannabis-specific crime and theft policy. Standard commercial crime policies obtained through traditional insurance markets typically exclude cannabis-related businesses from coverage due to the continued federal classification of cannabis as a Schedule I controlled substance under the Controlled Substances Act (21 U.S.C. § 812). As a result, standard commercial crime carriers will not write coverage for cannabis dispensary operations. Greenleaf\'s existing commercial broker confirmed that its standard markets declined to quote this line of coverage when approached in late 2023 and early 2024.

Canopy Risk Underwriters is one of a limited number of specialty carriers in the United States that currently write cannabis crime and theft policies for state-licensed dispensary operators. Greenleaf submitted a detailed application to Canopy Risk Underwriters on March 18, 2024.

**Current Status:** Under negotiation --- not yet bound. Canopy Risk Underwriters has reviewed Greenleaf\'s initial application and has requested the following additional documentation before it can complete its underwriting review and issue a binder:

> \(a\) A copy of the comprehensive security plan being prepared by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC (Marcus Wren, Principal);
>
> \(b\) Floor plans showing the secure storage area (approximately 800 square feet) and vault specifications; and
>
> \(c\) A copy of the Illinois state conditional license (No. ADC-285-0147).

Greenleaf has provided items (b) and (c) to Canopy Risk Underwriters. However, item (a) --- the final security plan --- is still being finalized by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC and has not yet been delivered to Canopy Risk Underwriters.

**Estimated Premium:** Canopy Risk Underwriters has provided a preliminary indication of approximately \$18,000 to \$24,000 annually for \$500,000 in cannabis crime and theft coverage, subject to final underwriting review and approval.

**Flag for Counsel:** This policy must be obtained prior to permit issuance. Given the limited market for cannabis crime and theft insurance and the dependency on the security plan completion by Pinnacle Compliance Advisors, there is a meaningful risk of delay. We recommend prioritizing the completion of the Pinnacle security plan to unblock the Canopy Risk Underwriters underwriting process. If Canopy Risk Underwriters ultimately declines to write the policy or imposes unacceptable terms, the lead time required to identify and bind coverage with an alternative specialty carrier could be significant and may jeopardize our permit timeline. We request counsel\'s guidance on any mitigation strategies available to Greenleaf in that contingency.

**[5. Performance Bond / Irrevocable Letter of Credit]{.underline}**

The City of Evanston requires a \$50,000 performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit to be submitted \"prior to permit issuance\" per the application checklist. This instrument serves as security for the permittee\'s compliance with permit conditions, including premises maintenance, remediation obligations, and operational standards.

Greenleaf has made a preliminary inquiry with Thornfield Surety Group regarding a \$50,000 performance bond. Thornfield Surety Group has reviewed Greenleaf\'s financial statements, including evidence of capitalization totaling \$1,850,000 in member contributions, and has indicated verbally that Greenleaf would likely qualify for the bond based on the company\'s financial position. However, no formal commitment letter or bonding capacity letter has been obtained from Thornfield Surety Group to date.

We understand from the application checklist that the performance bond is not required until permit issuance, so we have not prioritized obtaining a formal commitment at this stage. Our focus has instead been directed toward securing the insurance coverages that must be evidenced in the application package itself.

Additionally, Ridgeline Community Bank has indicated a general willingness to discuss the issuance of an irrevocable letter of credit as an alternative to a surety bond, should Greenleaf prefer that structure. However, no formal discussions regarding the terms, conditions, or fees associated with a letter of credit have been initiated with Ridgeline Community Bank at this time.

**Flag for Counsel:** We request counsel\'s guidance on whether Greenleaf should obtain a formal bonding commitment letter from Thornfield Surety Group, or alternatively a letter of credit commitment from Ridgeline Community Bank, prior to the May 15, 2024 application submission. Specifically, we would appreciate counsel\'s assessment of whether the current preliminary inquiry status is sufficient for the application package, or whether additional documentation of bonding capacity would strengthen the submission.

**[6. Summary Status Table]{.underline}**

The following table summarizes the status of each insurance and bonding requirement applicable to Greenleaf\'s City of Evanston dispensary permit application:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Coverage Type**        **City Requirement**    **Current Status**              **Carrier / Provider**                               **Action Needed**
  ------------------------ ----------------------- ------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  General Liability        \$2M occ. / \$4M agg.   **Obtained**                    Commercial broker                                    None --- certificate available

  Product Liability        \$2M occ. / \$4M agg.   **Obtained**                    Bundled with GL policy                               None --- certificate available

  Workers\' Compensation   Statutory limits (IL)   **Quoted; binding upon hire**   Commercial broker                                    Bind prior to first employee hire

  Cannabis Crime/Theft     \$500,000 minimum       **Under negotiation**           Canopy Risk Underwriters                             Complete security plan; finalize underwriting

  Performance Bond / LOC   \$50,000                **Preliminary inquiry only**    Thornfield Surety Group / Ridgeline Community Bank   Obtain commitment letter (see counsel guidance request)
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[7. Recommended Next Steps and Open Questions for Counsel]{.underline}**

Based on the foregoing, Greenleaf respectfully submits the following action items and open questions for counsel\'s review and guidance:

> 1\. **Prioritize completion of the security plan.** Greenleaf requests that Marcus Wren of Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC prioritize the finalization of the comprehensive security plan so that it may be promptly delivered to Canopy Risk Underwriters, enabling completion of the cannabis crime/theft policy underwriting process.
>
> 2\. **Bonding commitment letter.** Greenleaf requests counsel\'s advice on whether a formal bonding commitment letter from Thornfield Surety Group, or an irrevocable letter of credit commitment from Ridgeline Community Bank, should be obtained and submitted with the application by May 15, 2024, or whether the current preliminary inquiry status is acceptable for purposes of the application package.
>
> 3\. **Inclusion of certificates in application.** Greenleaf requests counsel\'s confirmation as to whether certificates of insurance for the obtained policies (general liability and product liability) should be included in the application package at the time of submission, or whether these are to be reserved for the pre-issuance stage only.
>
> 4\. **Contingency carrier identification.** In the event that Canopy Risk Underwriters declines to write the cannabis crime/theft policy or cannot complete underwriting in time, Greenleaf requests that counsel identify any alternative specialty carriers that may be able to provide this coverage on an expedited basis.
>
> 5\. **Additional insured endorsements.** Greenleaf will confirm that all insurance policies will be endorsed to name the City of Evanston as an additional insured, as required by the application materials, and requests counsel\'s guidance on the timing and format for those endorsements.

Greenleaf\'s operations team is available to provide any additional financial documentation, underwriting materials, or information that may be needed by carriers, counsel, or Pinnacle Compliance Advisors in connection with the foregoing items. We are committed to resolving all outstanding insurance and bonding matters in advance of the May 15, 2024 submission deadline and appreciate counsel\'s prompt attention to the questions raised in this memorandum.

Respectfully submitted,

GREENLEAF THERAPEUTICS LLC

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Dara Okonkwo Managing Member

1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B Evanston, IL 60201 dokonkwo@greenleaftherapeutics.com (847) 555-0193

**[SUMMARY OF KEY LEASE TERMS]{.underline}**

**[Commercial Lease Agreement]{.underline}**

**Prepared for Inclusion in the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application**
