**Date of Summary:** April 2024

This summary extracts key terms from the executed Commercial Lease Agreement dated January 8, 2024 (the \"Lease\" or \"Lease Agreement\"), between Kimball Properties Inc. (\"Landlord\") and Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC (\"Tenant\"). This summary is provided for informational and regulatory application purposes only and does not supersede, amend, or modify the full Lease Agreement in any respect. In the event of any inconsistency between this summary and the terms of the executed Lease, the terms of the executed Lease shall control. All capitalized terms used but not defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to them in the Lease Agreement.

**[Section 1: Parties to the Lease]{.underline}**

**Landlord:** Kimball Properties Inc., an Illinois corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Illinois, with its principal offices located at 2200 Central St., Suite 400, Evanston, IL 60201. Kimball Properties Inc. is the fee simple owner of the building located at 1420 Sherman Ave., Evanston, IL 60201, as more particularly described in Section 11 below.

**Tenant:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC, an Illinois limited liability company, formed on March 14, 2023, under the Illinois Limited Liability Company Act (805 ILCS 180/). Tenant\'s Employer Identification Number (EIN) is 88-3921047. Tenant\'s registered agent for service of process is Northwest Statutory Services, located at 205 N. Michigan Ave., Suite 810, Chicago, IL 60601.

**Membership Interests:** As of the Lease Commencement Date, the membership interests in Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC are held as follows:

> • **Dara Okonkwo** --- 60% ownership interest; Managing Member
>
> • **Felix Ramachandran** --- 40% ownership interest; Silent Member (non-managing)

**Authorized Representative:** Dara Okonkwo, in her capacity as Managing Member, is the sole authorized representative of Tenant for all matters arising under or related to the Lease, including the execution of amendments, delivery and receipt of notices, and communications with Landlord.

**Personal Guaranty:** In connection with the execution of the Lease, Dara Okonkwo executed a limited personal guaranty of Tenant\'s obligations under the Lease for the first twenty-four (24) months of the Lease Term. The guaranty is capped at an amount equal to six (6) months of Base Rent (i.e., \$48,000 based on the Year 1--3 Base Rent rate). The terms of the personal guaranty are set forth in Exhibit E to the Lease Agreement.

**[Section 2: Premises Description and Square Footage]{.underline}**

**Premises Address:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201

**Zoning District:** The Premises is located within the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning district as designated by the City of Evanston Zoning Map. The C1a district permits cannabis dispensary operations subject to the issuance of a special use permit or dispensary-specific permit under Chapter 9, Article 5 of the Evanston Municipal Code.

**Total Rentable Square Footage:** 3,200 square feet

**Square Footage Breakdown:** As depicted in Exhibit A to the Lease (Floor Plan and Square Footage Diagram), the Premises is divided into the following functional areas:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Area**                            **Square Footage**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Retail Sales Floor                  1,600 sq. ft.

  Secure Storage Area                 800 sq. ft.

  Office and Employee Area            600 sq. ft.

  Vestibule / Waiting Area            200 sq. ft.

  **Total**                           **3,200 sq. ft.**
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**Building Description:** Unit B is located on the ground floor of a two-story mixed-use commercial building. Unit A, the adjacent ground-floor commercial unit, is currently occupied by a retail clothing boutique. The second floor of the building contains residential apartment units. The building is of masonry construction, originally constructed in approximately 1948, with subsequent renovations completed in 2011.

**Rear Access:** The Premises includes a dedicated rear entrance for deliveries and secure product intake, accessible from the building\'s service alley located along the northern boundary of the property. This rear entrance is separate from the primary customer entrance on Sherman Avenue and is designed to facilitate secure cannabis product deliveries in compliance with state and local delivery intake requirements.

**Parking:** Pursuant to Exhibit B to the Lease (Parking Allocation Map), Tenant shall have access to eight (8) designated parking spaces in the rear parking lot, which is shared with other building tenants. The parking allocation satisfies the City of Evanston\'s minimum off-street parking requirements applicable to the Premises based on its square footage and intended commercial use.

**[Section 3: Lease Term and Renewal Options]{.underline}**

**Lease Commencement Date:** January 8, 2024.

**Initial Lease Term:** Seven (7) years, commencing on January 8, 2024, and expiring on January 7, 2031, unless sooner terminated or extended in accordance with the terms of the Lease.

**Renewal Options:** Tenant is granted two (2) successive options to renew the Lease for additional terms of three (3) years each, as follows:

> *First Renewal Term:* January 8, 2031 through January 7, 2034
>
> *Second Renewal Term:* January 8, 2034 through January 7, 2037

If both renewal options are exercised, the total potential Lease Term, inclusive of the initial term and both renewal periods, is thirteen (13) years.

**Renewal Exercise Requirements:** To exercise a renewal option, Tenant must deliver written notice of its intent to renew to Landlord not less than one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Failure to timely deliver such notice shall be deemed a waiver of the renewal option for the applicable renewal term and all subsequent renewal terms.

**Early Termination:** Tenant has no right to terminate the Lease prior to the expiration of the then-current term, except in the following limited circumstances:

> \(a\) **Condemnation.** Condemnation or taking by eminent domain of more than twenty-five percent (25%) of the rentable area of the Premises, rendering the remainder inadequate for the operation of a cannabis dispensary in Tenant\'s reasonable judgment.
>
> \(b\) **Casualty.** A fire or other casualty rendering the Premises untenantable for a period exceeding one hundred eighty (180) consecutive days, where Landlord has not substantially completed restoration within such period.
>
> \(c\) **Permanent Permit Denial.** A final and permanent denial of Tenant\'s application for a City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit, as described more fully in Section 5 below.

**Rent Commencement Date:** Notwithstanding the Lease Commencement Date, Tenant\'s obligation to pay Base Rent shall begin on the later of: (i) April 1, 2024, or (ii) the date on which Tenant receives its local cannabis dispensary permit from the City of Evanston (the \"Rent Commencement Date\"). However, if Tenant has not received such permit by October 1, 2024, the Rent Commencement Date shall be deemed to be October 1, 2024, regardless of whether the dispensary permit has been issued. During the period from the Lease Commencement Date through the Rent Commencement Date, Tenant shall occupy the Premises in a pre-rent-commencement occupancy status, during which Tenant is obligated to pay only its proportionate share of common area maintenance charges and the cost of Tenant\'s insurance obligations under the Lease, as described in Section 4.

**Minimum Lease Term Compliance:** The seven-year initial term of the Lease exceeds the City of Evanston\'s minimum three-year lease requirement for cannabis dispensary permit applicants, thereby satisfying the applicable regulatory threshold for demonstrating site control.

**[Section 4: Rent and Financial Terms]{.underline}**

**Base Rent (Initial Term):** Base Rent for the initial seven-year Lease Term is structured with ten percent (10%) escalations at three-year intervals, as set forth below:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Period**                                  **Monthly Base Rent**   **Annual Base Rent**   **Per Sq. Ft. (Annual)**
  ------------------------------------------- ----------------------- ---------------------- --------------------------
  Years 1--3 (Jan. 8, 2024 -- Jan. 7, 2027)   \$8,000.00              \$96,000.00            \$30.00

  Years 4--5 (Jan. 8, 2027 -- Jan. 7, 2029)   \$8,800.00              \$105,600.00           \$33.00

  Years 6--7 (Jan. 8, 2029 -- Jan. 7, 2031)   \$9,680.00              \$116,160.00           \$36.30
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Renewal Term Rent:** Base Rent during each renewal term shall be the fair market rent for comparable commercial retail space in the Evanston C1a Commercial Mixed-Use district, as determined by mutual agreement of the parties. If the parties are unable to agree upon fair market rent within sixty (60) days following Tenant\'s exercise of a renewal option, the fair market rent shall be determined by an independent MAI-certified appraiser jointly selected by the parties (or, if they cannot agree on an appraiser, each party shall select one MAI appraiser, and the two selected appraisers shall jointly select a third, whose determination shall be binding). In no event shall the renewal term Base Rent be less than the Base Rent payable during Year 7 of the initial term (\$9,680.00 per month) or more than one hundred fifteen percent (115%) of such amount (\$11,132.00 per month).

**Additional Rent / Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Charges:** In addition to Base Rent, Tenant shall pay its proportionate share of common area maintenance costs, real property taxes, and building insurance premiums. Tenant\'s proportionate share, based on the ratio of the Premises\' rentable square footage to the total rentable square footage of the building, is estimated at thirty-eight percent (38%). As of the Lease Commencement Date, the estimated monthly CAM charges payable by Tenant are approximately \$2,400.00 per month. CAM charges are subject to annual reconciliation based on actual costs incurred by Landlord.

**Pre-Rent-Commencement Charges:** From the Lease Commencement Date (January 8, 2024) through the Rent Commencement Date, Tenant\'s sole financial obligations under the Lease are: (a) payment of Tenant\'s proportionate share of CAM charges, and (b) payment of the cost of procuring and maintaining all insurance coverages required of Tenant under the Lease. No Base Rent is due during the pre-rent-commencement occupancy period.

**Security Deposit:** Tenant has deposited with Landlord the sum of \$24,000.00, equal to three (3) months of Year 1--3 Base Rent, as a security deposit. The security deposit shall be held by Landlord in an interest-bearing escrow account in accordance with applicable Illinois law. The security deposit, less any deductions for damages, unpaid rent, or other sums due under the Lease, shall be returned to Tenant within sixty (60) days following the later of Lease termination or Tenant\'s surrender of the Premises.

**Late Payment Fee:** Any installment of Base Rent, Additional Rent, or other sum due under the Lease that is not received by Landlord within ten (10) days after its due date shall be subject to a late payment fee equal to five percent (5%) of the overdue amount. In addition, all unpaid amounts shall accrue interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month from the due date until paid in full.

**Build-Out Allowance:** Landlord shall provide Tenant with a tenant improvement allowance in the amount of \$75,000.00 (the \"TI Allowance\"). The TI Allowance shall be payable to Tenant upon substantial completion of Tenant\'s build-out and Tenant\'s submission to Landlord of paid invoices and lien waivers from all contractors and subcontractors. Tenant is responsible for all build-out costs in excess of the TI Allowance. The estimated total cost of Tenant\'s build-out is \$425,000.00, resulting in an estimated net cost to Tenant of \$350,000.00 after application of the TI Allowance.

**[Section 5: Permitted Use and Cannabis-Specific Provisions]{.underline}**

**Permitted Use:** The Premises shall be used solely and exclusively for the operation of a licensed adult-use and medical cannabis dispensary, in full compliance with the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/) (the \"Act\"), all applicable rules and regulations promulgated by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (\"IDFPR\"), and all local ordinances and regulations of the City of Evanston, including but not limited to Chapter 9, Article 5 of the Evanston Municipal Code (the \"Dispensary Ordinance\"). No other use of the Premises is authorized without Landlord\'s prior written consent and an amendment to this Lease.

**Landlord\'s Cannabis Acknowledgment:** Landlord expressly acknowledges and consents to Tenant\'s intended use of the Premises for the retail sale of cannabis and cannabis-infused products as authorized under the laws of the State of Illinois. Landlord further acknowledges that:

> \(a\) Cannabis remains classified as a Schedule I controlled substance under the federal Controlled Substances Act (21 U.S.C. § 812), and the manufacture, distribution, dispensation, and possession of cannabis remain illegal under federal law regardless of state authorization;
>
> \(b\) Landlord has been advised by independent legal counsel of the risks associated with leasing property for cannabis-related use, including but not limited to the risk of federal criminal prosecution, civil forfeiture of the property under 21 U.S.C. § 881, and potential adverse impacts on financing, insurance, and property valuation; and
>
> \(c\) Landlord voluntarily and knowingly accepts the foregoing risks in consideration of the rental income and other benefits of the Lease.

A fully executed counterpart of Landlord\'s Cannabis Use Acknowledgment and Consent is attached to the Lease as Exhibit D.

**Compliance Obligation:** Tenant shall at all times maintain in full force and effect all required state and local licenses, permits, and approvals necessary for the lawful operation of a cannabis dispensary at the Premises. Tenant shall promptly notify Landlord, in writing, of any revocation, suspension, non-renewal, or material modification of any such license or permit, including any conditional or provisional status change, within five (5) business days of Tenant\'s receipt of notice thereof from the issuing authority.

**Permit Denial Termination Right:** In the event that Tenant\'s application for a City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit is permanently denied --- defined for purposes of the Lease as a final denial after all available administrative appeals have been exhausted or after the time for filing an appeal has expired without Tenant having filed such appeal --- Tenant shall have the right to terminate this Lease by delivering written notice to Landlord within thirty (30) days after such permanent denial. Upon such termination, Tenant shall forfeit the security deposit in its entirety as liquidated damages, but shall have no further obligation to pay Base Rent, Additional Rent, or any other sum under the Lease beyond the effective date of termination. Tenant shall surrender the Premises in broom-clean condition within thirty (30) days following delivery of the termination notice.

**Operating Hours:** Tenant shall operate the dispensary only during hours permitted by applicable law and regulation. As of the Lease Commencement Date, the City of Evanston Dispensary Ordinance restricts cannabis dispensary operating hours to 6:00 AM through 10:00 PM daily. Tenant shall comply with any subsequent changes to permitted operating hours enacted by the City during the Lease Term.

**Signage:** All exterior and interior signage visible from outside the Premises shall comply with City of Evanston signage regulations, including any cannabis-specific signage restrictions imposed under the Dispensary Ordinance or regulations promulgated thereunder. Tenant must obtain Landlord\'s prior written approval for all exterior signage, which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Tenant\'s signage shall not include depictions of cannabis plants, leaves, or paraphernalia except as permitted by applicable law.

**Security Requirements:** Tenant shall install and maintain, at Tenant\'s sole cost and expense, a comprehensive security system meeting or exceeding all requirements imposed by state law, IDFPR regulations, and the City of Evanston Dispensary Ordinance. The security system shall include, at a minimum: (i) twenty-four hour, seven day per week (24/7) video surveillance of all areas of the Premises, including the retail sales floor, secure storage area, vestibule, employee areas, and all points of ingress and egress, utilizing cameras with a minimum resolution of 1080p and a recording retention period of not less than thirty (30) days; (ii) panic alarm buttons accessible to employees and connected directly to the Evanston Police Department; and (iii) a secure entry vestibule with controlled access capable of limiting entry to one customer or group of customers at a time, consistent with state and local security protocols.

**Odor Control:** Tenant shall install and maintain, at Tenant\'s sole cost and expense, an HVAC and carbon filtration system adequate to prevent cannabis-related odors from migrating to adjacent units within the building or to common areas. The odor mitigation system shall be designed, installed, and maintained in compliance with all applicable state and local requirements. Landlord reserves the right, upon receipt of documented odor complaints from other tenants of the building or from adjacent property owners, to require Tenant to undertake additional remediation measures at Tenant\'s cost, including upgrading or replacing the carbon filtration system or engaging a qualified environmental consultant to assess and remediate the issue.

**[Section 6: Property Survey and Distances to Nearby Sensitive Uses]{.underline}**

This section summarizes the findings set forth in Exhibit C to the Lease, which is a property survey and site plan prepared by Meridian Land Surveyors LLC (IPLS License #035-004728), dated December 15, 2023 (the \"Survey\").

**Purpose:** The Survey was commissioned by Kimball Properties Inc. in anticipation of Tenant\'s intended cannabis dispensary use of the Premises. The Survey was prepared for the specific purpose of documenting straight-line distances from the Premises to nearby sensitive uses identified under the City of Evanston Dispensary Ordinance, thereby facilitating a preliminary zoning compliance assessment prior to execution of the Lease.

**Measurement Methodology:** All distances set forth below are straight-line (Euclidean) distances measured from the nearest point of the Premises boundary (i.e., the boundary of Unit B at 1420 Sherman Ave.) to the nearest point of the property boundary of each identified sensitive use, as calculated by a licensed Illinois Professional Land Surveyor in accordance with standard surveying practices. Distances are rounded to the nearest ten feet.

**Distances to Sensitive Uses:**

**1. Chute Middle School** (K--8 public school)

> Address: 1400 Oakton St., Evanston, IL 60201
>
> Straight-Line Distance: **1,180 feet** City of Evanston Required Minimum Buffer: 1,000 feet **Status: Compliant.** The Premises is located 180 feet beyond the required minimum buffer distance from the nearest K--12 school.

**2. Tiny Acorns Learning Center** (licensed daycare center)

> Address: 1635 Hinman Ave., Evanston, IL 60201
>
> Straight-Line Distance: **890 feet** City of Evanston Required Minimum Buffer: 500 feet **Status: Compliant based on straight-line measurement.** The Premises is located 390 feet beyond the required minimum buffer distance as measured on a straight-line basis.

**3. First United Methodist Church of Evanston** (house of worship)

> Address: 516 Church St., Evanston, IL 60201
>
> Straight-Line Distance: **1,450 feet** Note: The City of Evanston Dispensary Ordinance (Chapter 9, Article 5) does not include houses of worship among the categories of sensitive uses subject to buffer distance requirements. This distance is provided for informational purposes only and is not required for zoning compliance.

**4. Prairie Wellness Dispensary** (existing licensed cannabis dispensary)

> Address: 807 Church St., Evanston, IL 60201
>
> Straight-Line Distance: **2,100 feet** City of Evanston Required Minimum Buffer from Another Licensed Cannabis Dispensary: 1,500 feet **Status: Compliant.** The Premises is located 600 feet beyond the required minimum inter-dispensary buffer distance.

**Important Note Regarding Measurement Methodology:** The distances set forth above are straight-line distances as determined by the licensed surveyor retained by Landlord. Tenant\'s counsel should note that the City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit application form (Section 3, Question 7) requests that applicants provide distances measured \"along the nearest pedestrian route\" from the Premises to each identified sensitive use. Pedestrian-route distances will generally be equal to or greater than straight-line distances due to the grid pattern of Evanston\'s street network. However, applicants should independently calculate and verify pedestrian-route distances for submission on the application form, as the measurement methodology specified in the application form differs from the straight-line survey methodology utilized in the Survey and summarized herein. The underlying City ordinance (Chapter 9, Article 5, Section 9-5-9) establishes minimum buffer distances but does not itself specify a measurement methodology (i.e., the ordinance does not state whether buffers are to be measured on a straight-line basis or along a pedestrian route). The Survey was prepared using straight-line measurements, which is the standard methodology for property surveys in the State of Illinois. Copies of the full Survey, including the surveyor\'s certification and plat, are available as part of the Lease exhibit package.

**[Section 7: Insurance Requirements Under the Lease]{.underline}**

**Tenant\'s Insurance Obligations:** Tenant shall procure and maintain throughout the Lease Term, at Tenant\'s sole cost and expense, the following minimum insurance coverages:

> \(a\) **Commercial General Liability Insurance:** Minimum limits of \$1,000,000 per occurrence and \$2,000,000 in the aggregate, covering bodily injury, property damage, personal injury, and advertising injury arising from Tenant\'s operations at the Premises.
>
> \(b\) **Property Insurance (Contents and Tenant Improvements):** Replacement cost coverage for all of Tenant\'s personal property, trade fixtures, inventory, and tenant improvements located within the Premises.
>
> \(c\) **Workers\' Compensation Insurance:** Statutory limits as required by the Illinois Workers\' Compensation Act (820 ILCS 305/) and employer\'s liability coverage with minimum limits of \$500,000 per accident.
>
> \(d\) **Cannabis-Specific Business Insurance:** Such additional insurance coverages as may be required by applicable state or local law for the operation of a cannabis dispensary, including but not limited to product liability coverage and, if available, coverage for loss or theft of cannabis inventory.

**Note Regarding City of Evanston Insurance Requirements:** The minimum commercial general liability limits required under the Lease (\$1,000,000 per occurrence / \$2,000,000 aggregate) are lower than the insurance requirements imposed by the City of Evanston for cannabis dispensary permit holders, which require minimum limits of \$2,000,000 per occurrence and \$4,000,000 in the aggregate. Tenant must comply with the higher of the two standards and is therefore required to carry commercial general liability insurance at the City-mandated levels for as long as the dispensary permit is in effect.

**Landlord as Additional Insured:** Landlord, Kimball Properties Inc., shall be named as an additional insured on Tenant\'s commercial general liability insurance policy.

**Certificates of Insurance:** Tenant shall provide Landlord with certificates of insurance evidencing all required coverages prior to taking possession of the Premises and annually thereafter upon each policy renewal, and at such other times as Landlord may reasonably request.

**Waiver of Subrogation:** Each party waives any and all rights of recovery, claims, actions, or causes of action against the other party for any loss or damage to the extent such loss or damage is covered by valid and collectible insurance policies maintained by the waiving party, regardless of the cause or origin of such loss or damage. Each party shall cause its insurance carriers to include appropriate waiver of subrogation endorsements in the applicable policies.

**[Section 8: Build-Out and Tenant Improvements]{.underline}**

**Tenant\'s Build-Out Responsibility:** Tenant shall be responsible for all interior improvements necessary to prepare the Premises for operation as a licensed cannabis dispensary in compliance with all applicable state and local requirements. The scope of Tenant\'s build-out shall include, without limitation, the following:

> • Construction of a secure storage vault encompassing approximately 800 square feet, with reinforced walls, a commercial-grade vault door, and access controls meeting IDFPR specifications
>
> • Installation of a secure vestibule and entry system with controlled access, capable of verifying customer identification prior to entry into the retail sales floor
>
> • Installation of a comprehensive 24/7 video surveillance system utilizing cameras with a minimum resolution of 1080p and a recording retention period of not less than thirty (30) days, covering all interior and exterior areas as required by state and local regulations
>
> • Installation of a panic alarm system directly connected to the Evanston Police Department
>
> • HVAC upgrades and installation of a carbon filtration system for odor mitigation, designed to prevent cannabis-related odors from migrating to adjacent units or common areas
>
> • Installation of point-of-sale system infrastructure and integration with the state-mandated seed-to-sale inventory tracking system (BioTrack or successor platform)
>
> • All modifications necessary to ensure full compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and applicable Illinois accessibility standards

**Landlord Improvement Allowance:** Landlord shall provide the TI Allowance of \$75,000.00, as set forth in Section 4 above, toward the cost of Tenant\'s build-out.

**Plans and Permits:** Prior to commencing any construction or build-out work, Tenant shall submit complete architectural and construction plans to Landlord for review and approval. Landlord\'s approval shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Separately, Tenant is solely responsible for obtaining all building permits, construction permits, and other governmental approvals required by the City of Evanston for the build-out work.

**Estimated Build-Out Cost:** The total estimated cost of the build-out is \$425,000.00. After application of the \$75,000.00 TI Allowance, Tenant\'s net estimated cost is \$350,000.00.

**Completion Deadline:** Tenant shall substantially complete all build-out work within one hundred twenty (120) days after the date on which the City of Evanston issues the cannabis dispensary permit to Tenant. \"Substantial completion\" means that the build-out is sufficiently complete to allow the commencement of dispensary operations, subject only to minor punch-list items that do not materially interfere with operations.

**Security Consultant:** Tenant has engaged Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC, under the direction of Marcus Wren, Principal, to design the security plan for the Premises and to ensure that all surveillance, alarm, and access control systems meet or exceed the security requirements imposed by the IDFPR and the City of Evanston.

**[Section 9: Assignment and Subletting]{.underline}**

**Prohibition on Assignment:** Tenant shall not assign, transfer, mortgage, pledge, or encumber the Lease or any interest therein, nor sublet any portion of the Premises, without the prior written consent of Landlord, which consent may be withheld in Landlord\'s sole and absolute discretion.

**Change of Control:** Any transfer, whether voluntary or involuntary, of a controlling ownership interest in Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC --- defined as a transfer resulting in a change in the identity of the person or persons holding, directly or indirectly, more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding membership interests --- shall be deemed an assignment of the Lease requiring Landlord\'s prior written consent.

**Permitted Transfers:** Notwithstanding the foregoing restrictions, transfers of membership interests between the existing members (Dara Okonkwo and Felix Ramachandran) that do not result in a change of the Managing Member shall not require Landlord\'s consent, provided that Tenant delivers written notice to Landlord of any such transfer within fifteen (15) days after its completion.

**Cannabis License Condition:** Any permitted or consented assignment or subletting shall be further conditioned upon the assignee or subtenant holding, or having obtained conditional approval for, all state and local cannabis licenses and permits required for the operation of a cannabis dispensary at the Premises.

**[Section 10: Default, Remedies, and Termination]{.underline}**

**Events of Default by Tenant:** Each of the following shall constitute an Event of Default under the Lease:

> \(a\) Failure by Tenant to pay any installment of Base Rent, Additional Rent, or other sum due under the Lease within ten (10) days after delivery of written notice from Landlord that such payment is past due;
>
> \(b\) Failure by Tenant to procure or maintain any insurance coverage required under the Lease;
>
> \(c\) Revocation, suspension, or non-renewal of Tenant\'s cannabis dispensary permit or state license, where such revocation, suspension, or non-renewal is not cured or reinstated within sixty (60) days;
>
> \(d\) Use of the Premises for any purpose other than the Permitted Use described in Section 5;
>
> \(e\) Violation of any applicable federal, state, or local law or regulation resulting in a governmental order to cease operations at the Premises; or
>
> \(f\) The filing of a voluntary petition in bankruptcy by Tenant, the filing of an involuntary petition against Tenant that is not dismissed within sixty (60) days, or any general assignment by Tenant for the benefit of creditors.

**Landlord\'s Remedies:** Upon the occurrence of an uncured Event of Default, Landlord may, at its option: (a) terminate the Lease upon thirty (30) days\' written notice to Tenant; (b) re-enter and repossess the Premises in accordance with applicable law; and (c) pursue damages, including accelerated rent for the balance of the then-current term, discounted to present value at a rate of six percent (6%) per annum.

**Cure Periods:** Tenant shall have ten (10) days to cure any monetary default following written notice from Landlord. For non-monetary defaults, Tenant shall have thirty (30) days to cure following written notice, provided that if the nature of the default is such that it cannot reasonably be cured within thirty (30) days, the cure period shall be extended for such additional time as may be reasonably necessary, so long as Tenant has commenced cure within the initial thirty-day period and is diligently and continuously pursuing cure to completion.

**Tenant\'s Termination Right --- Permit Denial:** As described in Section 5, Tenant may terminate the Lease upon permanent denial of the City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit, subject to forfeiture of the security deposit.

**Mutual Termination:** The Lease may be terminated at any time by mutual written agreement of the parties, upon such terms and conditions as the parties may agree.

**[Section 12: Miscellaneous Provisions]{.underline}**

**Governing Law:** The Lease shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

**Dispute Resolution:** Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to the Lease or the breach, termination, or validity thereof shall first be submitted to non-binding mediation conducted by a mutually agreed-upon mediator in Cook County, Illinois. If the dispute is not resolved through mediation within sixty (60) days of the initial mediation session, either party may submit the dispute to binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules. The arbitration shall be conducted in Cook County, Illinois, before a single arbitrator. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding and may be entered as a judgment in any court of competent jurisdiction.

**Notices:** All notices, demands, requests, and other communications required or permitted under the Lease shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly delivered when personally delivered, or one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier, or three (3) business days after deposit in the United States mail (certified, return receipt requested, postage prepaid), addressed to the parties at the addresses set forth in Section 1, or at such other addresses as either party may designate by written notice to the other party.

**Entire Agreement:** The Lease, together with all Exhibits attached thereto (Exhibits A through E), constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, representations, understandings, and agreements, whether written or oral.

**Amendments:** No amendment, modification, supplement, or waiver of any provision of the Lease shall be effective unless set forth in a written instrument executed by both Landlord and Tenant.

**Severability:** If any provision of the Lease is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable, or if such modification is not possible, such provision shall be severed from the Lease, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

**Recording:** Neither party shall record the Lease or any memorandum thereof in the public records without the prior written consent of the other party.

**Broker:** No real estate broker, agent, or finder was involved in the negotiation or execution of this Lease. Each party represents and warrants to the other that it has not dealt with any broker, agent, or finder in connection with this transaction, and each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other party from and against any claims for brokerage commissions or finder\'s fees arising from a breach of this representation.

**[Exhibit List]{.underline}**

The following exhibits are attached to the full executed Lease Agreement and form an integral part thereof. These exhibits are not reproduced in this summary but are available for review upon request. Complete copies of all exhibits will be included in the full application package submitted to the City of Evanston as required.

> **Exhibit A:** Floor Plan and Square Footage Diagram --- depicting the layout of the Premises, including the 1,600 sq. ft. retail sales floor, 800 sq. ft. secure storage area, 600 sq. ft. office and employee area, and 200 sq. ft. vestibule/waiting area.
>
> **Exhibit B:** Parking Allocation Map --- identifying the eight (8) designated parking spaces allocated to Tenant in the rear parking lot.
>
> **Exhibit C:** Property Survey and Site Plan --- prepared by Meridian Land Surveyors LLC, dated December 15, 2023, including straight-line distance measurements from the Premises to identified sensitive uses, as summarized in Section 6 above.
>
> **Exhibit D:** Landlord\'s Cannabis Use Acknowledgment and Consent --- executed counterpart signed by Kimball Properties Inc.
>
> **Exhibit E:** Personal Guaranty of Dara Okonkwo --- limited personal guaranty covering the first twenty-four (24) months of the Lease Term, capped at six (6) months of Base Rent.

Copies of Exhibits A through E are available upon request and will be included in the full application package submitted to the City of Evanston as required by the cannabis dispensary permit application process.

This Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License is issued subject to all terms, conditions, limitations, and requirements set forth herein and in the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act, 410 ILCS 705/ et seq., and the administrative rules at 68 Ill. Admin. Code Part 1291. This conditional license is valid for the period commencing on September 22, 2023, and terminating on September 21, 2024, unless extended, suspended, or revoked by the Department in accordance with applicable law. The licensee shall not engage in the sale, dispensing, possession, or distribution of cannabis or cannabis-infused products under this conditional license. This license is issued solely to authorize the licensee to seek local governmental approval and to undertake site preparation activities necessary for the establishment of a dispensing organization at the proposed dispensary location identified above.

**[SECTION 2: PRINCIPAL OFFICERS AND OWNERSHIP DISCLOSURE]{.underline}**

In accordance with Section 15-30 of the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/15-30) and 68 Ill. Admin. Code § 1291.40, the following persons have been disclosed as holding ownership interests of five percent (5%) or more in the licensee, or as serving as principal officers of the licensee:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Felix Ramachandran   Member                                        40%                      Wilmette, IL    None
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All persons identified above have been subject to fingerprint-based background investigation through the Illinois State Police and the Federal Bureau of Investigation and have been found to meet the requirements of 410 ILCS 705/15-30. The Department has reviewed the criminal history records, financial disclosures, and personal history statements submitted by each principal officer and has determined that no disqualifying offenses or conditions exist as of the date of issuance of this conditional license. The licensee is required to notify the Department of any change in the information set forth in this Section within fourteen (14) days of such change, including but not limited to changes of residence, arrest, indictment, conviction, or change in professional license status of any principal officer.

**[SECTION 3: SOCIAL EQUITY DESIGNATION]{.underline}**

The Department has determined that Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC qualifies as a Social Equity Applicant as defined in Section 1-10 of the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/1-10) and as further elaborated in Section 7-10 of the Act.

**Qualifying Basis.** The applicant qualifies on the following grounds:

> \(a\) the applicant has a principal officer who has resided for at least five (5) of the preceding ten (10) years in a Disproportionately Impacted Area as designated by the Department; and
>
> \(b\) the applicant has a principal officer who has been arrested for, convicted of, or adjudicated delinquent for any offense that is eligible for expungement under the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act.

**Qualifying Individual.** The individual upon whose qualifications the social equity designation is based is **Dara Okonkwo**, Managing Member, holding a **sixty percent (60%)** ownership interest in the licensee.

**Ownership Threshold.** For purposes of this license, \"Social Equity Applicant,\" as applied to an entity applicant, means an applicant in which at least 51% of ownership interest is held by one or more individuals who meet the qualifying criteria set forth in Section 7-10 of the Act. The Department has verified that the qualifying individual identified above holds an ownership interest equal to or exceeding the 51% threshold.

**Ongoing Compliance Notice.** The social equity designation reflected on this conditional license is based on the ownership structure and qualifying individual(s) disclosed at the time of application. Any change in ownership that causes the licensee to no longer meet the 51% qualifying ownership threshold shall be reported to the Department within fourteen (14) days and may result in revocation of social equity designation. Loss of social equity designation may affect the licensee\'s eligibility for social equity fee reductions, priority scoring, technical assistance, and other benefits available under the Act.

**[SECTION 4: CONDITIONS OF CONDITIONAL LICENSE]{.underline}**

This Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License is issued subject to the following conditions. Failure to satisfy any condition within the timeframes specified herein may result in the conditional license being deemed abandoned, revoked, or otherwise rendered void at the discretion of the Department.

**Condition 1 --- Local Zoning and Permit Approval.** The licensee must obtain all required local zoning approvals, special use permits, and a local cannabis dispensary permit from the municipality or county in which the proposed dispensary is to be located. Proof of local approval, including certified copies of all permits, zoning certificates, and resolutions or ordinances of approval, must be submitted to the Department. The licensee shall not represent to any municipality, county, or other governmental body that this conditional license constitutes authority to operate a dispensing organization or to sell cannabis or cannabis-infused products.

**Condition 2 --- Conversion Timeline.** The licensee must submit a complete application for conversion from conditional to permanent license status within one hundred eighty (180) days of the date of issuance of the final local dispensing permit. The conversion application must include all documentation required by 68 Ill. Admin. Code § 1291.50, including but not limited to evidence of local governmental approval, proof of lease or ownership of the dispensary premises, a security plan, staffing plan, and evidence of seed-to-sale tracking system integration. Failure to submit a complete conversion application within this 180-day period shall result in the conditional license being deemed abandoned. For purposes of this Condition, the 180-day period begins on the date reflected on the face of the final local dispensing permit issued by the applicable municipality or county.

**Condition 3 --- Conditional License Validity Period.** This conditional license is valid for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of issuance, commencing September 22, 2023, and terminating September 21, 2024. The licensee may not operate a dispensing organization under a conditional license. The conditional license serves only as evidence of the licensee\'s authorization to seek local approval and to undertake site preparation activities, including lease execution, architectural design, construction or buildout of the proposed premises, and procurement of security and point-of-sale systems. No cannabis, cannabis-infused products, or cannabis paraphernalia may be purchased, possessed, stored, sold, or dispensed under the authority of this conditional license.

**Condition 4 --- Extension Requests.** A licensee that is unable to satisfy the conditions for conversion within the validity period of this conditional license may petition the Department for a single extension of up to six (6) months, provided that the petition is filed no later than sixty (60) days prior to the expiration date of the conditional license and demonstrates good cause for the delay, including but not limited to documented delays in local government approval processes, construction delays beyond the licensee\'s control, or force majeure events. Extension petitions must be submitted in writing to the Cannabis Regulation Oversight Section using Department Form CR-EXT-01 and must be accompanied by all supporting documentation. Extension petitions filed fewer than sixty (60) days before expiration may be denied without review. The Department shall issue a written determination on extension petitions within thirty (30) days of receipt of a complete petition.

**Condition 5 --- Conversion Fee.** Upon submission of the conversion application, the licensee shall remit the conversion fee of one hundred thousand dollars (\$100,000.00) as set forth in 410 ILCS 705/15-35(c). Social equity applicants who maintain their social equity designation at the time of conversion may be eligible for a reduced conversion fee as determined by the Department. The conversion fee is non-refundable.

**Condition 6 --- No Transfer.** This conditional license is not transferable. Any change in ownership of more than ten percent (10%) of the licensee\'s ownership interests, or any change in the identity of the designated Agent-in-Charge, requires prior written approval of the Department. Requests for approval of ownership changes must be submitted at least thirty (30) days prior to the proposed effective date of the change and must include complete personal history disclosures and background investigation authorizations for any proposed new principal officer. Unapproved transfers or ownership changes shall constitute grounds for immediate revocation.

**Condition 7 --- Compliance with All Applicable Laws.** The licensee shall at all times comply with the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/), all administrative rules promulgated thereunder (68 Ill. Admin. Code Part 1291), and all applicable federal, state, and local laws, ordinances, and regulations, including but not limited to laws relating to taxation, labor, zoning, building codes, fire safety, accessibility, and environmental protection.

**Condition 8 --- Site Readiness.** Prior to conversion, the licensee must demonstrate that the proposed dispensary location at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201 is fully built out and operational-ready, including installation of all required security systems (video surveillance, alarm, access control), point-of-sale systems, inventory management systems, and seed-to-sale tracking system integration with the Illinois Cannabis Tracking and Compliance System (COMPASS). The Department or its authorized agents may conduct an on-site inspection of the proposed premises prior to issuance of a permanent license, and the licensee shall provide access upon reasonable notice.

**[SECTION 5: GROUNDS FOR REVOCATION OR NON-RENEWAL]{.underline}**

The Department may revoke, suspend, or decline to renew this conditional license upon any of the following grounds:

> \(a\) Failure of the licensee to satisfy the conditions set forth in Section 4 within the applicable validity period or any approved extension thereof;
>
> \(b\) Material misrepresentation or omission in the application for this conditional license, or in any subsequent filing with the Department;
>
> \(c\) Any change in ownership of the licensee without prior written approval of the Department, as required by Condition 6;
>
> \(d\) Conviction of a disqualifying offense by any principal officer of the licensee, including: controlled substance felonies, sex offenses, and violent felonies (no time limitation); or any other felony conviction within five (5) years preceding the date of discovery;
>
> \(e\) Loss of social equity designation due to changes in ownership structure that cause the licensee to fall below the 51% qualifying ownership threshold;
>
> \(f\) Failure to maintain the proposed dispensary location, including abandonment, termination, or non-renewal of the lease or ownership interest in the premises identified in this license; or
>
> \(g\) Any other grounds for revocation or discipline as set forth in the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act or the administrative rules promulgated thereunder.

**[SECTION 6: CERTIFICATION AND EXECUTION]{.underline}**

This Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License, bearing License Number **ADC-285-0147**, is issued by the authority of the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, Division of Professional Regulation, pursuant to the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act, 410 ILCS 705/, on this 22nd day of September, 2023.

ILLINOIS DEPARTMENT OF FINANCIAL AND PROFESSIONAL REGULATION Division of Professional Regulation

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Title: Director, Division of Professional Regulation

Date: September 22, 2023

\[SEAL\]

License Certificate Number: ADC-285-0147

This license must be displayed at the licensed premises upon commencement of operations under a permanent license. Conditional licenses may not be displayed to the public as evidence of authority to sell cannabis or cannabis-infused products. Unauthorized display of this conditional license for such purpose may constitute a violation of the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act and applicable administrative rules.

**[APPENDIX A: DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

*Selected definitions excerpted from the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/1-10) and related provisions for the convenience of the licensee. In the event of any conflict between the definitions set forth below and the statutory text, the statutory text shall control.*

**\"Adult-Use Dispensing Organization\"** means a facility operated by an organization or business that is licensed by the Department of Financial and Professional Regulation to acquire cannabis from a licensed cultivation center, craft grower, processing organization, or infuser organization for the purpose of selling or dispensing cannabis, cannabis-infused products, cannabis paraphernalia, or related supplies to purchasers who are twenty-one (21) years of age or older, in accordance with the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act.

**\"Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License\"** means a license issued by the Department that entitles the holder to seek a local permit and prepare a dispensary location but does not entitle the holder to purchase, possess, sell, or dispense cannabis or cannabis-infused products. A conditional license is valid for the period specified on the face of the license and is subject to the conditions set forth therein and in applicable law.

**\"Social Equity Applicant\"** means an applicant that is an Illinois resident and that meets one or more of the following criteria:

> \(i\) an individual who has resided for at least five (5) of the preceding ten (10) years in a Disproportionately Impacted Area as designated by the Department;
>
> \(ii\) an individual who has been arrested for, convicted of, or adjudicated delinquent for any offense that is eligible for expungement under the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act; or
>
> \(iii\) an individual who has a parent, legal guardian, child, or spouse who has been arrested for, convicted of, or adjudicated delinquent for any offense that is eligible for expungement under the Act.

For an entity applicant, at least 51% of the ownership interest must be held by one or more individuals who qualify under the criteria set forth above. The qualifying ownership interest must be maintained throughout the period of licensure for the entity to retain its social equity designation.

**\"Principal Officer\"** means an individual who holds an ownership interest of five percent (5%) or more in the applicant organization, or who serves as a member of the board of directors, executive officer, or manager of the organization. For purposes of the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act, a principal officer includes any person with the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of the organization, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise.

**\"Agent-in-Charge\"** means the principal officer designated by the dispensing organization as the primary contact responsible for day-to-day operations and regulatory compliance at the licensed premises. The Agent-in-Charge must be an Illinois resident and must be available to the Department for communication regarding all matters related to the license.

**\"Disproportionately Impacted Area\"** means a census tract or comparable geographic area identified and designated by the Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, in consultation with the Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, that meets criteria related to historical cannabis-related arrest rates, incarceration rates, poverty rates, and unemployment rates, as set forth in Section 10-40 of the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act. The list of Disproportionately Impacted Areas is published by the Department and updated periodically.

**END OF LICENSE DOCUMENT**

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

{"file_path": "requirements-matrix.md", "content": "# Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application — Compliance Requirements Matrix

**Applicant:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC
**Proposed Location:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201
**State Conditional License:** ADC-285-0147 (issued Sept. 22, 2023; expires Sept. 21, 2024)
**Application Window:** February 1, 2024 – May 31, 2024 (target submission: May 15, 2024)
**Review Authority:** Evanston Municipal Code, Title 9, Ch. 9, Art. 5 ("Article 5"); City of Evanston Application Checklist & Submission Guide (Jan. 15, 2024); Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form v3.2 (Jan. 12, 2024)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This matrix evaluates the Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC ("Greenleaf") dispensary permit application package against the controlling Evanston Municipal Code (Article 5), the City's Application Checklist & Submission Guide, and the City's Application Form. The package reviewed consists of: (i) the Application Form (v3.2); (ii) the Greenleaf Business Plan Summary; (iii) the Community Engagement Sessions Report; (iv) the Insurance & Bonding Status Memo; (v) the Lease Summary of Key Terms; and (vi) the State Conditional License.

**Headline conclusions:**

1. **The application form (v3.2) is included as an unfilled template.** All data fields, signature blocks, and notarization are blank. The substantive applicant information resides in the supporting documents. The form must be completed in full (every field or "N/A"), signed, and notarized before the package can be accepted.

2. **Three critical, application-blocking deficiencies** must be cured before submission or the application will be deemed incomplete: (a) the **Odor Mitigation Plan lacks the required Illinois Professional Engineer certification** (the form states the application "will not proceed to scoring" without it); (b) the **Security Plan is not yet finalized** (confirmed in the Insurance Memo), which also blocks underwriting of the required cannabis crime/theft insurance; and (c) **Community Engagement Session 2 was noticed only 12 days in advance**, short of the 14-day minimum, which under §9-5-8(F) renders the application incomplete and requires an additional compliant session (no waiver of the two-session minimum is available).

3. **Bonding capacity is undocumented.** Only a "preliminary inquiry" exists; no commitment letter has been obtained. This both fails Guide Item 17 / Form Q26 expectations and forfeits the 10-point bonding-capacity sub-score within Financial Viability.

4. **Several internal inconsistencies** must be reconciled (registered agent name, parking space count, contact phone/email, R3 designation attribution) because §9-5-5(C) requires materials to be "complete and accurate" and material misrepresentation is a revocation ground (§9-5-25(D)(6)).

5. **The Application Form and Submission Guide contain numerous conflicts with the controlling Municipal Code** (zoning districts, buffer measurement method, occupancy formula, cure period, fee payment methods, and others). Where the Code and subordinate documents conflict, the enacted Code controls. These conflicts are catalogued in Part A and should be raised with the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator.

**Status summary (70 requirements assessed):**

| Status | Count |
|---|---|
| Compliant | 26 |
| Partial — Gap | 18 |
| Non-Compliant / Deficient | 4 |
| Not Submitted / Missing | 12 |
| Pending (acceptable pre-issuance) | 6 |
| Discrepancy / Informational | 4 |

---

## 2. Methodology and Status Legend

Each requirement is traced to its authority: the Municipal Code section (controlling), the Submission Guide checklist item, and/or the Application Form question. "Submission Status / Evidence" describes what the package actually contains. Status is assigned per the legend below.

| Status Label | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Compliant** | Requirement fully satisfied by the package as presented. |
| **Partial — Gap** | Core requirement addressed but one or more specified elements are missing, incomplete, or not yet finalized. |
| **Non-Compliant** | Requirement violated or a mandatory threshold not met. |
| **Not Submitted** | Required item is absent from the package and not addressed. |
| **Pending (Pre-Issuance)** | Item is properly deferred to the post-approval/pre-issuance stage; acceptable at application stage. |
| **Discrepancy** | Conflicting information within the package or between the Code, Guide, and Form. |

**Priority flags used in remediation notes:** **[P1 — Blocking]** must be cured or the application will be deemed incomplete / will not proceed to scoring; **[P2 — High]** materially weakens the application or forfeits scoring points; **[P3 — Moderate]** should be cured to avoid a deficiency notice; **[P4 — Clean-up]** accuracy/consistency item.

## 3. Part A — Authority Conflicts: Municipal Code vs. Submission Guide vs. Application Form

The Code is the enacted ordinance and controls over the Guide and Form. The following conflicts were identified during the review and should be confirmed with the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator, because they affect how the applicant should prepare the submission.

| # | Subject | Municipal Code (controlling) | Submission Guide | Application Form | Impact / Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A-1 | Application fee payment method | §9-5-6(C): certified check or electronic funds transfer (EFT) | §1: cashier's check or money order | §13: certified check, cashier's check, or wire transfer | Three-way conflict. Code does not authorize money order or wire transfer; Guide omits certified check/EFT. Applicant should use certified check or EFT to satisfy the Code. |
| A-2 | Deficiency cure period | §9-5-7(C): 30 calendar days to cure | §1, §4: 14 calendar days | §13: 14 calendar days | Guide/Form give 16 fewer days than the Code. The Code controls; applicant is entitled to 30 days. |
| A-3 | Completeness determination timeline | §9-5-7(C): within 90 calendar days of receipt | §1/§4: 30-day completeness "target"; 90-day review after completeness | §6: 90-day review after completeness determination | Code's 90 days runs from receipt for the completeness determination; Guide/Form measure different periods. Confusing but not a hard conflict. |
| A-4 | Permitted zoning districts | §9-5-9(A): C1a, C2 Commercial-Service, oC1 Office-Commercial (all special use) | Item 20: references C1a only | §3 Q3 note: "C1a, C1 Commercial, and B2 Business" | **Major Form error.** Form lists C1 and B2 (not authorized) and omits C2 and oC1 (authorized). Greenleaf's C1a site is valid regardless. |
| A-5 | Buffer measurement method | §9-5-9(C): nearest property line to nearest property line (straight-line) | Item 8: "as set forth in the Application Form" | §3 Q7: "nearest pedestrian route"; straight-line "not acceptable" | **Major Code/Form conflict.** The Lease Survey uses straight-line (matches Code). Form demands pedestrian route. Greenleaf's margins are large enough to satisfy either method, but the Form's instruction conflicts with the Code. |
| A-6 | Maximum occupancy formula | §9-5-13(J): 1 customer per 40 sq. ft. of **retail floor area** (1,600 ÷ 40 = 40) | §7(8): "based on retail floor area per the ordinance" | §5 Q12: **total** dispensary square footage ÷ 40 (3,200 ÷ 40 = 80) | **Major Form error.** Form would double the lawful occupancy. Business Plan correctly uses retail floor area (40). |
| A-7 | Certificate of Good Standing recency | §9-5-5(B)(3): within preceding 60 days | Item 4: within 60 days | §13 checklist: within 30 days | Form is more restrictive than Code/Guide. |
| A-8 | Principal-officer SSN | §9-5-5(B)(4): Social Security number (full) | Item 5: not specified | §10 Q14A: last 4 digits only | Form does not collect what the Code requires. |
| A-9 | Buffer survey recency | §9-5-5(B)(7): no recency stated | Item 8: not specified | §3 Q6 note: survey dated within 60 days of submission | Form adds a requirement not in the Code. The Lease Survey (Dec. 15, 2023) would exceed 60 days if submitted May 15, 2024. |
| A-10 | Agent-in-Charge residency | §9-5-13(I): no residency rule; present or reachable within 30 minutes | Item 19: not specified | §2 Q13(d): must reside within 30 miles | Form adds a requirement not in the Code. Okonkwo (Evanston) satisfies it. |
| A-11 | State license at application | §9-5-5(B)(2): conditional/permanent license **or** a pending State application in good standing | §2: must hold a valid license | §1 Notice 9: must hold a valid license; pending not accepted | Guide/Form are more restrictive than the Code. Greenleaf holds a conditional license, so satisfied either way. |
| A-12 | Form section-citation errors | n/a | n/a | §5 Q11 cites §9-5-17 for operating hours (should be §9-5-13(F)); §7 Q22 cites §9-5-15 for community engagement (should be §9-5-8) | Form cites wrong Code sections. |
| A-13 | Social-equity ownership threshold | §9-5-19(B): "a principal officer holding a majority of the ownership interest" | §2: ≥51% held by one or more individuals | §6 Q19 note: "individuals" (plural) | Code requires a single principal officer holding a majority; Guide/Form/State allow 51% by one or more. Greenleaf (Okonkwo 60%) satisfies all. |
| A-14 | Social-equity workforce definition | §9-5-19(D): "all employees, including full-time and part-time" | Item 14: not specified | §6 Q21(a): excludes principal officers and legal counsel | Form narrows the workforce base vs. the Code. Affects the 50% R3 calculation. |
| A-15 | Coordinator contact phone | n/a | (847) 448-4311 | (847) 448-8163 | Inconsistent contact number for the same official. |
| A-16 | Coordinator contact email | n/a | rvasquezmorris@… | rvasquez-morris@… (hyphenated) | Inconsistent email format. |

Obtain at least a binder or commitment letter from Canopy (or an alternate specialty carrier) before submission; finalize the security plan to unblock underwriting. Coverage must be bound before permit issuance. |

| R-59 | Insurers rated ≥A- by AM Best | §9-5-16(C) | AM Best ratings not documented for any carrier. | Partial — Gap | **[P3]** Confirm and document A- (or equivalent) AM Best ratings for all carriers; include on certificates. |

Insurance Memo flags this as an open question. | Non-Compliant | **[P2 — High]** Obtain a formal commitment letter (on surety/financial-institution letterhead, signed by an authorized officer, dated ≤60 days) confirming ability to provide the $50,000 bond/LOC naming the City as obligee/beneficiary. **Without it, the application forfeits the 10-point bonding-capacity sub-score** (Form §8 rubric note: 0 points if not provided). Confirm surety AM Best ≥A- (§9-5-17(B)). The bond/LOC itself is due pre-issuance. |

Two bases asserted: (a) R3 residency in tract 8092.02 — **24-month continuous duration not documented**; (b) 2016 expunged misdemeanor — qualifies, but Code phrasing "expunged, sealed, or pardoned by the Governor of Illinois" is ambiguous as to whether "by the Governor" modifies "expunged/sealed" or only "pardoned." State has granted SE designation under the State's broader standard. | Partial — Gap | **[P2]** Cleanest path: document Okonkwo's ≥24-month continuous residency in R3 tract 8092.02 under §9-5-19(B)(1). Duration and two proofs of residency not in package. | Not Submitted | **[P2]** Complete Form Q20 with ≥24-month duration and attach two proofs of residency (utility bill, bank statement, lease, tax bill, or vehicle registration, ≤90 days). |

Partnerships not yet formalized (R-47). | Partial — Gap | Formalize workforce-development partnerships (R-47); sign Form Q21 commitment. Note Form's workforce definition excludes principal officers/counsel (A-14) — confirm the calculation basis with the Coordinator. |

R3 areas are designated by the **Evanston City Council** (§9-5-19(G)); the State designates "Disproportionately Impacted Areas" (a different program). | Discrepancy | **[P4]** Correct the attribution: R3 areas are City-Council-designated; State DIAs are separate. Tract 8092.02 is validly on the City's R3 list. |

Form's 30-mile residency rule (A-10) is satisfied (Evanston). |

Form's formula (total ÷ 40 = 80) is erroneous (A-6). | Compliant | Use the Code-correct figure (40) on Form Q12; post the limit conspicuously at the retail-floor entrance. |

Vendor confirmation letter gap (R-34). | Partial — Gap | Cure R-34 (vendor letter). Confirm real-time recording and daily physical reconciliation in the finalized plan. |

---

## 5. Part C — Internal Package Inconsistencies

These accuracy items should be reconciled before submission (§9-5-5(C); material misrepresentation is a revocation ground under §9-5-25(D)(6)).

| # | Item | Conflict | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| C-1 | Registered agent name | State License: "Keystone Statutory Services"; Business Plan & Lease: "Northwest Statutory Services" (same address). | Verify the correct registered agent with the IL SOS and correct all documents. |
| C-2 | Parking spaces | Lease §2/Ex. B: 8 designated spaces; Community Engagement Report (Session 1): "twelve (12) dedicated parking spaces." | Reconcile to the Lease figure (8) and correct the community-engagement narrative. |
| C-3 | Applicant contact phone | Business Plan: (872) 555-0193; Insurance Memo: (847) 555-0193. | Use one consistent number on the Form and all exhibits. |
| C-4 | Applicant contact email | Business Plan/Engagement Report: greenleaftherapeuticsllc.com (and info@…); Insurance Memo: greenleaftherapeutics.com (no "llc"). | Standardize one domain across all documents. |
| C-5 | R3 designation attribution | Business Plan attributes R3 designation to the "State of Illinois"; Code §9-5-19(G) attributes it to the City Council. | Correct to "City of Evanston City Council." (See R-66.) |
| C-6 | Coordinator contact (City) | Guide phone (847) 448-4311 vs. Form (847) 448-8163; Guide email unhyphenated vs. Form hyphenated. | City-side inconsistency; confirm the correct contact with the Department of Community Development. |

---

## 6. Part D — Critical Deficiencies & Priority Remediation Plan

### D.1 Blocking items (P1) — cure before submission or the application will be deemed incomplete / will not proceed to scoring

1. **Complete and execute the Application Form (R-01).** All fields, signature, and notarization are currently blank.
2. **Pay the $5,000 application fee (R-02)** by certified check or EFT (Code method).
3. **Obtain the Illinois PE certification for the Odor Mitigation Plan (R-43).** The Form states the application will not proceed to scoring without it; finalize HVAC specs and air-exchange rates.
4. **Finalize the Security Plan (R-35, R-41).** Currently unfinished; its completion also unblocks cannabis crime/theft insurance underwriting.
5. **Remedy the Session 2 notice deficiency (R-49).** Only 12 days' notice was given (14 required). Hold an additional community engagement session with compliant ≥14-day newspaper and City-website notice. No waiver of the two-session minimum is available (§9-5-8(F)).
6. **Obtain a binder or commitment letter for Cannabis Crime/Theft insurance (R-57).** No evidence of ability to obtain the required $500,000 coverage exists yet.

### D.2 High-priority items (P2) — materially weaken the application or forfeit scoring points

7. **Obtain a bonding commitment letter (R-61).** Without it, Greenleaf forfeits the 10-point Financial Viability bonding-capacity sub-score and does not meet Guide Item 17 expectations.
8. **File the special-use permit application (R-20)** and submit evidence of filing; the C1a site requires ZBA special-use approval before the permit can issue.
9. **Document ≥24-month continuous R3 residency (R-62, R-63)** with two proofs of residency, and resolve the §9-5-19(B)(2) interpretive question with the Coordinator.
10. **Provide the architect/engineer-certified site plan and floor plan (R-22)** with all required elements.
11. **Reconcile buffer measurement methodology (R-27)** — submit pedestrian-route distances on the Form in addition to the straight-line survey.
12. **Obtain the Certificate of Good Standing (R-07)** and EIN verification letter (R-08).
13. **Complete City background-check authorizations and fingerprint receipts (R-12)** and principal-officer disclosures (R-11, R-14).
14. **Confirm Employer's Liability at ≥$1M/accident (R-56)** and document AM Best ratings (R-59).
15. **Prepare the standalone Diversity & Inclusion Plan (R-46, R-47)** and Environmental Sustainability Plan (R-44) as separate exhibits.
16. **Obtain the seed-to-sale vendor confirmation letter (R-34, R-70).**
17. **Confirm the Agent-in-Charge's Agent ID Card status (R-67).**

### D.3 Moderate / clean-up items (P3–P4)

18. Reconcile internal inconsistencies (Part C): registered-agent name, parking count, contact phone/email, R3 attribution.
19. Complete criminal-history disclosures (R-13); include publisher's affidavit and any written public comments (R-52); correct notice content for future notices (R-50).
20. Clarify that the community benefit contribution is paid to the City's Cannabis Community Benefit Fund (R-45).
21. Address one-dispensary-per-block confirmation (R-21) and FT/PT staffing split (R-31).
22. Note Form errors (zoning list, occupancy formula, section citations) for the Coordinator (Part A).

---

## 7. Part E — Scoring Impact Note

Under the §9-5-10 / Guide §4 rubric (250 base + 20 social-equity bonus = 270 max), the following gaps have direct scoring consequences if not cured:

| Scoring Category (max) | Gap | Potential Point Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Financial Viability (30) | No bonding commitment letter (R-61) | −10 (bonding-capacity sub-score) |
| Security Plan (50) | Security plan not finalized (R-35) | At risk — cannot be fully scored until submitted |
| Environmental Sustainability (20) | No standalone plan (R-44) | At risk |
| Diversity & Inclusion (25) | No standalone plan; partnerships not formalized (R-46/R-47) | At risk |
| Business Plan (60) | Missing vendor COMPASS letter (R-34) | Minor |
| Social Equity Bonus (20) | R3 residency duration undocumented; (B)(2) ambiguity (R-62) | Bonus at risk until verified |

Curing the P1 and P2 items above protects the maximum attainable score and avoids a completeness deficiency notice.

---

*Prepared as a compliance review of the Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC dispensary permit application package against Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5, the City Application Checklist & Submission Guide, and the Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form (v3.2). This matrix is an analytical work product and does not constitute a City completeness determination, which is made solely by the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator under §9-5-7(C).*"}
