{"file_path": "requirements-matrix.md", "content": "# Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application — Compliance Requirements Matrix

**Applicant:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC
**Proposed Location:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201
**State Conditional License:** ADC-285-0147 (issued Sept. 22, 2023; expires Sept. 21, 2024)
**Application Window:** February 1, 2024 – May 31, 2024 (target submission: May 15, 2024)
**Review Authority:** Evanston Municipal Code, Title 9, Ch. 9, Art. 5 ("Article 5"); City of Evanston Application Checklist & Submission Guide (Jan. 15, 2024); Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form v3.2 (Jan. 12, 2024)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This matrix evaluates the Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC ("Greenleaf") dispensary permit application package against the controlling Evanston Municipal Code (Article 5), the City's Application Checklist & Submission Guide, and the City's Application Form. The package reviewed consists of: (i) the Application Form (v3.2); (ii) the Greenleaf Business Plan Summary; (iii) the Community Engagement Sessions Report; (iv) the Insurance & Bonding Status Memo; (v) the Lease Summary of Key Terms; and (vi) the State Conditional License.

**Headline conclusions:**

1. **The application form (v3.2) is included as an unfilled template.** All data fields, signature blocks, and notarization are blank. The substantive applicant information resides in the supporting documents. The form must be completed in full (every field or "N/A"), signed, and notarized before the package can be accepted.

2. **Three critical, application-blocking deficiencies** must be cured before submission or the application will be deemed incomplete: (a) the **Odor Mitigation Plan lacks the required Illinois Professional Engineer certification** (the form states the application "will not proceed to scoring" without it); (b) the **Security Plan is not yet finalized** (confirmed in the Insurance Memo), which also blocks underwriting of the required cannabis crime/theft insurance; and (c) **Community Engagement Session 2 was noticed only 12 days in advance**, short of the 14-day minimum, which under §9-5-8(F) renders the application incomplete and requires an additional compliant session (no waiver of the two-session minimum is available).

3. **Bonding capacity is undocumented.** Only a "preliminary inquiry" exists; no commitment letter has been obtained. This both fails Guide Item 17 / Form Q26 expectations and forfeits the 10-point bonding-capacity sub-score within Financial Viability.

4. **Several internal inconsistencies** must be reconciled (registered agent name, parking space count, contact phone/email, R3 designation attribution) because §9-5-5(C) requires materials to be "complete and accurate" and material misrepresentation is a revocation ground (§9-5-25(D)(6)).

5. **The Application Form and Submission Guide contain numerous conflicts with the controlling Municipal Code** (zoning districts, buffer measurement method, occupancy formula, cure period, fee payment methods, and others). Where the Code and subordinate documents conflict, the enacted Code controls. These conflicts are catalogued in Part A and should be raised with the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator.

**Status summary (70 requirements assessed):**

| Status | Count |
|---|---|
| Compliant | 26 |
| Partial — Gap | 18 |
| Non-Compliant / Deficient | 4 |
| Not Submitted / Missing | 12 |
| Pending (acceptable pre-issuance) | 6 |
| Discrepancy / Informational | 4 |

---

## 2. Methodology and Status Legend

Each requirement is traced to its authority: the Municipal Code section (controlling), the Submission Guide checklist item, and/or the Application Form question. "Submission Status / Evidence" describes what the package actually contains. Status is assigned per the legend below.

| Status Label | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Compliant** | Requirement fully satisfied by the package as presented. |
| **Partial — Gap** | Core requirement addressed but one or more specified elements are missing, incomplete, or not yet finalized. |
| **Non-Compliant** | Requirement violated or a mandatory threshold not met. |
| **Not Submitted** | Required item is absent from the package and not addressed. |
| **Pending (Pre-Issuance)** | Item is properly deferred to the post-approval/pre-issuance stage; acceptable at application stage. |
| **Discrepancy** | Conflicting information within the package or between the Code, Guide, and Form. |

**Priority flags used in remediation notes:** **[P1 — Blocking]** must be cured or the application will be deemed incomplete / will not proceed to scoring; **[P2 — High]** materially weakens the application or forfeits scoring points; **[P3 — Moderate]** should be cured to avoid a deficiency notice; **[P4 — Clean-up]** accuracy/consistency item.

---

## 3. Part A — Authority Conflicts: Municipal Code vs. Submission Guide vs. Application Form

The Code is the enacted ordinance and controls over the Guide and Form. The following conflicts were identified during the review and should be confirmed with the Cannabis Licensing Coordinator, because they affect how the applicant should prepare the submission.

| # | Subject | Municipal Code (controlling) | Submission Guide | Application Form | Impact / Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A-1 | Application fee payment method | §9-5-6(C): certified check or electronic funds transfer (EFT) | §1: cashier's check or money order | §13: certified check, cashier's check, or wire transfer | Three-way conflict. Code does not authorize money order or wire transfer; Guide omits certified check/EFT. Applicant should use certified check or EFT to satisfy the Code. |
| A-2 | Deficiency cure period | §9-5-7(C): 30 calendar days to cure | §1, §4: 14 calendar days | §13: 14 calendar days | Guide/Form give 16 fewer days than the Code. The Code controls; applicant is entitled to 30 days. |
| A-3 | Completeness determination timeline | §9-5-7(C): within 90 calendar days of receipt | §1/§4: 30-day completeness "target"; 90-day review after completeness | §6: 90-day review after completeness determination | Code's 90 days runs from receipt for the completeness determination; Guide/Form measure different periods. Confusing but not a hard conflict. |
| A-4 | Permitted zoning districts | §9-5-9(A): C1a, C2 Commercial-Service, oC1 Office-Commercial (all special use) | Item 20: references C1a only | §3 Q3 note: "C1a, C1 Commercial, and B2 Business" | **Major Form error.** Form lists C1 and B2 (not authorized) and omits C2 and oC1 (authorized). Greenleaf's C1a site is valid regardless. |
| A-5 | Buffer measurement method | §9-5-9(C): nearest property line to nearest property line (straight-line) | Item 8: "as set forth in the Application Form" | §3 Q7: "nearest pedestrian route"; straight-line "not acceptable" | **Major Code/Form conflict.** The Lease Survey uses straight-line (matches Code). Form demands pedestrian route. Greenleaf's margins are large enough to satisfy either method, but the Form's instruction conflicts with the Code. |
| A-6 | Maximum occupancy formula | §9-5-13(J): 1 customer per 40 sq. ft. of **retail floor area** (1,600 ÷ 40 = 40) | §7(8): "based on retail floor area per the ordinance" | §5 Q12: **total** dispensary square footage ÷ 40 (3,200 ÷ 40 = 80) | **Major Form error.** Form would double the lawful occupancy. Business Plan correctly uses retail floor area (40). |
| A-7 | Certificate of Good Standing recency | §9-5-5(B)(3): within preceding 60 days | Item 4: within 60 days | §13 checklist: within 30 days | Form is more restrictive than Code/Guide. |
| A-8 | Principal-officer SSN | §9-5-5(B)(4): Social Security number (full) | Item 5: not specified | §10 Q14A: last 4 digits only | Form does not collect what the Code requires. |
| A-9 | Buffer survey recency | §9-5-5(B)(7): no recency stated | Item 8: not specified | §3 Q6 note: survey dated within 60 days of submission | Form adds a requirement not in the Code. The Lease Survey (Dec. 15, 2023) would exceed 60 days if submitted May 15, 2024. |
| A-10 | Agent-in-Charge residency | §9-5-13(I): no residency rule; present or reachable within 30 minutes | Item 19: not specified | §2 Q13(d): must reside within 30 miles | Form adds a requirement not in the Code. Okonkwo (Evanston) satisfies it. |
| A-11 | State license at application | §9-5-5(B)(2): conditional/permanent license **or** a pending State application in good standing | §2: must hold a valid license | §1 Notice 9: must hold a valid license; pending not accepted | Guide/Form are more restrictive than the Code. Greenleaf holds a conditional license, so satisfied either way. |
| A-12 | Form section-citation errors | n/a | n/a | §5 Q11 cites §9-5-17 for operating hours (should be §9-5-13(F)); §7 Q22 cites §9-5-15 for community engagement (should be §9-5-8) | Form cites wrong Code sections. |
| A-13 | Social-equity ownership threshold | §9-5-19(B): "a principal officer holding a majority of the ownership interest" | §2: ≥51% held by one or more individuals | §6 Q19 note: "individuals" (plural) | Code requires a single principal officer holding a majority; Guide/Form/State allow 51% by one or more. Greenleaf (Okonkwo 60%) satisfies all. |
| A-14 | Social-equity workforce definition | §9-5-19(D): "all employees, including full-time and part-time" | Item 14: not specified | §6 Q21(a): excludes principal officers and legal counsel | Form narrows the workforce base vs. the Code. Affects the 50% R3 calculation. |
| A-15 | Coordinator contact phone | n/a | (847) 448-4311 | (847) 448-8163 | Inconsistent contact number for the same official. |
| A-16 | Coordinator contact email | n/a | rvasquezmorris@… | rvasquez-morris@… (hyphenated) | Inconsistent email format. |

---

## 4. Part B — Compliance Requirements Matrix

### B.1 Application Form & Submission Mechanics

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| R-01 | Completed, signed, notarized application form | §9-5-5(B)(1); Guide Item 1; Form §§1–14 | Form v3.2 included but **all data fields, certification, and notarization are blank**. Substantive content is in supporting docs. | Not Submitted | **[P1 — Blocking]** Complete every field (or "N/A"), obtain authorized-signatory signature and notarization (Form §14), and index all exhibits by checklist item. Blank fields cause return without processing (Form §1). |
| R-02 | Non-refundable $5,000 application fee | §9-5-5(B)(18); §9-5-6; Guide Item 2; Form §13 | No evidence of payment in package; Form §13 payment fields blank. | Not Submitted | **[P1 — Blocking]** Remit $5,000. Per the controlling Code, pay by **certified check or EFT** (see A-1). No application is deemed complete until the fee is received (§9-5-6(D)). |
| R-03 | Submission format: 3 hard-copy sets + 1 electronic (PDF/USB) | §9-5-5(C); Guide §1; Form §1 | Content assembled; delivery format is a submission logistics item. | Compliant | Confirm three collated/bound hard-copy sets and one USB PDF, tabbed by section/exhibit, delivered to the Civic Center during business hours. |
| R-04 | Submission within application window | §9-5-7(A); Guide §1 | Target submission May 15, 2024, within the Feb. 1–May 31, 2024 window. | Compliant | Maintain the May 15 target to preserve maximum processing time; do not slip past May 31. |

Code/Guide require dated within 60 days; Form says 30 days — obtain within 30 days to satisfy the strictest standard (see A-7). |

Okonkwo discloses a 2016 expunged misdemeanor (Business Plan). | Partial — Gap | **[P3]** Complete Q14B for both officers, including the expunged 2016 matter (disclosure of expunged records is required by the Form; expunged/sealed convictions are not disqualifying per §9-5-22(D)(3)). |

At 120–150 days from a 5/15/2024 submission, local issuance may not occur before expiration. Consider petitioning IDFPR for the single 6-month extension (State Cond. 4) no later than ~7/23/2024. |

---

## 6. Part D — Critical Deficiencies & Priority Remediation Plan

### D.1 Blocking items (P1) — cure before submission or the application will be deemed incomplete / will not proceed to scoring

1. **Complete and execute the Application Form (R-01).** All fields, signature, and notarization are currently blank.
2. **Pay the $5,000 application fee (R-02)** by certified check or EFT (Code method).
3. **Obtain the Illinois PE certification for the Odor Mitigation Plan (R-43).** The Form states the application will not proceed to scoring without it; finalize HVAC specs and air-exchange rates.
4. **Finalize the Security Plan (R-35, R-41).** Currently unfinished; its completion also unblocks cannabis crime/theft insurance underwriting.
5. **Remedy the Session 2 notice deficiency (R-49).** Only 12 days' notice was given (14 required). Hold an additional community engagement session with compliant ≥14-day newspaper and City-website notice. No waiver of the two-session minimum is available (§9-5-8(F)).
6. **Obtain a binder or commitment letter for Cannabis Crime/Theft insurance (R-57).** No evidence of ability to obtain the required $500,000 coverage exists yet.

### D.2 High-priority items (P2) — materially weaken the application or forfeit scoring points

7. **Obtain a bonding commitment letter (R-61).** Without it, Greenleaf forfeits the 10-point Financial Viability bonding-capacity sub-score and does not meet Guide Item 17 expectations.
8. **File the special-use permit application (R-20)** and submit evidence of filing; the C1a site requires ZBA special-use approval before the permit can issue.
9. **Document ≥24-month continuous R3 residency (R-62, R-63)** with two proofs of residency, and resolve the §9-5-19(B)(2) interpretive question with the Coordinator.
10. **Provide the architect/engineer-certified site plan and floor plan (R-22)** with all required elements.
11. **Reconcile buffer measurement methodology (R-27)** — submit pedestrian-route distances on the Form in addition to the straight-line survey.
12. **Obtain the Certificate of Good Standing (R-07)** and EIN verification letter (R-08).
13. **Complete City background-check authorizations and fingerprint receipts (R-12)** and principal-officer disclosures (R-11, R-14).
14. **Confirm Employer's Liability at ≥$1M/accident (R-56)** and document AM Best ratings (R-59).
15. **Prepare the standalone Diversity & Inclusion Plan (R-46, R-47)** and Environmental Sustainability Plan (R-44) as separate exhibits.
16. **Obtain the seed-to-sale vendor confirmation letter (R-34, R-70).**
17. **Confirm the Agent-in-Charge's Agent ID Card status (R-67).**

### D.3 Moderate / clean-up items (P3–P4)

18. Reconcile internal inconsistencies (Part C): registered-agent name, parking count, contact phone/email, R3 attribution.
19. Complete criminal-history disclosures (R-13); include publisher's affidavit and any written public comments (R-52); correct notice content for future notices (R-50).
20. Clarify that the community benefit contribution is paid to the City's Cannabis Community Benefit Fund (R-45).
21. Address one-dispensary-per-block confirmation (R-21) and FT/PT staffing split (R-31).
22. Note Form errors (zoning list, occupancy formula, section citations) for the Coordinator (Part A).

---

## 7. Part E — Scoring Impact Note

Under the §9-5-10 / Guide §4 rubric (250 base + 20 social-equity bonus = 270 max), the following gaps have direct scoring consequences if not cured:

| Scoring Category (max) | Gap | Potential Point Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Financial Viability (30) | No bonding commitment letter (R-61) | −10 (bonding-capacity sub-score) |
| Security Plan (50) | Security plan not finalized (R-35) | At risk — cannot be fully scored until submitted |
| Environmental Sustainability (20) | No standalone plan (R-44) | At risk |
| Diversity & Inclusion (25) | No standalone plan; partnerships not formalized (R-46/R-47) | At risk |
| Business Plan (60) | Missing vendor COMPASS letter (R-34) | Minor |
| Social Equity Bonus (20) | R3 residency duration undocumented; (B)(2) ambiguity (R-62) | Bonus at risk until verified |

Curing the P1 and P2 items above protects the maximum attainable score and avoids a completeness deficiency notice.

---

*Prepared as a compliance review of the Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC dispensary permit application package against Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5, the City Application Checklist & Submission Guide, and the Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form (v3.2). text:
The deliverable is complete and validated. Here's a summary of what I produced.

## Summary

I reviewed the complete Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC dispensary permit application package (8 documents) against the controlling **Evanston Municipal Code (Ch. 9, Art. 5)**, the **Application Checklist & Submission Guide**, and the **Application Form (v3.2)**, and produced **`output/requirements-matrix.docx`** — a validated Word document containing a comprehensive compliance requirements matrix with status assessments and remediation notes.

### Document structure
- **Executive Summary** — headline conclusions + a status tally (70 requirements assessed)
- **Methodology & Status Legend** — six status labels and four priority flags (P1–P4)
- **Part A — Authority Conflicts (16 items)** — where the Code, Guide, and Form disagree (the Code controls). Notable: the Form lists the wrong zoning districts (C1/B2 instead of C2/oC1), uses the wrong occupancy formula (total sq ft instead of retail floor area, doubling lawful occupancy), demands pedestrian-route buffer measurement that conflicts with the Code's straight-line method, and cites wrong section numbers.
- **Part B — Compliance Matrix (70 requirements, 19 sub-categories)** — each traced to its Code section / Guide item / Form question, with submission evidence, status, and remediation notes.
- **Part C — Internal Inconsistencies (6 items)** — e.g., registered-agent name (Keystone vs. Northwest), parking count (8 vs. 12), contact phone/email, R3-designation attribution.
- **Part D — Critical Deficiencies & Priority Remediation Plan** — P1 blocking items, P2 high-priority items, and P3–P4 clean-up items.
- **Part E — Scoring Impact Note** — point exposure under the §9-5-10 rubric.

### Most significant findings
1. **Application Form is an unfilled template** — all fields, signatures, and notarization are blank.
2. **Three P1 blocking deficiencies:** (a) Odor Mitigation Plan lacks the required Illinois PE certification (Form says it won't proceed to scoring without it); (b) Security Plan is not yet finalized (confirmed in the Insurance Memo), which also blocks cannabis crime/theft underwriting; (c) Community Engagement Session 2 was noticed only **12 days** in advance vs. the 14-day minimum — under §9-5-8(F) this renders the application incomplete and requires an additional compliant session (no waiver available).
3. **Bonding capacity undocumented** — only a preliminary inquiry; no commitment letter, forfeiting the 10-point Financial Viability sub-score.
4. **Social-equity R3 residency** — Okonkwo's 24-month continuous residency in tract 8092.02 is not documented (though the expunged-conviction basis and State SE designation provide support, subject to a Code-interpretation question).

The .docx passed the mandatory schema/ZIP validation gate.
